summary: Bonds RTS: Limit Price editing - Limit price is editable when an RTS is sent with a limit price on it.
description_text: Issue: The following issues were observed while testing    in Pano2 Bonds QA:- As per the requirements in  , User should NOT be able to edit an RTS which was submitted with an existing limit. Only adding limits should be allowed. This is not working. If an RTS is submitted with a limit price, user is able to edit the limit price on the RTS order. Note: IMP:- The requirements also state - if the original RTS had no limit, the user should be able to update the limit multiple times on the same RTS. This is working. However, as discussed with   , for both scenarios to work, we would require some easy way to determine if there was a limit price on the original RTS (other than the limit price column: 3066 which is the one being edited). Which may require a design overhaul. This is for bonds-1.83 .

summary: EuroBonds - POG: The Asset class on 'EU' parent orders is displayed as 'EQ' instead of Bond.
description_text: Issue: The following issue was observed in Pano2 Bonds QA - version bonds-1.83:- In bonds-1.83 branch, for any EU parent orders (RfqRegion - 2041 = EU), the Asset Class column (6003) is displaying the value ‘EQ’ instead of ‘BOND’. As seen in the attached screenshots, for US parent orders (RfqRegion - 2041 = US), the Asset Class column (6003) is displaying ‘BOND’ as expected. Checked POG logs for both. And as seen in the logs, 6003 = EQ for EU orders and 6003 = BOND for US orders. Confirmed from Ayub that it is a POG issue. Additional notes from Ayub:- EUBOND orders use SecurityType `EuroCorporateBond`, which POG does not support. POG derives AssetClass currently as follows:- `FX` for `ForeignExchangeContract`, `BOND` for `CorporateBond`, `RATES` for `USTreasuryNote` or `USTreasuryBond`, and `EQ` for anything else or missing. we need a bug ticket to address this in POG. Notes:- Also, note that this underlying issue is causing other issues. For e.g. re-opened    - this is not working as expected because of the Asset class issue. This issue is only for Parent orders in the Adaptive workspace. It’s working as expected for RTS orders as well as ADX child orders.

summary: Rules -  What-If endpoint retirns 401 in staging when VIEW_ALL entlmnt is missing
description_text: Summary User  panotest1  gets a 401 on the what-if endpoint in staging when selecting scope  ny5-te-isi1 . The user has  RULE_SPACE_ACCESS  (space=ctp, access=read) but not  VIEW_ALL . Root Cause Scope  ny5-te-isi1  is not present in the staging  rule_svc_client_scope_mapping.csv . When the CSV lookup misses,  UserAuthorizationService  falls back to regex extraction via  TeConfigUtils.extractClientFromScope , which extracts  "isi"  instead of the correct firm  "pragma" . The firm check then fails and returns 401. Why It Works Locally The local CSV has the  pragma,ny5-te-isi1  mapping, so the lookup succeeds and returns firm  "pragma"  correctly. Fix Add the missing scope mapping(s) to the staging  rule_svc_client_scope_mapping.csv . Any scope not in the CSV will have this issue for non-VIEW_ALL users.

summary: BLGW: Race Condition  RTS arrives before Command status (SUCCESS)  for unsolicited RTS sumbit

summary: Nuc TestHarness improvements
ADX/PRO - Handle application unavailable on initial load
description_text: When the ADX/PRO application is unavailable during initial load, the user should be presented with a clear, informative message instead of a blank or broken page This covers scenarios where backend services are down, network issues prevent loading, or the application fails to bootstrap Implementation Details Detect application unavailability during the initial load sequence (e.g., failed health check, unreachable API) Display a user-friendly "Application Unavailable" screen with: A clear message explaining the service is temporarily unavailable A retry/refresh action Estimated recovery information if available Ensure the unavailability state is handled before the main app shell renders Acceptance Criteria User sees a clear unavailability message when the app cannot load User can retry loading without manually refreshing the browser No broken UI or blank screen is displayed during outages Error state is visually consistent with the ADX/PRO design system

summary: ADX/XPRO - Playwright setup for ADX tests
description_text: Set up the  adx/playwright  package to enable AI-assisted migration of AMS Protractor E2E tests to ADX Playwright tests Provides the project structure, knowledge base, migration tracker, and an agent-driven workflow to convert existing tests in incremental chunks Scope: 129 AMS Protractor tests (881 test cases) across 15 categories – 46 auto-triaged as non-relevant, 83 pending for triage and migration Implementation Details Project lives at  adx/playwright/  with three key areas:  knowledge/ ,  migration-tracker/ , and  src/ Knowledge base persists findings, assertion rules, component mappings, and migration patterns across sessions Migration tracker backed by  migration-status.json  with a browser UI (Vite app) for visibility 6-step migration pipeline per test: SELECT > ANALYZE > PREPARE > MIGRATE > VALIDATE > EXECUTE Dedicated Claude Code agents orchestrate each step: e2e-manager, e2e-analyzer, e2e-prepare, e2e-migrator, e2e-validator Workflow Starting with Grid tests (19 files, 132 test cases) as the most mature ADX feature Each test is individually analyzed, migrated, validated for assertion integrity, and executed Assertion integrity: no assertion values change unless covered by an approved rule in  knowledge/assertion-rules.md PREPARE step is the only one that modifies ADX application code (adding  data-testid  attributes, creating e2e-components) Failed migrations get 2 attempts max, then marked as failed with notes for manual review Notes This story covers the initial project setup and infrastructure – actual test migration progress will be tracked via the migration tracker Follow-up work will proceed category-by-category starting with Grid

summary: Nuc - Risk-policy tests migration
Nuc - Risk-utilization tests migration
Compile Error Fixer Agent
description_text: User story As a developer, when I receive a translation report from the Translation Agent, I want to have a Fixer Agent that automatically attempts to fix compile errors so that I can ensure the translated tests compile successfully. Context LLM assisted translation may contain compile errors. Some errors, like references to non-migrated Protractor components, cannot be fixed without further translation or human intervention. Other errors, such as invalid import paths, can be fixed through LLM assistance. Acceptance criteria Build a Fixer Agent that processes the translation report from the Translation Agent. The Fixer Agent should attempt to fix errors that are correctable, such as: Invalid import paths Function calls instead of property accesses Use tools like Microsoft TS-FIX and bespoke LLM rules for error correction. Other information N/A

summary: Add Translator Compile Gate
description_text: User story As a developer, when a Playwright test has been translated by the Translator Agent, I want to run the Fixer Agent to review and fix the compile errors so that the test can be executed successfully. Context The issue involves a process where Playwright tests are translated by the Translator Agent. If there are errors in the Translation Report, a Fixer Agent is needed to address these compile errors. Acceptance criteria The system should detect when the Translation Report contains errors. The Fixer Agent must be able to review the errors. The Fixer Agent should fix the compile errors identified in the Translation Report. Other information N/A

Bonds: Selecting multiple parent orders across US and EU and amending should display an error message instead of displaying a blank screen.
description_text: Issue: The following issue was observed in Pano2 Bonds QA - version bonds-1.83:- In bonds-1.83 branch, selecting 2 or more parent orders (shift-click, control-click etc.), with the selection containing a mix of US and EU orders in the Adaptive workspace, doing a right click and selecting Amend from the context menu, followed by selecting a Strategy from the Strategy drop-down is not working and is leading to a blank screen with an error. Re-loading the application gets the application back to it’s current state, but the amend fails. Please see the attached screenshot for the same. Notes:- Sometimes, the error occurs as soon as soon as Strategy is selected. And other times, the error happens when selecting ‘New and Start’ button the order ticket. This issue is only for multi-select. Able to amend single orders. And this is only happening when the selected orders are a mix of US and EU orders. This was previously reported as a bug and was converted to a requirement Story since this requirement was not previously captured. The message when orders from multiple regions are selected: “Please select orders from a single region.“ Region Message takes precedence over multiple strategy message. E.g. If user select EU take and US Provide, we will show message related to multiple region. Requirement / Proposed Solution:- As discussed with   , for this situation, i.e. when a mix of US and EU orders are selected and Amend is clicked, there should be an info message stating that this is not allowed. You can check with Teddy for the message content.

summary: RX Services to allow a subset of columns to be requested
description_text: Column Subset Subscriptions for Background ADX in xPro When ADX is embedded within xPro, WebSocket subscriptions to rx-services must remain active even when the ADX panel is hidden (not  visible to the user). To minimize bandwidth and processing overhead for these background subscriptions, a new optional requestedColumns parameter has been added to the rx-services streaming endpoints. How it works: Clients can include a requestedColumns field in their SnapshotRequest or LiveUpdatesRequest payload — a set of column IDs representing the subset of columns they need. The server computes the intersection of requestedColumns with the user's entitled columns. Any requested column the user is not entitled to is silently excluded — entitlements remain the security boundary. When requestedColumns is omitted or empty, the full set of entitled columns is returned (backwards-compatible with existing clients). This applies to all three data endpoints: snapshots, live updates, and snapshot-and-stream.

summary: ADX/XPRO - ADX embedded in XPRO Shell App
description_text: Groundwork for running ADX as an embedded micro-frontend inside the XPro Shell environment. Interop Layer An interop context needs to be established to handle bidirectional communication between ADX and the XPro shell via postMessage. This includes receiving JWT tokens from the shell (bypassing Okta when embedded), syncing theme changes, and registering ADX as "Adaptive Auto-X" with the shell's intent system. A  interop=xpro  query param is used to detect when ADX is running inside XPro. Auth to support external Cognito token The backend needs to support Fake Cognito (Hybrid) JWT tokens from XPro alongside existing Okta tokens.  On the frontend, the auth context needs an externalJwt mode where tokens are injected by the shell rather than fetched from Okta. Environment XPro-specific local dev environments need to be configured with non-conflicting ports (8185/8180/8187 instead of 8085/8080/8087) so ADX and XPro backends (docker) can run simultaneously.

summary: Data API - Implement Server Side Filtering
description_text:
User Story As a platform operator, I want the Data API to apply configurable server-side filters to high-volume data streams (for example, DMA orders), so that unwanted traffic is blocked even when client applications fail to include appropriate filters in their API calls. Overview Server-side filters are merged with client-provided filters at runtime. The merged filter set is applied to all data returned by the API. Filter Syntax ┌────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┬───────────────┐
│   Format   │              Meaning              │    Example    │
├────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼───────────────┤
│ col:value  │ Include rows where column = value │ 3015:VWAP     │
├────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼───────────────┤
│ col:!value │ Exclude rows where column = value │ 3015:!firmdma │
├────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼───────────────┤
│ col:v1|v2  │ Include rows matching v1 OR v2    │ 3645:ht|lt    │
├────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┼───────────────┤
│ col:!v1|v2 │ Exclude rows matching v1 OR v2    │ 3645:!ht|lt   │
└────────────┴───────────────────────────────────┴───────────────┘ Column IDs are integers (e.g.,  3015  = Strategy,  3645  = Firm Group,  3066  = LimitPx) Value types depend on column (String, Double, etc.) Configuration Environment Variables ┌────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┐
│        Variable        │             Description              │          Default          │
├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
│ API_FILTER_ENABLED     │ Enable/disable server-side filtering │ true                      │
├────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┤
│ API_FILTER_CONFIG_PATH │ Path to filter config file           │ classpath:api-filters.yml │
└────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────…

summary: ASAP Response grid not sorting correctly
description_text: Ping    for any qs - he reported this bug According to the specification, ASAP RFQs should be sorted in the Response grid as follows: SortRank (2094) — smaller values sort higher (values may be negative). Example order: -3.4, -1.5, -0.6, 0.2, 2.1, 3.3 TransactTime (2036) — earlier times sort higher CounterPartyShortName (2086) — alphabetical sort However, the executed response is not appearing at the top of the stack as expected (see screenshot 1). Additionally, the documentation states (FYI only, no code changes required): For ASAP RFQs, the Too-Good level should normally appear at the top, which should occur naturally when sorting by SortRank. If an ASAP RFQ has the <skip-too-good> taskParam set, the Too-Good level should still appear at the top. This behavior differs from BIN RFQs, where Too-Good sorts to the bottom based on DecisionTimeLevel.

summary: Investigate report of no reason for NVO halt in Portfolio Grid, but reason in alert
description_text: At 11:35:29 on 2026-02-03, symbol NVO halted. While the halt showed in the UI, and the reason was shown in the alert, we received a report that no reason was displayed in the “Portfolio grid”. No screen shot accompanied the report, and to save disk space JSON logging is disabled, so we have nothing but the verbal report. QA could not reproduce the issue. The proper reason is shown in alerts. When we receive a trading state change, it is always logged. We do not currently log the reason, although it would be good to do so to help with future investigations like this. The following was logged: 2026-02-03 11:35:29.186  INFO 8036 --- [t thread. Id: 1] c.p.pano2.mdf.data.MarketDataCache       : Trading state change NVO: QUOTE_ONLY Requirements Update logging to log the trading state change reason. This will be helpful for the future. Ask Activ what the reason code was for this halt. (If they sent us a reason code of '', then we were right not to display anything.) Analysis Activ sent us the reason code, and the reason code was properly displayed in the alert. So, MDF is properly receiving the halt reason codes. For disk space reasons, we do not enable JSON logging in QA. So, we do not have a record of the data sent from MDF to the UI. We know that the alert (which is also read from MDF) has the reason, and we see the reasons in the “Portfolio Grid” for other currently-halted instruments. Without the logging or a screen shot to accompany the bug report, further investigation is stalled unless QA can reproduce the bug with another halted instrument. (It would be  very  good to install a large hard drive in  ws-qa001.ny5.pragmafs.com , so that it can run all the time with logging enabled, to save time during problem investigation. Several hours per ticket times many tickets adds up to a lot of expensive hours.)

When assigning roles to a user, it is easy to misclick and unintentionally add the wrong role. Enhancement 1 (already done) : Add a search and/or filter capability when selecting roles to minimize the risk of incorrect role assignment Enhancement 2 : Add a confirmation dialog box prompting the user to confirm adding the role to a user. Other information Sonarqube container logs indicate multiple failures to create worker threads due to pthread_create errors. Previous attempts to restart the service and adjust memory limits did not resolve the issue. We will not attempt to resolve the current issues; lets upgrade and then test. summary: PAD enhancements for Bonds
description_text: Requirement Implement feature  ADX-997 . Content copied below for convenience 1. Search / Filter Roles PAD contains hundreds of roles with very similar names. When assigning roles to a user, it is easy to misclick and unintentionally add the wrong role. Enhancement 1 (already done) : Add a search and/or filter capability when selecting roles to minimize the risk of incorrect role assignment Enhancement 2 : Add a confirmation dialog box prompting the user to confirm adding the role to a user. The dialog box should prompt the user with appropriate information like:  Role <rolename> is being added to <user>. Please double check the right entitlements are being set. 2.Environment Indicator (PROD / UAT) PAD looks identical in PROD and UAT, and users exist in both environments. This creates a high risk of support accidentally modifying users or roles in the wrong environment. Enhancement : Display a clear environment indicator (e.g., PROD or UAT) in the top header of the application. 3.Entitlements Validation users can have entitlements where the client value differs, with no validation or warning. Enhancement : Add validation that a user cannot have two VIEW_CLIENT_TRADER entitlements where the client values differ. 4. Long term solution (copied from Sree comment on ADX-997) In addition to the above, we want the code to enforce that a client-firm cannot see another client-firm's order under any misconfiguration circumstances. We have this for EQ, FX as each user is in their own firm, and code will not let one firm see another firm's orders, regardless of configuration. This check breaks down for Mktx clients as they are all configured under the Mktx firm. We need to enhance code (not just configuration) to protect against possible misconfiguration for the Mktx scenario. Additionally, consider redoing  the requirements so that we introduce the concept of client-firm at a structural (not naming conventions + filtering) level, so that once a user is configured as belonging …

summary: WS - Hotbutton highlight the latest applied HB
description_text: Original req 2/20/25 The hotbutton should be rendered with a visual accent indicating that the hotbutton has been applied to the order ticket. If the fields in the order ticket are then edited, the hotbutton accent should be removed. Manasi q's / comments 8/27: where will we show the  hot button accent? more than this what clients want is to be able to show on the order which hot button is being used. Like if they have 3 hot buttons, they want us to show the name of the hot button which was applied on the parent order Next steps Discuss with Manasi, and nail down reqs Document reqs, rationale here Implement Discussion with Teddy, Yan and Andrey: 1. Client want to see last applied hotbutton details for a particular order. 2. This can be a behaviour for last 1 or 2 days since older ones might not be relevant. 3. This can be shown as a column in the PO grid. 4. Client will not see this info across different devices since it's stored on the client side only. 5. Render hot-button column with similar colour same as hotbutton. Current Implementation:  1. Last Applied Hot Button for an order persist even if the order is modified after that later on. 2. Order and Hot Button Mapping Persist for Max 2 days. (similar to cleared alerts behaviour) 3. Ways to clear hot button from order:     * Remove hot-button from the hot-button panel     * Update to different hot-button     * If none of the above 2 step is taken. The mapping will be cleared after 2 days of applying for the   order. This happens because we keep record of mapping for max 2 days only in local-storage. 4. The mapping is stored in the browser only. No backend API is called to save this info for the user. 5. Saving only on the browser also implies that the user won’t see the saved hot-button across different devices. 6. Changes have been implemented for AMS only as part of  .  This ticket has been created to handle similar changes for Polaris.

Part of   change to be able to Send a message with Subject only.

summary: Auto-migrate hot buttons for the new Counterparty options
description_text: Context In prod today, the Take Order ticket has a field 3355 (Anonymous) = true/false/null. The next release of 1.76 will replace 3355 on the OrderTicket with 3886 (CounterpartyOptions) = Anon OT Only / Counterparty Selection / null. There may be hotbuttons that specify 3355. We want to migrate those hotbuttons seamlessly to use 3886 as per the mapping table below. We also want to make sure that if there’s a problem with 3886 on the order ticket (there was a problem earlier due to TradePlant misconfiguration, hence the paranoia), we want to be able to rollback the order-ticket to 3355, and have any old 3355-specific hotbuttons continue to work. Requirement Migrate any hot-buttons that specify 3355 (AnonOnly) to also specify 3886 (Counterparties) instead. The mapping from 3355 (old) to 3886 (new) is as shown below Make sure the change supports a rollback of the order ticket to 3355 i.e. any hotbuttons that had 3355 earlier will continue to set 3355 to true/false as was configured. The following use cases are  not supported: User creates a new hotbutton with 3886. Order ticket is rolled-back to 3355. In this case, the hotbutton will do nothing, as the hotbutton has only 3886, and order ticket has 3355. User had a 3355 hotbutton, but later changed the 3886 hotbutton to a different value than the mapped value. In the event of an order-ticket rollback to 3355, the hotbutton will set 3355 to the original value, which may be inconsistent with the user-modified 3886 value. Mapping from 3355 values to 3886 values Old value - 3355 New value - 3886 true Anon OT Only false My Counterparty Selection null null Test scenarios & expected behavior Existing hotbutton that had 3355 set - with new Pano (this PAN) and order ticket specifying 3886, hotbutton should set 3886 to the mapped value Pano / order ticket rolled back to 3355 - hotbutton will set 3355 to the orig value User edits hotbutton to a different value - hotbutton will set 3886 to the mapped value Order ticket rolled back…

description_text: Scope:- Fix the failing Polaris tests from the nightly runs for release/1.75 with MOG General TODO for Test fixes:- Fix the failing Polaris release/1.75 Nucleus v129.10.0 and MOG tests. Refer the last 3 runs from the latest Flaky tests report for the same. For each failed test in each of the below test reports:- Analyze the failures to determine if they are Test failures or Functional failures (See notes below for the definition of each of these terms). Fix the test failures. Jot down the functional failures. Will analyze them to determine what needs to be done for these. Re-run through the respective pipelines once fixed to validate that the test fixes work correctly. Notes:- Test failures - Flaky tests that pass and fail intermittently or something else that is causing the tests to fail. Either way, the functionality hasn't changed or broken. Only test updates are required. Functional failures - The underlying functionality has now changed or there is a bug in it.

summary: RS - BE Diff Service
description_text: User story As a user, when I select two versions of scope (ruleset), I want to create a diff between the two versions so that I can see the differences between the two. Context This issue involves the backend (BE) Diff service. The goal is to load two selected versions, such as WORKING and CURRENT, and create a diff between them using a git library on the backend. Acceptance criteria Load two selected versions (e.g., WORKING and CURRENT). Create a diff between the two versions. Use git diff style for the diff output. Leverage a git library on the backend. Send the diff to the frontend (FE). Other information N/A

summary: RS - FE Diff 2 Versions
description_text: User story As a user, when I am on the display page, I want a quick button to compare WORKING & CURRENT so that I can easily see the differences. As a user, when I am on the audit page, I want to select any two versions and perform a diff so that I can analyze changes between them. As a user, I want to select two different scopes, such as two instances in CTP, so that I can compare them effectively. Context This issue involves adding a feature to allow users to compare different versions and see differences in a user-friendly manner. The feature will be accessible from both the display and audit pages. Acceptance criteria A quick button should be added to the display page to compare WORKING & CURRENT. The diffs should be displayed in the table editor. There should be a toggle option to show/hide non-diffs in affected rules. There should be a toggle option to show/hide unchanged rules. Users should be able to select any two versions on the audit page to perform a diff. Users should be able to select two different scopes, such as two instances in CTP. Other information N/A

description_text: Summary : The frontend implementation will include the creation of a "what-if" field/button that allows users to initiate what-if scenarios. This feature will enable users to configure and execute scenarios directly from the UI. Additionally, the execution results will be displayed clearly, including a comprehensive rules trace that outlines the conditions and effects of the rules that are triggered, as well as the final output data. This will ensure that users can easily understand the implications of their configurations and the outcomes of the scenarios they run. See David Mechner and Sreeram POCs for bootstrap ideas.

summary: Pipeline consistently failing on SonarQube Quality Gate during Static Code Analysis (Pano)
description_text:
The CI pipeline has been  consistently failing across multiple consecutive runs  due to  SonarQube Quality Gate errors  in the  Static Code Analysis – Pano  stage. This issue is  reproducible and persistent , affecting both  develop  and multiple  release/*  branches, even in runs with  no code changes , indicating this is  not commit-specific  but a pipeline or quality gate configuration problem. Impacted Builds (examples) #947 → #956 (Jan 21–23) Branches affected: Pano: develop Pano: release/1.75 Pano: release/1.76 Pano: release/1.77 UIGateways stage is also marked as failed, but  Pano Static Code Analysis is the first hard failure . Observed Behavior Checkout and build stages complete successfully. Static Code Analysis (Pano) runs for ~16–18 minutes. SonarQube analysis  completes successfully , but the  Quality Gate returns  ERROR , causing the pipeline to fail. Relevant logs: ./gradlew sonarqube -Dsonar.branch.name=${BRANCH_OR_TAG}
Sleep -- 300
Wait for SonarQube analysis to be completed and return quality gate status

SonarQube task status: SUCCESS
Quality gate status: ERROR

summary: ADX/XPRO - Force using backward compatible Layout
description_text: As a developer, when implementing the layout, I want to ensure it is backward compatible so that it works seamlessly with both ADX and AMS. Also we will move ADX required redux states in common-platform Context ADX and AMS utilize different reducers for the layout. ADX uses redux saga, while AMS uses RTK. This creates a need for a single entry point to manage the layout, independent of the Redux implementation.

summary: Internal DevOps: Update cleanup pipeline for new product-prefixed release branches
description_text: Background The existing cleanup pipeline was designed for the  legacy release naming convention  (e.g.  release/0.* ) and no longer reflects the current branch strategy. With the introduction of  product-prefixed release branches  (e.g.  release/ams-0* ,  release/bonds-0* , etc.), the cleanup logic must be updated to correctly identify and remove  stale branches and tags  that follow the new naming scheme. This is an  internal DevOps-only change  and does not impact application behavior or release logic. Goal Update the cleanup pipeline so it: Recognizes the new product-prefixed release branch patterns Cleans up obsolete branches and tags safely and consistently Avoids deleting active or recently-used release branches Scope Update cleanup rules to include: release/ams-0* release/bonds-0* release/rates-0* release/ws-0* Maintain existing age-based cleanup threshold (e.g. branches/tags older than 2 weeks) Apply changes in: tooling_cluster/jenkins_utils/Jenkinsfile.devops_repo_cleanup

summary: AMS Docker pipeline: Support downloading & deploying existing release artifacts (new naming convention)
description_text: With the introduction of  product-prefixed release branches  (e.g.  release/ams-1.83 ), the AMS Docker pipeline must support downloading existing artifacts. Currently, the pipeline behavior is still biased toward rebuilding artifacts, which is: unnecessary for validation/testing workflows, and inconsistent with how release artifacts are meant to be consumed. This ticket focuses  only  on enabling  download    of  existing artifacts  that were previously built and published to Artifactory. Goal Update the AMS Docker pipeline so it can: Resolve the correct release artifact  based on the new branch naming convention Download that artifact from Artifactory Deploy it into the Docker-based test environment Run validation/testing  without rebuilding artifacts

summary: Rule-svc configs
description_text: This ticket documents all environment variables required for the Rule Service. Users deploying to any environment (QA, PE, or Production) must configure these environment variables according to their specific needs. Domain Configuration A key requirement for the Rule Service is to use the same domain as the Pano2 UI. To achieve this, the nginx configuration has been modified to serve the Rule UI under the /rule path using the same domain. Example: https://pano2-devl.pragmafs.com/rule/ Implementation Details: The nginx configuration routes requests to /rule/* to the Rule UI application This ensures seamless integration with the existing Pano2 domain and SSO infrastructure Users access the Rule UI through the same domain as the main Pano2 application Environment Variable Documentation : All environment variables for the Rule Service are documented in the sections below. Administrators must review and configure these variables appropriately for their target environment before deployment. General Env variables: RULE_SVC_PORT : The port on which the Rule Service will listen for HTTP requests. RULE_SVC_LISTEN_IP : The IP address on which the Rule Service will bind. Default value is “0.0.0.0”. RULE_SVC_AUTHORIZATION_ENABLED : This environment variable controls whether authorization checks are enabled in the Rule Service application. default false Set to true to enable authorization logic, requiring users to have appropriate permissions for accessing rule spaces and performing actions. Set to false to disable authorization logic, allowing all users to access rule spaces and perform actions without permission checks. RULE_SVC_CLIENT_SCOPE_MAPPING_FILE : Environment variable that specifies the path to a CSV file containing mappings between client identifiers and their associated scopes. This mapping is used by the Rule Service for client-to-scope resolution and authorization.The CSV file must include a header row with "client" and "scope" columns, though column order is flexi…

summary: Adapt AMS Docker pipeline to build docker once
description_text: Description AMS pipeline running on Docker runtime often falls into  no space space left on device . The issue is caused by low storage resources on the agent during the build. An alternative is to refactor the Docker build process as we did for [Polaris Docker Pipeline.| pano2/jenkins/Jenkinsfile.docker.polaris at develop · PragSec/pano2 ]. Problem and solution The current implementation splits tests to be executed in some containers to reduce the build time. The decision to split tests into multiple containers was taken to reduce build time by running multiple Docker stacks in parallel. The problem resides in the Docker build process. The approach of starting multiple stacks in parallel using docker-compose build the same Dockerfile multiple times as well, it's redundant and inefficient, since the Dockerimage build is always the same, and each build will generate a new image and consume redundant disk space to store the image. The problem scales when the number of replicas is increased. Note: Same approach was implemented on [Polaris Docker Pipeline.| pano2/jenkins/Jenkinsfile.docker.polaris at develop · PragSec/pano2 ] and works well. Changes needed Instead of building a docker image for all stack Remove docker build from  Docker Compose file . The docker build needs to be done once in a previous step, like  https://github.com/PragSec/pano2/blob/138f813339c656712450721d5e56a3420e3c6fc2/jenkins/Jenkinsfile.docker.polaris#L196  -  The built image does not need to be published. The image will be used only during the build. Set imageName built in the container  https://github.com/PragSec/pano2/blob/138f813339c656712450721d5e56a3420e3c6fc2/ams/docker-compose.yml#L39 Adapt ams.sh file -  https://github.com/PragSec/pano2/blob/138f813339c656712450721d5e56a3420e3c6fc2/ams/ams.sh

Scope Update branch protection rules in the following repositories: pano2 UIGateways pano2cfg Acceptance Criteria Branch protection rules allow: release/ams-* release/ws-* release/bonds-* release/rates-* Existing protection rules remain intact for non-release branches Changes are validated by attempting a test branch creation

summary: Update tests to use data-testid instead of data-qa
description_text: To leverage Playwright's  getByTestId  locator in ADX, we need to align our test ID attribute with the  @mktx/ui  convention, which uses Playwright's default  data-testid . Scope This task includes: Creating a validation script to detect any remaining  data-qa  usage Creating a migration script to automate the attribute rename Updating all  data-qa  attributes to  data-testid  across ADX components The validation script will be integrated into the build process to prevent regressions and ensure no tests reference the deprecated  data-qa  attribute. Summary of Changes What was updated The migrate-data-testid and validate-data-testid scripts now handle additional patterns used in Protractor and Playwright tests. Impact Protractor tests (test/protractor/):   - The custom locator definitions in locators.ts will be renamed:     - by.qa() → by.testid()     - by.qaPrefix() → by.testidPrefix()     - by.qaDataValue() → by.testidValue()     - by.qaContains() → by.testidContains()   - All usages of these locators throughout protractor test files will be updated automatically Playwright tests (test/playwright/):   - The custom selector registrations in selectors.ts will be renamed:     - qa-value → testid-value     - qa-contains → testid-contains     - qa-prefix → testid-prefix   - All usages like locator('qa-prefix=tab-') will become locator('testid-prefix=tab-')

summary: ADX/XPRO - Grids Tabs
description_text: User story As a user, when I view the grid tabs in ADX, I want to see a linked/global icon so that I can easily identify the type of tabs. As a user, when I interact with the grid tabs, I want to access menu items like Link, Duplicate, Close, Delete, Rename, and Share so that I can manage my tabs effectively. As a user, I want to see an unread count on all global (unlinked) tabs so that I can keep track of my unread items. Context This issue aims to enhance the functionality of grid tabs in ADX by introducing specific features that improve user experience. Acceptance criteria Display a linked/global icon on the grid tabs. Include the following menu items for grid tabs: Link Duplicate Close Delete Rename Share Show an unread count on all global (unlinked) tabs. Omit the Show/Hide unread count menu item. Other information N/A

summary: Suppress TOB quotes which have conditions that exclude them from the NBBO
description_text:
Background During investigation of  , we saw cases where a Top-of-books quote was better (closer to the inside) than the NBBO, and wondered why that quote was not reflected in the NBBO. To investigate this, I added logging of the quote conditions for TOB quotes. We found that when the above circumstance occurred, the TOB quote had a condition which causes it to be excluded from the NBBO. For example, see: In this case, the NBBO bid showed $98.2, and the top-of-books showed one bid of $102.7, and the question was: why is the NBBO Bid not $102.7? Checking the log, we saw top-of-books coming in from BATSY at the right time, the only occurrences of 102.7 in a TOB bid: 2025-12-23 10:39:24.606  INFO 17443 --- [t thread. Id: 1] feed_log                                 : Top-of-books: WSHP (WSHP.BY [0]) at BATSY: 100 @ $102.7(Y) | -
2025-12-23 10:39:24.609  INFO 17443 --- [t thread. Id: 1] feed_log                                 : Top-of-books: WSHP (WSHP.BY [0]) at BATSY: 100 @ $102.7(L) | - The (Y) and (L) indicate quote conditions on the bid. Per  https://support.activfinancial.com/feed-info/feeds/detail?id=88  , they are: L: Closed Quote. Not included in the NBBO. Y: Regular, One-Sided Open Quote Automated. Included in the NBBO. At the time of the screen shot, the BATSY top-of-book quote had condition L, excluding it from the NBBO, which is why the NBBO bid price was lower. Requirement Read the FID_PERMISSION_ID when reading TOB quotes. Cache the permission ID in the Security Master record, because permission ID is delivered only on the first update, not every update, since it does not change. Use the permission ID to look up the feed, the same as we do with trades. Using the feed ID, check the quote conditions (if there are any) to verify that the quote should be included in the NBBO. The quote conditions are different between CTA and UTP, so we need to know which feed the quote came from. If a TOB quote should not be included in the NBBO, note that the quote is bein…

summary: Data API - Entitlement exception handler may return 406 for non-metadata routed
description_text: OrderStatsExceptionHandler returns ResponseEntity<MetadataResponseDto> for every EntitlementException from OrderStatsController. If the exception is thrown by non-metadata routes (snapshot/NDJSON/SSE endpoints), Spring will try to render a JSON metadata body while the client’s Accept is application/x-ndjson(-gzip) or text/event-stream, which will produce a 406/not-writable error instead of the intended “graceful” empty snapshot/stream. Scope the handler to metadata or return endpoint-appropriate empty payloads.

summary: RS - Effect Masking
description_text:
As an operator, I can prevent a particular client from seeing a list of effects with a given key. This masking of key/effects is performed on the backend before ruleset is sent to the UI. It applies ONLY to read-only entitlement, RULE_MASK_EFFECT(< space >, < key >) Parameters <space> Logical rule space identifier (e.g. ctp, csfr) No wildcards, regex, or comma-separated values <key>  effect key.  may be a comma-separated list of keys . Filtering filterRulesetViewForUser(user, ruleset):
  if NOT isReadOnly(user, ruleset.space):
     return ruleset  
  eMask = maskedEffectKeys(ruleset.space)    // effect keys to hide
  for each scope in ruleset.scopes:     
    scope.rules = [
      r' for each r in scope.rules:
        r'.effects = [e for e IN r.effects if e.key NOT IN eMask]
        WARN if r'.effects IS EMPTY             
    ]  
  return ruleset

getRulesetForUi(user, rulesetId):
  base = loadRuleset(rulesetId)
  return filterRulesetViewForUser(user, base)

whatIf(user, rulesetId, input):
  base = loadRuleset(rulesetId)
  view = filterRulesetViewForUser(user, base)  
  return evaluate(view, input) What-if This is problematic because what is presented on the CTP Viewer may not match what is seen in the actual deployment (effects may be filtered). According to   it is never (almost never) the case that (1) a rule has just a single effect and (2) that effect will be filtered. So, rules will always have at least 1 non-filtered effect for display (no rule with 'empty' effect)

summary: RTK migration - Layout handling
description_text: --- ON HOLD --- The initial RTK migration attempt revealed several architectural challenges that made the implementation error-prone. Challenges Encountered: RTK Effects Timing : getState() in RTK effects doesn't contain the modified state yet (unlike sagas where state is updated before the success handler runs) PATCH Request Ordering : Successive PATCH requests may arrive at the backend out of order, causing state corruption State Desynchronization : Multiple browser instances modifying the same layout can cause conflicts (no optimistic locking or conflict resolution) Migration Order Dependency : Actions were bridged to RTK before all saga consumers were updated, breaking AMS functionality A revised migration plan has been created based on lessons learned. The new approach: Simplifies the backend first (removes side effects, makes it "dumb" CRUD) Flattens effect chains (no effects triggering other effects) Then performs the RTK migration in the correct order Documented plans can be found in the branch: feature/PAN-10933-layout-rtk-migration V2 Plan: adx/docs/LAYOUT_RTK_MIGRATION_V2.md V1 Plan: adx/docs/LAYOUT_RTK_MIGRATION.md (original plan, kept for reference) Description The Layout handling related code contains common functionality that needs to be shared across ADX, WS, and AMS applications. Currently, the Layout code uses legacy Redux patterns (reducers, selectors, sagas) that are incompatible with the new codebase architecture. Objective Migrate the Layout Redux-related code to Redux Toolkit (RTK) to enable code reuse across all three applications. Scope Refactor Layout reducers to RTK slices Update selectors to align with RTK conventions Replace Redux Saga and Saga request logic with RTK where needed Additional Context This work aligns with the ongoing initiative to deprecate Redux Saga in favor of RTK patterns across the codebase.

summary: Data API - Update OAuth Token Authentication for M2M
description_text: Summary : Okta sets the  sub  claim to  app.clientId . For the Data API authorization server, we add a custom claim -  app_user_email  - which contains the API M2M client email. Problem is that Pano Rx entitlement code expects the email address to be in  sub . And ** The  sub  claim cannot be set to  app_user_email  in Okta - doing so generates below error: {"error":"server_error","error_description":"The 'sub' system claim could not be evaluated."} Solution: Modify  SecurityConfigUtils.getUserEmail()  to extract  app_user_email  from token (if present). If not present, which will be case for non-Data API logins, do what it does now.

summary: Manual Rates Order Ticket >> Adx time out error.
description_text: Click on Rates Order Ticket Select TWAP strategy Submit an order Validate no adx time out alerts are displayed. Why? We assumed two ExtendedCommandStatus messages(one just after submitted, one when succeed) we gets for an Adx submit from BondLink but that's not the case. We are getting one message ExtendedCommandStatus message for and Adx sumbit. And can be succeed or Fail. So, on successful Adx submit we were not clearing timeout alert action(scheduled at time of ADX submit), that's why it is generating a false timeout alert. As the part if the fix we are clearing scheduled timeout alert when we gets a successful ADX command status message,

summary: ADX/XPRO - New client package "common-platform" (Groundwork)
description_text: Currently, adx-client depends on @pragmatrading/pano-platform, which contains code specific to AMS/WS clients. This creates ambiguity in exports and forces ADX to pull in irrelevant code. This is a cleaner package boundaries where ADX references only shared code via common-platform, while AMS/WS continue using pano-platform which extends common-platform. Changes Rename @pragmatrading/rx-grid → @pragmatrading/common-platform to serve as the shared foundation for all trading apps Remove @pragmatrading/pano-platform dependency from adx-client (pano-platform remains for AMS and WS only) Delete @pragmatrading/rx-react-components and distribute its code between pano-platform (AMS/WS-specific) and common-platform (shared)

summary: ADX/XPRO - Build and use @mktx/ui library locally (Groundwork)
description_text: The  @mktx/ui  package available in the mktx artifactory does not always contain the latest UI library code. Additionally, there are cases where we need to modify Platform monorepo components and test those changes within the ADX project before submitting a PR to the Platform monorepo. This feature provides a workflow to locally modify the  @mktx/ui  package in the monorepo, build it, and temporarily use the modified version in the ADX project. Usage From the  adx/client  directory, run: pnpm publish-mktx-ui This script packages the local UI library and adds an override to the root package.json to use the local version instead of the published one. Important: The package.json override is intended for local development only and should not be merged into the production branch. To revert the override and restore the original package resolution, run: pnpm publish-mktx-ui:revert

summary: WS:  Split pane displays confusing false warns when splitting orders.
description_text: Version:    1.75.0-rc102-patch-p2 (prod version)  - reproduced with same version in QA.  Reported by BA. Issue :  Erroneous staged Qty warn messages:             a.  For subsequent Hotbutton use where Qty Staged is available but falsely warns of:   "Must be lower than or equal to Qty Staged."             b.  Another erroneous error is received when a final (no qty left) CO Split is performed, with text:  "Unknown reason". See attached screenshots.

summary: WS:  PO tab switching keeps sticky selections and uses previously selected tab Order for Hotbutton-split action.
description_text: Version:   1.75.0-rc102-patch-p2 (prod version)  - reproduced with same version in QA.  Reported by BA. This is a priority bug needed for PROD fix on the same patch branch:  1.75.0-rc102-patch. Issue :  PO tab switching keeps sticky selections and uses previously selected tab Order for Hotbutton-split action.            This issue is on both Desktop and Tablet. Repro steps: Setup at least two PO tabs (PO-Tab1, PO-Tab2) that are unlinked, so all Symbols are visible in the PO grid. Setup at a Hotbutton for any destination/qty. Send/Have at least two different Symbols (AAPL, BAC) with available Qty to Split. Select AAPL from PO-Tab1 and see the POD details with AAPL,. > Split-confirm dialog shows appropriate details. Switch to PO-Tab2 and select a different Symbol (BAC) and apply the same Hotbutton. >  Split-confirm dialog shows appropriate details. Go back PO-Tab1 and WITHOUT clicking into the grid, apply the same Hotbutton again: > Issue1:  See the POD details are still showing for the previous PO-Tab2 symbol, BAC. > Issue2:  Applying the Hotbutton and clicking Split, still keeps the previous PO-Tab2 symbol, BAC for the Split/Confirm dialog; and sends out a CO for it (vs. the current PO tab Order row selection).  Screenshot attached. For Tablet:  Repeat above case, except use the checkbox selection for the different PO tabs/Symbols. Fix  - Proposed/BA agreed solution:   We clear all the selections upon any tab switching (including workspaces) for both Desktop and Tablet.

summary: RS - Cleanup Runtime
description_text: Refactor Rules runtime module based on feedback from David Mechner: 1. We seem to have lost jacoco coverage for the runtime? This is important. We should have close to 100% unit test coverage for this project. We seem to be low on coverage for the service. Good catch. Dropped during refactor/merge into Pano. Will be added 2. Dependencies should be kept tight. Why add logback for tests? Good catch. Will be removed == 3. Runtime PatternCache. I see why you did this but it's an anti-pattern to have global static state (effective java). I think the better way to solve the problem would be to move the matches method out of the Condition class to an evaluator class or utility, and you can privately cache compiled patterns inside that. I think the cache itself should be an instance variable not static global - at this point there's really no need to optimize for memory. Agree: Will make it an instance variable in an evaluator/utility, maybe in the RuleEvaluator instance 4. EvaluationOptions - seems wrong that the RuleSet Status should be here. The ruleset itself is passed in to evaluate, so it will be unaware whether it's CURRENT. Agree: CURRENT/WORKING/OLD is not a runtime concern (service layer concept). Will be removed 5. EvaluationMetadata. What's the need for this? I don't see it used. It doesn't seem worth adding additional classes (this metadata and the EvalautionResponse wrapper) in order to include timing data, and the other stats that seem of questionable value, as a standard part of the service. We'll want to measure performance but I don't see it being something we monitor at runtime or that normal users will want. Agree: this belongs in development/debugging. Will be removed 6. Execution Strategy. I think could be named better - maybe "OutputInitializationStrategy"? Also why mention order in the enum names if it's always ordered? Point noted. Need to revisit 7. MatchingStrategy. I don't think we should add strategies and associated code until we have a real us…

summary: Client Ruggedization - Frontend logging cleanup
description_text: During investigation of connectivity issues related to the new WebSocket connections implementation, several logging problems were identified: Duplicate log entries caused by session ID (sid) grouping Incorrect chronological ordering of log entries Stale logs appearing from previous days Resolution This ticket addresses these logging issues to ensure accurate and reliable log output for debugging WebSocket connectivity problems. Notes remains relevant as log ordering issues can still occur when logs are sent by different worker threads running on different timers.

summary: Client Ruggedization - Websocket reconnect WARN duplicate rows
description_text: Upon WebSocket reconnection, the WebWorker table snapshot performs a state reset. When updates are pending due to conflation, rows may be inserted twice, triggering WARN logs. While functionally harmless, this creates severe performance issues: Many WARN messages logged in a single reconnection event (locally seen  ~5,000) Client becomes sluggish due to log overhead Excessive diagnostic requests sent to backend Root Cause The teardown process does not account for pending conflated updates, creating a race condition where: Reconnection triggers state reset Pending updates from pre-disconnect state are still queued Duplicate insertions occur as both old and new snapshots process Solution Fix teardown logic to properly clear/handle pending conflated updates before reconnection state reset Only log duplicate row warnings once, preventing log flooding if other issues occur in the future Notes I revived toxyproxy Docker container for controlled disconnect/reconnect scenarios. This was required to easily test disconnect/reconnect scenario. This has no impact on production code. See compose/toxiproxy/README.md for more information.

summary: Server side front-end logs splitting and reordering
description_text:
Problem Client logs are difficult to read due to these issues: Out of order: Different user sessions and worker threads run on independent timers and send log batches at different times, causing entries to appear out of sequence Delayed relative to server logs: When comparing client logs side-by-side with Java logs, there's a time offset that makes correlation difficult Next-day delivery: Logs persisted in local storage may not be sent until the following day, creating significant gaps Proposed Solution Implement server-side buffering and reordering in ClientLoggerService: Buffering mechanism: Client continues sending logs every 5 seconds Backend buffers incoming logs for CLIENT_LOG_BUFFER_SECS (30 seconds? Longer is better) Backend reorders and writes logs that are older than ServerTime - CLIENT_LOG_BUFFER_SECS - PADDING (where padding is ~10 seconds. Need to be > than the 5 secs client interval) Written logs are removed from the buffer Logs from previous days will be discarded Logs older than the buffer window will be marked as "DELAYED" Client logs will be written to a dedicated file: app-svcs-client.log  (also clad and pad since they use the same logger?) Easy to read logs Format [TIME LEVEL] [SESSION_INFO] [MESSAGE] eg: 2025-11-21 12:30:36.610 ERROR panotest1@pragmatrading.com-d-37a1 (10.150) [RSocket Connection Manager] Client error from connectionStatus: ws://localhost:3000/api/rx-stream.
Error: RSocketClient: Connection closed.
    at Object.onNext (http://localhost:3000/node_modules/.vite/deps/chunk-GT7NHYJQ.js?v=a210f843:4163:36)
    at FlowableSubscriber.onNext (http://localhost:3000/node_modules/.vite/deps/chunk-GT7NHYJQ.js?v=a210f843:2232:55)
    at http://localhost:3000/node_modules/.vite/deps/rsocket-websocket-client.js?v=99007997:162:68
    at Set.forEach (<anonymous>)

2025-11-21 11:30:36.611 INFO panotest1@pragmatrading.com-d-37a1 (DELAYED 136.69): [RSocket Connection Manager] Client error from connect callback: ws://localhost:3000/api/rx-st…

After clicking on another row the bright yellow selection highlight is replaced with lighter shade yellow color which also overrides previously applied Red Error or Yellow Warn row highlight color. The logic of this behavior is located in  client/rx-react-components/src/grid/with-rx-grid-targeted-row.hoc.tsx Per   we will need to keep the initial bright yellow highlight that gets applied after clicking an alert action for the sake of focusing on the order that triggered the alert. summary: Web Socket reconnect retry count does not reset
description_text: During a recent code simplification, the line that resets the Web Socket connection retry counter after a successful reconnection was accidentally removed. The application allows 5 reconnection attempts. Once those are exhausted, the Application disconnected modal appears. With this issue, if a user disconnects and reconnects on the second attempt, the counter is not reset, so only 3 attempts remain for the next disconnect. This affects only the old websocket connection logic. Users entitled with WS_SHARED_CONNECTION are not impacted.

summary: Netty unable to execute native library from /tmp
description_text: Background On startup, MDF (and possibly other apps) log: 2025-11-14 06:00:15.888  INFO 49189 --- [pool-3-thread-1] i.n.util.internal.NativeLibraryLoader    : /tmp/libnetty_transport_native_epoll_x86_6415520532628075935686.so exists but cannot be executed even when execute permissions set; check volume for "noexec" flag; use -Dio.netty.native.workdir=[path] to set native working directory separately. The Problem, and Workaround This is being logged by the NeTTY library, a web I/O library used by our Pano2 applications. It has  native libraries  for many of the platforms on which it operates, and uses pure Java on the others. The native libraries are more performant, and we would like to use those when we can. The native library is unpacked into the temp directory, usually  /tmp , and is attempted to be executed from there. Often, as a safety procedure, the tmp directory is its own volume, with a "noexec" flag set, which prevents native code loaded from that volume from being executed. This prevents the NeTTY library from using its native transports. To work around this, there are two options: remove the "noexec" flag from the volume of the tmp directory add a JVM startup flag to set a new directory for net to extract its native libraries. To do this, add  -Dio.netty.native.workdir=/home/pragma/tmp  as a new JVM argument to the launch script for the application, specifying a path which is writeable and readable by the user ID under which the app will be running, on a volume without the "noexec" flag. Option #1 is the simplest. If we cannot do this for safety reasons, then use option #2. Adding the JVM argument if it is not needed has no bad side effects. Just make sure that /home/pragma/tmp exists, is writeable by the app's user ID, and does not have the "noexec" flag.

summary: POL - Respect badge when splitting
description_text: Context : Floor brokers are prohibited (NYSE Rule 122) from abusing parity by having multiple orders at NYSE for the same client/side/symbol but with different badges (ExternalUserID). There are exceptions - for example, a client may have multiple desks - so a more conservative view of this is that users should not have multiple  live  child orders  of the same parent order  at NYSE (or algo providers accessed via NYSE) but with different badge ids. Work : Users are prevented from splitting HighTouch child orders on desktop and tablet if there is a pre-existing  live  child order with a FirmTrader <> this user (email). Order Split To avoid confusion, we should not simply gray out the split option, but instead show the reason why this is disabled.  There are various options for this (toaster, modal, etc) - what makes sense to me is that we add a "chip" next to the Split option (action button in tablet, context menu on desktop) saying "parity". Example: ChildOrder.FirmTrader = "DPolen@pragmatrading.com". Sherlock Chan tries to split the Parent Order. Since ChildOrder.FirmTrader <> "SChan@pragmatrading.com", the user may not split the order. Group Split See  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-6389 These parent orders will display as ineligible if their ChildOrder(s).OrderStatus = Live and ChildOrder.FirmTrader <> this.user. For these orders, the Ineligibility Reason is "Parity". Routing Rules Out of scope for this ticket. No change required.

summary: POL - Respect badge when amending
description_text: Context : Floor brokers are prohibited (NYSE Rule 122) from abusing parity by having multiple orders at NYSE for the same client/side/symbol but with different badges (ExternalUserID). There are exceptions - for example, a client may have multiple desks - so a more conservative view of this is that users should not have multiple  live  child orders  of the same parent order  at NYSE (or algo providers accessed via NYSE) but with different badge ids. Work : Users are prevented from amending HighTouch child orders on desktop and tablet where FirmTrader <> user (email).  To avoid confusion, we should not simply gray out the amend option, but instead show the reason why this is disabled.  There are various options for this - what makes sense to me is that we add a "chip" next to the Amend option (action button in tablet, context menu on desktop) saying "not yours". Example: ChildOrder.FirmTrader = "DPolen@pragmatrading.com". Sherlock Chan tries to amend the order. Since ChildOrder.FirmTrader <> "SChan@pragmatrading.com", the user may not amend the order. Auto Amend This is out of scope. No change required. View Amend This is out of scope. No change required.

summary: AMS - DataQueryService should handle back pressure
description_text: Root Cause Analysis: Exception: reactor.core.Exceptions$OverflowException: Could not emit buffer due to lack of requests Location: DataQueryService requesting snapshot Root Cause DataQueryService lacks backpressure handling - When requesting a snapshot for the ROUTING table, the reactive stream uses FluxBufferTimeout_Patch.bufferTimeout() with:   - Fixed buffer size: 100 items (hardcoded)   - Timeout: 250ms   - NO overflow strategy configured The Problem // DataQueryService.java - NO backpressure handling   FluxBufferTimeout_Patch.bufferTimeout(flux, effectiveBatchSize, LIVE_BUFFER_MAX_DELAY) Fix Options : Add backpressure  buffer with  DROP_OLDEST  strategy Make buffer size configurable  via  application.yml Increase default buffer size  from 100 to something more reasonable (1000-10000) Add   logging  when items are dropped due to backpressure (Optional) Find other similar requests  in DataQueryService that do not have a backpressure strategy, add backpressure strategy Do not merge into 1.75 release branch without OK from Ambrish. May need to create AMS release branch off 1.75 (if not already created).

summary: RS - Merge Rules Repository into Pano2
description_text:
Context : Rules repository is a standalone repo, not part of Pano2. As a result, it cannot take advantage of Pano2 artifacts like Jenkins pipelines, CI/DP, Git PR reviews, etc. Work: Merge Rules Repository into Pano2. Key Differences & Challenges 1.  Spring Boot Version Mismatch Rules:  Spring Boot 3.3.5 (requires Java 17+) Pano2:  Spring Boot 2.6.10 (compatible with Java 17) Impact:  High - Major version difference Resolution: * Keep Rules on Spring Boot 3.x in separate module (compatible) 2.  Architecture Style Rules:  Spring MVC REST (blocking) Pano2 Backend:  Spring Webflux (reactive, non-blocking) Impact:  Medium - Different programming models Resolution:  Rules can run as REST module alongside Webflux modules 3.  Package Naming Rules:  com.pragmafs.rule.* Pano2:  com.pragmatrading.pano2.* Impact:  Low - No conflict, can coexist Resolution:  Keep Rules package as-is 4.  Configuration Location Rules:  Config in /config/application.yml (external) Pano2:  Standard /src/main/resources/application.yml Impact:  Low - Just a convention Resolution:  Move config to standard location during merge 5.  Database Rules:  PostgreSQL, schema `pragma_rules`, manual init.sql Pano2:  Flyway-managed migrations Impact:  Medium - Need to migrate to Flyway Resolution:  Convert init.sql to Flyway migration scripts — Merge Strategy Rules as Backend Module Add Rules as a new backend module `backend/rule-services` within Pano2's multi-module structure. Advantages: Clear separation (Rules is independent service) Minimal changes to existing Pano2 modules Can maintain Spring Boot 3.x + Java 21 in Rules module Structure After Merge: pano2/
├── backend/
│ ├── rule-services/ ← NEW MODULE (from Rules)
│ │ ├── build.gradle
│ │ └── src/
│ │ ├── main/
│ │ │ ├── java/com/pragmatrading/pano2/rule/ ← Renamed package
│ │ │ └── resources/
│ │ │ ├── application.yml
│ │ │ └── db/migration/ ← Flyway scripts
│ │ └── test/
│ ├── common/
│ ├── rx-services/
│ └── ...
├── rule-frontend/ ← NEW MOD…

summary: MDM-L2:  11-13 issue reported by FB support for L2 for Symbol INGR.
description_text: Background Reported in PROD. Reproduced in QA:  1.75.0-rc99 Scenario:      reported L2 being off for Symbol INGR.   L2 showed cross markets while trying to reproduce in QA.  Got Jamie to look at the logs around the time.   See his analysis below. Requirements Explain why the Top-of-books appears to show a different inside than the SIP. Analysis Summary The SIP and the top-of-books quotes from various exchanges are independent data streams, they are not guaranteed to be completely synchronized. If one venue is crossing another, some order is usually quickly routed out to rectify the cross. A transient communications problem may cause a quote to appear 'stale' at a venue, showing a cross which does not really exist (the quote is unlikely to be hittable). This usually results in "self-help", where the crossing, unresponsive venue is temporarily removed from the SIP calculation for this symbol. I do not have any information about this particular case to know if that happened here or not. The prices were moving rapidly, and some venues were slow to respond, causing them to temporarily cross the SIP. Details I looked at time 14:01:00. At that time the NBBO for INGR was  200 @ $108.66 / 300 @ $108.84. This was obtained by grepping for lines containing "NBBO" and "INGR". 2025-11-13 14:01:00.009  INFO 63610 --- [t thread. summary: MDF Log Warnings
description_text:
Background See IL-3819 Requirement For each warning in IL-3819, document below if it can be removed in the code (changed to INFO). If it can be changed to INFO, do so. If it cannot safely be changed to INFO (if it is warning of a real issue), document why, and document remedial action which can be taken to address the concern. Update IL-3819 with the results. Copied from the IL: 1. Upstream conflation of  Quotes or Trades  is not available. 2025-11-13 06:00:16.518  WARN 25693 --- [           main] c.p.p.m.provider.activ.ActivFeedAdapter  :
Upstream conflation of Quotes is not available. Not using upstream conflation of Quotes.
2025-11-13 06:00:16.518  WARN 25693 --- [           main] c.p.p.m.provider.activ.ActivFeedAdapter  : Upstream conflation of Trades is not available. Not using upstream conflation of Trades. These warnings are logged when MDF is configured to use conflation but the Activ user ID which it is using is not configured to conflate quotes or trades. This warning is provided to notify PE that the application is unable to perform in the way that it was configured, so it is degrading to the best available performance (not conflated, in this case.) Currently, MDF is hard-coded to request upstream conflation, at a half-second interval. If we'd like to expose this setting as a variable that can be adjusted by PE, please raise a ticket for it. To enable conflation, check the Activ user ID that is being used for this MDF, and reach out to Activ to enable quote and/or trade conflation for this user. 2. Stack Trace This is caused by a software defect, which is fixed in this ticket. The fix removes the exception which causes the stack trace to be logged. 2025-11-13 07:40:14.467  WARN 25693 --- [tor-http-nio-43] com.pragmafs.common.activ.feed.LogOnce   :
Could not find primary market for BOWN in secmaster
2025-11-13 07:40:14.469  WARN 25693 --- [t thread. Id: 1] c.p.pano2.mdf.data.MarketDataCache       :
Failed to look up 'BOWN' in secmaster, could not set …

summary: Nucleus JDK21 patch version for OSX Tahoe Docker bug
description_text: Background: When running JDK21 in a docker container on OSX Tahoe, Java crashes. Nucleus will not be able to run on local develop machines.   is facing this issue now. More details from Jamie: This issue was seen yesterday on a Tahoe Mac running OpenJDK 21:   A fatal error has been detected by the Java Runtime Environment: # # SIGILL (0x4) at pc=0x0000ffff6bdccc5c, pid=828, tid=829 # # JRE version: (21.0.3+9) (build ) # Java VM: OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM (21.0.3+9-LTS, mixed mode, sharing, tiered, compressed oops, compressed class ptrs, g1 gc, linux-aarch64) # Problematic frame: # j java.lang.System.registerNatives()V+0 java.base@21.0.3 # # No core dump will be written. Core dumps have been disabled. To enable core dumping, try "ulimit -c unlimited" before starting Java again # # The crash happened outside the Java Virtual Machine in native code. # See problematic frame for where to report the bug. There is an OpenJDK bug with symptoms that match this. See  https://bugs.openjdk.org/browse/JDK-8345296  The workaround is to add this to your Java command line:  -XX:UseSVE=0  This sets the Scalable Vector Extension level for AARch. See  https://developer.arm.com/Architectures/Scalable%20Vector%20Extensions This is an intermittent OpenJDK bug which occurs only on a Mac running Tahoe, when running  Java within Docker . It does not occur on Sequoia. If you have not yet done so,  I recommend that you don't upgrade to Tahoe yet. ---- Resolution: Jamie said he thinks they released the patch for JDK21; we are waiting for them to confirm. Nucleus team will need to test the JDK 21 patch version with the 2 latest nuc releases. After nucleus confirms that the patch JDK does not break nucleus, you will need to update the centos 7 base image for nucleus. add jdk 21 patch version to Artifactory update dockerfile in Tooling_cluster: centos7_base/pano/Dockerfile.centos_oracle_jdk21 rebuild the base centos_jdk21 base image make any necessary adjustments for selecting the new centos_jdk21…

summary: BOND - Ability to add a limit for an RTS within the staged workspace backend
description_text: Currently a user does not have a way to add a Limit to an RTS in the Staged workspace. However as part of the apply strategy order ticket we show the option in Provide for - Use Limit Only. If they enable this and then submit the order to ADX, the algo rejects this param since there is no limit on the order. the user then has to go in again and modify the order to add Use Limit Only This workflow in general is inefficient and involved multiple clicks for a user. Can we allow users to add a Limit within the RTS workspace for RTS which currently dont have a limit . Some things to note Implementation sketch Backend ctrl-svc/ctrlg recognizes the mod as an RTS Mod, and submits the mod to blgw When the Rx service receives an update, it applies entitlement checks and then forwards the data to the UI. RTS mod requests come with modification parameters (without entitlement data). These mod params are echoed back from BLGW as RTS updates. Since BLGW does not know about entitlements, we need to enhance the request parameters with entitlement data so that when Rx receives the update, it has the necessary entitlement fields to apply checks. mog - implement a new 'rts-order-mod' api. This api just takes the mod fields and republishes the RTS PO with the mod fields. blgw - implements a new 'rts-order-mod' api. This api just takes the mod fields and republishes the RTS PO with the mod fields. the RTS PO with mod fields are published to rx-svc and then to the the UI mod-fields are preserved as part of the RTS PO in rx, and published by rx like other PO fields, so UI refresh and other UIs will all see the mod fields (limitPx))

summary: Adjusting Patch release pipeline logic
description_text: Background:   There was an issue discovered when cutting release/1.76.0-rc49-patch-p1. The artifacts for UIGateways showed changes for release/1.77 and release/1.78. Investigation: We discovered that there is logic that looks for the branch_name parameter branch in the 3 following repos: pano2 uigateways pano2cfg If the branch does not exist, pipeline will create a new branch from develop branch. this is how the unexpected changes got into the RC for UIGateways. Resolution: adjust the logic in the Jenkinsfile.releasepatch Lines 111, 117, 123, and 201. If the branch does not exist, fail the pipeline with the error message "BRANCH DOES NOT EXIST, PLEASE CREATE THE BRANCH IN PANO2, UIGATEWAYS, AND PANO2CFG" this pipeline is going to be retired in the next 2 months; no need to make it fancy or rework it extensively. Developers are aware of this issue and agreed that they will be responsible for creating the branches prior to a release patch cut. create the PR for release/1.75, 1.76, 1.77, 1.78, and develop. will create a runbook incase a cut ends up having a similar issue, what to do. To test: create a test branch in all 3 repos from the base: 1.76.0-rc49 new branch name: release/0.76.0-rc49-patch Run with the following parameters from pano2-jenkins-dev: compare_tag: 1.76.0-rc49 release_type: new_release (for first RC; existing release for rc2) branch_name:  0.76.0-rc49-patch PAN-10786

summary: ADX - Columns disappeared after switching from "staged" workspace
description_text:
After applying some filters to do some searching in the "Staged" workspace and switching back to my main view- My summary grid columns disappeared on one of my workspaces under 1 column and on my POG in my other workspace – See screenshots below and note workspace. Resolution Looks like a race condition where the grid is destroyed and we read the grid state and the gridApi returns an empty state. Lucky, the grid throws a warning rx-grid-v2.component.tsx:256 AG Grid: Grid API function getColumnState() cannot be called as the grid has been destroyed.
Either clear local references to the grid api, when it is destroyed, or check gridApi.isDestroyed() to avoid calling methods against a destroyed grid.
To run logic when the grid is about to be destroyed use the gridPreDestroy event. Look like checking for "gridApi.isDestroyed()" before triggering a state change fixes the issue.

summary: Handle Fractional Shares Published by ACTIV
description_text: Context : Effective February 23, 2026, FINRA/NYSE TRF® ("NYSE TRF") will support fractional share quantity reporting and dissemination to the UTP and CTA Security Information Processors (SIPs) for NMS stocks, in accordance with FINRA's Trade Reporting Notice dated March 22, 2024. NYSE TRF will not support fractional share reporting for clearing transactions. Please refer to FINRA's notice above for additional information on firms' reporting obligations. Key Dates Customers are encouraged to test in the NYSE TRF testing environment which is expected to be ready by October 2025.  Additionally, the NYSE TRF has scheduled Saturday tests on the following days: November 15, 2025 December 13, 2025 January 10, 2026 February 7, 2026 Q101.14 : How should a trade for a fractional number of shares, for example, 100.5 shares, be reported? (Updated) A101.14 : When reporting a trade for a fractional number of shares, firms should report using both the Quantity and Fractional Share Quantity fields. Both fields must be populated for trades with a fractional component. In the Quantity field, firms should delete the fraction and report the whole number, except if the whole number would be 0 (zero). If the whole number would be 0, firms should round up to 1. Thus, for example, for a trade of 100.5 shares, the reported quantity in the Quantity field would be 100. For a trade of .5 shares, for example, firms should round up and report 1 in the Quantity field. Trade reports with a share quantity in the Quantity field containing a decimal or a fraction will be rejected. In the Fractional Share Quantity field, the entry of fractions must be in decimal format. Firms should report the entire quantity (with the fractional component) exactly as calculated up to six digits after the decimal, without rounding up. Firms may report with or without trailing zeros. Thus, for example, for a trade of 100.5 shares, the reported Fractional Share Quantity could be reported as either 100.5 or 100.500000. I…

summary: MDF:  Activ disconnect does not clear the "currently subscribed symbols" list.
description_text: Version:   Came across testing patch version 1.75.0-rc92-patch-p1. Issue :  It was observed that LastTrade was not getting updated while testing out a Size related patch fix.  (Screenshot attached).  And findings are per below. Sree's notes from Slack: ---------- Ok, this is a long-standing bug triggered by a disconnect from Activ - that happened in QA. When MDF disconnects from Activ, it does not clear the "currently subscribed users" list. . So one of these could happen a) the symbol was previously subscribed by at least 1 user-  MDF thinks it already has a  live subscription to Activ (but it does not bcos of the disco) so no NBBO, lastTrade b) symbol was not previously subscribed - MDF will make an Activ sub for this symbol, and it will work fine c) unlikely - symbol was prev. subscribed, all users unsubscribe by deleting the symbol from their Portfolio grid, AND no one subscribes for a minute (causing MDF to close the activ sub for that symbol) - the next subscriber for the symbol will find i works correctly. -----------

summary: Market Data displaying incorrect ask and bid size
description_text: Problem We are displaying the incorrect size time and sales of MDM window. We are showing 10k by 10k. That is incorrect. The correct size is 100 by 100. Why? We should be (before and now)  doing BID_SHARES =  FID_BID_SIZE x FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE. BUT, we were inadvertently doing BID_SHARE =  FID_BID_SIZE x secmaster.lotSize Last week, FID_BID_SIZE was # lots. FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE was the same as secmaster.lotSize, so it worked out fine. Today, FID_BID_SIZE = actual BID_SHARES, FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE = 1. If our formula was in-fact 1) above we would have been fine. But our formula is 2) - hence the error - for  most stocks we show x100. For stocks like GE where lot-size = 40, we show x40 Implementation: We know that going forward FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE will always be 1, and then deprecated at some point in the future. The reason it was there in the first place was so that the same calc i.e. 1) above would work before and after the transition. Since we're fixing after the transition, we might as well go to the 'works only after the transition' solution ie. BID_SHARE = FID_BID_SIZE. and ASK_SHARE = FID_ASK_SIZE So no more multiply by.

summary: Split ticket refactoring
description_text: The split ticket logic is difficult to follow due to how React hook dependencies are managed. Many <SplitTicket> props are objects instead of primitive values, causing unnecessary re-renders and unexpected state changes (e.g., form resets) when object references change. Converting these props to primitive values simplifies the component and reduces these side effects. Additional complexity comes from inconsistent initial value handling between hot buttons and child orders in amend mode. This has been unified for consistency. Finally, the order selection behavior differs for group splits. When an order is removed from the parent grid (e.g., by filtering), the ticket: Closes on Desktop Resets on Mobile This behavior has been stabilized to prevent disruptive resets and closures. QA This ticket fixes: PAN-10473 (IL-3671) and   (IL-3696)
