summary: EU - cog - Handle AJ Reject message
description_text: Background  - There can be cases where and AJ(Hit/Lift) message can be rejected (For eg, G spread calculation failed )by bondlink, we need to handle these rejects gracefully on pano side PI26 – Technical Design – Negative Yield & G-Spread Checks for ADX RFQ Child Orders - Auto-X/R (Mr. Robot) - Confluence Q&A The diagrams are silent on the messages that are sent back for a reject. Where can we get the info for what the reject message looks like when it hits SSFR ? 2 cases - when the Inquiry is rejected, and when the Take-request is rejected. Answer : Take Reject  (copied from comment 1/12/26 on AD-16939) When BL rejects Take (AJ),  They send AI = 297=17, 5727=DNT, 20017=MktxResponseId, 20117=MktxInquryId.    They send a 35=j which looks like  8=FIXT.1.1|9=204|35=j|34=172|49=MA|52=20251204-22:55:11.433|56=dcuspragmaCTAPI|45=59|372=AJ|379=AggressorQuoteRespID-1829|380=0|58=Negative G-Spread, -52.0|20117=6275874|20120=AdaptiveAutoXChildTrades-Open|20017=50534615|10=070| There could be more scenarios in which AJ message can be rejected {-}SSFR transforms it into an ER - 11=MktxResponseId, 39=4, 150=4   <{-}---   this is what is on tib for COG SSFR transform it into ER - 35=8 and 39=8, 150=8, COG receives it but need to check if it sends the message further Inquiry Reject to do Can we assume reject messages are similar for ASAP and BIN A: These rejects are simply 35=j(which is a businessMessageReject) What is the scenario where it the Inquiry passes validation for -ive G Spread, but the Take fails validation A: Inquiry will pass a -ve G spread validation because the validation actually happens only when response comes and we are validating response for all checks. Any diffs in handling for ASAP vs BIN to be aware of ? A:

summary: Ability to edit LimitPx (3066) straight from the PO grid
description_text: The Rates team is looking to have the ability to edit LimitPx (3066) straight from the PO grid like we do in ADX. Currently, for a user who wants to edit the limit price, they have to right click -> Amend and adjust the Worst Rate (same as LimitPx). The Rates team is ideally looking where a user can ideally double click into the LimitPx column in the PO grid and the cell transforms into something similar to what we have in the Amend screen with the up/down toggle arrows as well. These arrows if clicked on an order with no limit price snap the far side of the limit (offers for buys, bids for sells) and then increment/decrement the price. A user should also be allowed to type in the price just like they can do in the Amend screen.

summary: Rates - Populate Start/End times on Manual order ticket
description_text: The Rates team is looking for the order ticket for the Manual order ticket entry screen to have the Start Time (3026) and Firm End Time (3027) to be populated just like it is on a regular order. Start Time should be populated with the current system time at the time of opening the manual order ticket screen and Firm End time should be populated with the market hours end time for that day (most days 17:00). The Manual order ticket currently shows those two fields as blank: This change should be applied to  ALL  strategies in the manual order ticket.

This logic should be added to the method that enhances or sets reference data for bonds.

summary: Determine why instrument Q WI showed as "OPEN" before its opening trade on Oct 27th
description_text: Background On Monday, October 2nd, the instrument with pragma symbol "Q WI" opened late, at 10:47:01. (The ACTIV symbol for this instrument is "Qw".) Before 10:47, a trader reported seeing the instrument as "open" in the Pano2 UI. Investigation In MDF, we have a relatively rich selection of trading states: UNKNOWN: We do not yet know the trading state NON_REGULAR_TRADING: Either before the open, or after the close. This venue is not currently executing normal trades of this instrument, although pre-market or post-market trading is allowed. NON_REGULAR_TRADING_HALTED: Trading of this instrument has halted at this venue. It is before the open, or after the close. REGULAR_TRADING: The venue accepts and executes regular trades of this instrument. This is during regular trading (after the open, before the close), and we are not halted. REGULAR_TRADING_HALTED: Trading of this instrument has halted at this venue, during regular trading (after the open, before the close). because of this halt, this venue is not currently executing trades. QUOTE_ONLY: Trading of this instrument was halted at this venue, during regular trading (after the open, before the close), and was then halted. Part of the resumption of trading at this venue is a quote-only period, during which passive orders are accepted but aggressive orders are not. We are now in this quote-only period. NON_REGULAR_QUOTE_ONLY: Trading of this instrument was halted at this venue. It is before the open, or after the close. Part of the resumption of trading at this venue is a quote-only period, during which passive orders are accepted but aggressive orders are not. We are now in this quote-only period. The Pano2 user interface has a much narrower set of trading states. It defines only     OPEN,  HALTED, and SHORT_SALE_RESTRICTED. We map between the back-end trading states and the UI's trading states as follows: Trading State UI Displays REGULAR_TRADING OPEN NON_REGULAR_TRADING OPEN REGULAR_TRADING with short sale restric…

summary: Data API - Build Resume Stream Capability
description_text: Build capability to resume data delivery from a temporal marker. There is a  hidden  column (it does not appear in entitlements), 'time', that captures the time BLGW received update from BL: send 'time' to client for every order (in snapshot) and order-update (streaming) client can send a 'resume from' param, with 'time' filter snapshot orders by 'time' - everything order with time earlier than this is dropped Details: Specifications .

summary: Data API - Deliver Streaming Snapshot (instead of single JSON)
description_text: Snapshots should be delivered as framed (start/end) streaming events instead of a single JSON payload. This is necessary to prevent huge payloads that may stress/overload the server/network/client. Details: Specifications .

summary: Pol - use the single snapshot-plus-updates websocket call instead of 2 calls - snapshot, update for rx and db-svc
description_text: Background SA continues to find lots of quirks, problems around very short-lived websocket calls. Analysis of logs also shows that half the websocket calls have a lifetime of less than 1 sec - see  here for log analysis . If we can combine the snapshot+updates into a single snapshotPlusUpdates call, this will: halve the number of websocket calls. significantly reduce the number of less-than-1-sec-lifetime websocket calls, which  may  be contributing to the connectivity issues. To do Backend Implement a snapshotPlusUpdates websocket calls in rx and db-svc and mdf. (already done for rx and db. mdf does not have snapshots - see branch: feature/PAN-10729) Notes This is a new API endpoint, the old ones will remain, so this PAN is backward compatible. Dont believe we need nginx changes for this new endpoint UI Modify Polaris (only) to use the new call instead of the current 2 calls to snapshot, then updates. If possible use a feature flag so we can try this out for a few users while keeping the old behavior for the majority

summary: POL - Show Risk Rejects in Burnt Amber
description_text: Background Users may receive a flood of alerts at the end of the day. They use the alertCategory (INF, WAR, ERR) to help distinguish what they should prioritize. To make this easier, we use a gold background for WAR/ERR alerts to draw the eye, leaving INF as white. Motivation However, users want more information than this - they want to triage across different types of WAR/ERR alerts. To make this easier, they want alerts to show in different colors - for example, they want Risk alerts to be in red or burnt amber. Example: User receives a new order - and the new order alert appears in gold. User receives another new order - but this one fails risk. The risk alert appears in red/burnt amber. Goals Make it easy to identify which alerts are most important when the user is busy Backwards compatible as this change will roll out first in PAN and then later in Nuc https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/NYSEPOL-952

summary: POL - PAN CLAD remove risk policy defaults
description_text: Background Firms can control their client's risk checks. One set of risk checks is called the "Risk Policy" found in CLAD under Clients, and it checks each order (fat finger). This ticket is only about Risk Policy. Users have been accepting the Polaris defaults for Risk Policy without explicitly setting their own. Goals Force the user to explicitly choose the parameters for the risk policy for Clients and Client Groups. Do not affect existing clients. See  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/NYSEPOL-949  for details.

summary: Fix IL-3773 - add asset class filter before processing inbound-from-IS messages
description_text: Background blgw listens to the same topic for Bonds and Rates for inbound messages. So both Pano Rates and Pano Bonds receive both Rates and Bonds messages from IS. We want to make sure a Rates blgw drops all Bond messages as early as possible and vice versa. This is the root cause for IL-3773 Todo Review all code where blgw receives messages from IS. Update the code to drop messages that are not for the configured asset-class i.e. Rates should drop messages if they are not for Rates and Bonds likewise. Implementation Current Kafka request view consumer receives RequestViewModel which contains assetClass. that can be used to filter the view consumer messages KafkaRequestViewConsumerService#initReceiver. Note here this class already filtering but needs to refactor filtering method to using assetClass from config. And Current kafka command status consumer receives ExtendedCommandStatus which doesn't contain asset class. To address this. There are two type requests for which we get command status updates. one is adx-submit, other is unsolicited submit. In all cases, before submit request to kafka we generate UUID as groupId and set to kafka request message. and when we receive command status update from kafka we get groupId for each message(ExtendedCommandStatus). Our proposed solution is to enrich groupId with asset class(BOND or RATES) as prefix.  like <"stp" or "rts"> - <assetClass> - <generated-uuid-with-deleted-first- <4("stp-" or "rts-")+(5 or 4)length_of_assetClass> example if a UUID is b2a1fd80-dd37-4450-b328-3373e88b1855 then groupId we are gonna send to kafka for BOND as (stp/rts)-BOND-d80-dd37-4450-b328-3373e88b1855. and we will do that for adx-request and unsolicited order submit requests. The command status consumer receiver we will filter out message based on asset-class from groupId.

description_text: Context : Users on the NYSE trading floor experience intermittent errors noticed as general "slowness", "spinners", or even Polaris UI crashes. Impacted sessions sometimes show repeated authenticated api calls failing as network error, 403 forbidden, no content = leading to failures to load certain application front-end assets. Browsers (Chrome, Edge) are blocking redirect responses in CORS without credentials, which cascades into broken service-worker installation and PWA initialization. summary: Client Ruggedization - Single web socket connection per server

Scope: Introduce a single shared WebSocket connection for each backend, managed by the Main Thread. This centralizes connection lifecycle management and removes redundant connections. Each shared connection maintains its own reconnection logic. Add the CUSTOM_CONFIG.WS_SHARED_CONNECTIONS  feature flag to allow controlled rollout and testing before migrating all clients. See  https://confluence.pragmafs.com/display/PAN/PAD+Custom+Config+Overview

summary: Polaris nginx changes for better logging
description_text:
Background To help troubleshoot connectivity issues we want to stitch websocket requests across these logs - client (app-svc client logging) > nginx > rxsvc. However nginx logs websocket requests only when they are closed i.e. at the end, whereas we want the log at the start. We can do this using the 'mirror' directive in nginx. To do 1. Mirror endpoint Read up on nginx 'mirror' directive Modify nginx configs and add a mirror endpoint for all rsocket upgrade requests (rx, dbsvc, mdf). This will be a dummy endpoint of the same service . Make sure to use the works 'websocket ' in the endpoint name, so that it's clear on the nginx logs. Example endpoint = .../websocket-logging Add this new endpoint in rx-svc, mdf, db-svc to handle the dummy call - either return 200 immediately, or log and return 200. Logging may not be useful as the very next log entry is likely the websocket log entry. Logging may be useful if the websocket log does not have access to the sessionId or  requestId - this log entry WILL have that info Generate nginx configs, test and make sure it works - i.e. nginx should log a entry for the dummy endpoint at the start of the websocket upgrade request. 2. nginx logging related The ws_custom logformat currently logs $cookie_x_sid. We want to add the following: continue logging the $cookie_x_sid add header x_sid = $http_x_sid add header request_id = $http_req_id - this is for future use Update the log format in Polaris nginx templates as follows: log_format ws_custom_<env>
    '$time_local $cookie_x_sid $http_x_sid $http_req_id $remote_addr xff=$proxy_add_x_forwarded_for ::    '
    '($status/$upstream_status) $request_method "$host$request_uri" '
    '$cookie_pragma_zone/$arg_partition flr=$on_floor_prod/$http_x_on_floor '
    'us=$upstream_addr bs=$body_bytes_sent ssl=$ssl_protocol '
    'con=$connection ($connection_requests) tm=$request_time/$upstream_response_time '; 3.  ConnectionId header var for upstream  -- -Add a header var of the connection…

Spotless is a good tool for the job. Objectives: Configure Spotless in the root Gradle build for all submodules. Ensure Spotless uses the existing  checkstyle.xml  to enforce our project’s style rules (based on Google Java Style, with custom modifications). Automatically format Java (and optionally other relevant files) on build or via dedicated Spotless tasks. Include tasks:  spotlessCheck  (verify code style) and  spotlessApply  (auto-format code). summary: Analyze results of Oct 11th 40-share lot size test
description_text: Requirements Analyze results of the October 11th system-wide test of 40-share lot size. Determine which symptoms represent problems or bugs, describe those so we can make new tickets for them. Test Results Version:   1.75.0-rc92 in UAT   (Weekend test by BA from 10/11 with Activ for LotSize of 40). This is a critical bug ticket as this feature goes live early November. Issues  noted: L2 is incorrectly showing shares instead instead of lots. (DP:  I only tested TOB view; it (almost!) consistently showed shares._ Saw good examples of other reported issues including the T&S L1 being out of sync with the L2 TOB (SIP). See the attached Word doc. Development Notes : There are three independent issues reported here, which should be split into 3 tickets. TOB is being displayed in shares, often, and not lots. : All market data (NBBO, top-of-books, and depth-of-book) sent from MDF to the UI specifies quote sizes in  shares . When this should be displayed in lots (whole lots or fractional lots), the UI is to convert this quantity from shares into lots, and (where necessary) round as appropriate for display. I recommend that we begin investigating this in the UI. If it helps, enable client logging (JSON) in a non-production instance and record some top-of-books data being sent to the UI. TOB (a display that shows the inside bid and ask prices & quantities of multiple venues, with the venue names omitted from the display) does not match the SIP. [Update: we confirmed that this was expected in the test.] TOB sometimes showed a crossed market (bid at one venue being lower than the ask at what is probably another venue)  [Update: we confirmed that this was expected in the test.] : TOB displays the insides from a list of venues: AMER, BATSY, EDGX, NATL, NYSE, NSDQ, etc. Did these route to one another during the weekend's test, or otherwise take steps to ensure that they would not cross one-another, and that they would be consistent with the SIP? If not, that would explain why the to…

summary: Determine memory limit allocation for pnpm
description_text: Background We are getting gradle daemon disappeared unexpectedly more frequently now. Generally, we get this error when there is insufficient memory; however we have 16 CPU and 48GB allocated to the build agent. Gradle daemon we have 4GB max. Discussions have determined that there is no limit set for pnpm and it could consume more memory than the agent have available to it; thus causing an OOM error killing the gradle daemon. Requirements Determine what is the memory ceiling necessary for builds currently. Add a memory limit for pnpm (include a buffer, because pnpm's memory utilization will certainly increase over time as we add more code to Pano2) Inform DevOps what the limit is, and how much memory does pnpm need. DevOps will size the jenkins build agents accordingly.

summary: Investigate checksum mismatch Polaris_Concurrent Pipeline Runs
description_text: The nightly executions of the Polaris_Concurrent pipeline continue to experience intermittent connection failures (Error: connect ECONNREFUSED 10.70.5.109:443). These errors appear to occur frequently and correlate with test failures in both Protractor and Playwright stages. Example of Recent Failure: https://pragmallc.slack.com/archives/CKT0K5E95/p1760013967921409 Problem Summary: During nightly test executions, connections to the gateway at 10.70.5.109:443 are refused intermittently. These failures lead to a large portion of tests failing or becoming flaky, which significantly reduces reliability and makes regression validation inconsistent. Possible Causes to Investigate: Gateway (nucleus) availability or port saturation at 443 during high concurrency. Load balancer or network timeout configuration. TLS/SSL handshake interruptions or expired certificates. Limits in concurrent requests or rate-limiting applied by downstream services. Issues specific to pano2-mac test runners (connectivity, proxy, DNS resolution, etc.).

description_text: Issue:   As observed in Pano2 Bonds QA version 1.76.0-rc47 - Scheduled RFQ param is retaining dates in hot buttons, which is causing issues on the next day. When a Take hot button is created with the time as a past time in the 'Scheduled RFQ Time' field, it is saving the date as well. When a user comes in the next day and submits an order to ADX using this hot button, it is using the previous date for this field. Which leads to erroneous trading on the Take order. Note:- Reported by clients and reproduced by Teddy. Please reach out to him for further details. This issue is similar to Start and End time in order tickets back in the day.

summary: Rates - unsolicited - flag submit-to-algo for this flow so BL can differentiate it from a regular RTS submit-to-algo
description_text: Context Rates BL needs to differentiate the automated unsolicited (xls or order-ticket) > rts > submit-to-algo from a regular RTS > submit-to-algo. They need to do this because they do not want to create a new RTS for the balance in the case of a partial fill, as they do not want any RTS showing up in that flow. Need blgw should flag a submit-to-algo for the  unsol > rts > submit-to-algo  flow so that BL can differentiate these from the regular client rts > submit-to-algo, and act differently for this Implementation sketch blgw will use a different prefix for the group-id on the request: stp : for unsolicited > rts > submit-to-algo rts : for regular client rts > submit-to-algo Q&A Why the groupId ? why not some other field that's directly related to each order A: Ideally we'd add /modify a field to the order-details to indicate "stp". However we dont want to change the schema, as we're close to code freeze, and the only 'id' field on the orderDetails is the BLInquiryId which we cannot/should not modify. Does this mean that the groupId will now change for regular RTS A: For Rates, yes it will change from: <uuid 36 chars> to: rts-<uuid 20 chars> Will it change for Credit as well ? A: Not right now as we're only applying this to 1.77. However, when we release EU/ASAP, it'll be from the develop branch which WILL include this change

summary: Data API - Rate Limiting
description_text:
Rate limiting must apply to all endpoints: GET /api/v1/orders/metadata                                → Column (field) metadata
GET /api/v1/orders/snapshot                                → JSON snapshot 
GET /api/v1/orders/snapshot/compressed                     → Gzipped snapshot
GET /api/v1/orders/events                                  → SSE events only
GET /api/v1/orders/events/compressed?batchInterval=N       → Gzipped batched events, batchInterval in milliseconds, min: 1000 (default), max: 60000
GET /api/v1/orders/stream                                  → SSE snapshot + events 
GET /api/v1/orders/stream/compressed?batchInterval=N       → Gzipped snapshot + batched events Limits will be configured per client IP (default:  1 request per second ). Clients exceeding the requests limit must receive a  429 Too Many Requests  response. Clients exceeding the connects limit must receive a  503 Service Unavailable  response. Implementation   This rate limit is implement in nginx server via limit_conn and limit_req. value for limit_req is set 1r/s, Meaning it allows 1 request per second per client IP. and value for limit_cnn is set to 3. Meaning it allows 3 concurrent connection per client IP When request limit exceeds it returns 429 'Too many requests - your request rate is too high. Please try again later.' When connection limit exceeds it returns 503 'Too many connections - your connection rate is too high. Please try again later.'; QA Notes For testing limit use bash script like below for i in {1..5}; do
  curl -i -H "Authorization: Bearer test-token" http://localhost/api/v1/orders/events
  sleep 0.03 # 30ms
done

summary: EU - Response Data in Pricing Chart
description_text: For ASAP RFQs ONLY, sort the Response grid as follows: SortRank (2094) - smallest value sorts higher. Values may be negative. This is a sample sort order: -3.4, -1.5, -0.6, 0.2, 2.1, 3.3 TransactTime (2036) - earlier time sorts higher CounterPartyShortName (2086) - alpha sort Responses for BIN RFQs should continue to be sorted as is done currently - ( DecisionTimeRank, SortRank, DecisionTimeLevel, ResponseLevel ) Notes we do not expect to have partial quantity responses (they will be filtered out by BL ?), so quantity does not need to figure in the sort logic. FYI only related to too-good-level, nothing to code for these: for asap, too-Good should normally be on top, which will happen naturally when we sort on SortRank. If an ASAP RFQ has the <skip-too-good> taskParam set, we will continue to show the too-good level on top. This is unlike BIN where a too-good will sort to the bottom (based on DecisionTimeLevel value). Q&A How to determine if this Response grid requires ASAP logic. A: The PO will include a new (task-param) column RfqType (id tbd) whose value will be ASAP or BIN.  Pick a 20,000 series column for this for now, will definitely change later. Checking for ASAP does this mean we'll have BOND US, BOND EUR NON-ASAP, BOND EUR ASAP. A: NO. For now we have US/BIN, AND EUR/ASAP. In future we'll have US/ASAP. We dont expect to have EUR/BIN. We should use the logic noted above to determine when we want to sort using ASAP logic. Do NOT change the current sort-logic for BIN. Does this mean for BOND US? A: NO. It means use the current sort logic if <not ASAP> - see Q.1 above.

summary: EU asap - POG should not publish Response.SortRank for ASAP DecisionTime updates
description_text: Sree updated 10/18: This PAN is a no-op. POG does NOT publish Response.sortRank (2094). It only publishes Response.decisionTimeRank(2092). We will let POG publish DecisionTimeRank for ASAP as well as BIN. The UI will be modified to NOT use DecisionTimeRank when sorting ASAP. It will continue to use DecisionTimeRank when sorting BIN. This PAN is a no-op i.e. nothing to do. , Background The Algo publishes a DecisionTime task-stat. This stat is complex json that includes additional info for the PO, CO and  each  Response. This stat is parsed by POG, to extract info about each response, and then POG publishes Response updates for each response. The Response update includes a Response.SortRank column that is used by the UI to sort the response grid. For ASAP, we cannot depend on the SortRank from the DecisionTime stat because Responses come in before, and much more frequently than the DecisionTimeStat. Instead, COG will calculate and populate the sortRank on Response updates so it's available at the UI as part of the Response update. The SortRanks calculated by cog will be different than that calculated by the Algo. To prevent 2 different flavors of sortRank getting published, POG should NOT publish SortRank as part of the DecisionTime Response updates - and do so ONLY FOR ASAP. Q&A How to determine if this PO is a ASAP PO A: The PO will have a new task_param RFQType (id tbd) with a value of ASAP or BIN

summary: blgw - add kafka settings in app.yml to ruggedize against schema mismatches
description_text:
Add the following Kafka settings in the following places: ams/.../blgw/..../app.yml backend/...blgw/.../app.yml kafka producer: <entry key="auto.register.schemas" value="false"/>
kakfa consumer: <entry key="use.latest.version" value="false"/>

Make both configurable. The first setting ensures we auto-register new schemas. The second setting is likely more useful - will prevent errors because there's a newer version of the schema than the one we have in our mktx-data.jar.

summary: Rx-services support for MAX aggregation
description_text: Implement a solution for NYSEPOL-933 To meet the requirement of retrieving the most recent  OrderCreationTime  (7002) for Client and Rx services, we will introduce a new aggregate that captures the  latest OrderCreationTime  value. 1.  Rx services     –  Dev notes by AP: This is an embedded config and requires code changes if we need to update this. Two aggregation types were developed: MaxLatest Always returns the latest seen  OrderCreationTime  for a DMA summary row. No performance concerns. May produce  stale values  if a row moves from one aggregate to another (because the previous aggregate may contains an "old"  OrderCreationTime ). Max Returns  accurately  the maximum  OrderCreationTime  across the DMA_SUMMARY rows. Not has performant as  MaxLatest  but might be performant enough Implementation is complete but requires load testing to confirm it can handle  End-of-Day (EOD) DMA orders  without degradation. Using it will produce a WARN log to make sure it's not used without knowing the impacts. >>  Note :   The currently used solution is MaxLatest for this Rx service use. 2.  Client A new column aggregation has been implemented: MAX. For now, this is intended to be used only for DMA_SUMMARY since there might be some performance impact on larger data set (eg. EQ). There will be a WARN in PAD stating MAX is used for DMA_SUMMARY 7002 column. This is fine as long no other column shows up. PAD This will require a PAD sync to get the new DMA_SUMMARY.OrderCreationTime (7002) column and make it available by adding this column to the ViewColumnSet. Make sure DMA_SUMMARY.OrderCreationTime column is configured to use the new MAX aggregation.

summary: EU - Order ticket - hide fields that are not relevant for EU
description_text: The EU order ticket should hide the following fields that are not applicable to EU orders. LiveMarket related order params The AutoHedge option/button Code should hardcode the list of these cols, and hide these cols for EU/ASAP orders. TBD    or   - what triggers the "hide fields" logic. Options: EU orders - most likely If Secmaster can specify preferred price type, we could disable auto-hedge if priicetype = price Implementation options: DO THIS - Hide non-applicable fields based on the order type. For example trading on price -> hide AutoHedge; EU -> no LiveMarkets. Not this - Create separate order tickets for EU vs US. Depending on the order selected (EU or US), show the corresponding ticket. Because it appears that the hide-logic is based on characteristics of the order (e.g. Price -> no AutoHedge), and we'll have more of these down the line. Creating a separate ticket will not address this need Q&A 1. How do we identify a EU bond in the Client/backend A: TBD - most likely there's a set of product-codes in secmaster that mean Eu bonds and a different set of product codes for US bonds.  EU bonds will will not have Cusip; EU bonds ISIN will start with "X...", US will start with US.  2. What should be the error message for mix selection of RFQ (both US and EU). A: "Please select orders from a single region" 3. What should be the criteria/column to decide that these params should be hidden for the order ticket? A: If Demography is the best way to identify EU bond then we should use that 4. What should be the error message if Hot Buttons has the fields that should not be part of EU order Ticket and we try to apply the hot button? A: No need to show error message, just ignore the excluded fields for the EU RFQ. Notes: This logic kicks in for EU orders (for now). While we can create different EU vs US tickets in PAD, this is entitled per company. Consequently, this approach wont work for users who do both EU and US, as we need the same user to get different order ticke…

summary: EU - Change Take (hit/lift) and Pass buttons for ASAP
description_text: Background ASAP countdown timer workflow is different from BIN, and we need to adjust the behavior in Pano accordingly Requirement While an ASAP RFQ is active, the take/pass buttons must allow the user to either take or pass. We should not look at the countdown timers to decide whether to show the button or not, just TakeStatus. The “take” button should display the bestLevel as part of the button text. This ASAP take/pass behavior should be active only when the <bin-or-asap> column has a value of ASAP. For BIN, we want to retain the current behavior of the button. The BestLevel on the button must be updated in real time as the <BEST_RESPONSE_LEVEL 5365> column gets updates. A  Too-Good  bestLevel should be displayed differently on the button. Detecting too-good: If the best-level is too_good, then (5367 TooGoodResponseLevel) will be set. Note that the best-level displayed on the button will come from the <5365>PO column and may be stale compared the best-level shown on the Response grid. We see responses as soon as SSFR sends them. We see the PO.bestLevel only after the Algo publishes them - every 1 sec + some throttling. As a level is required to be specified on the “Take Now” API call, we should not allow TakeNow if there is no best-level. We should allow the “Pass” button even if there is no best-level. This is akin to the client canceling the RFQ, if for example there are no responses at all If BestLevel stat updates while the button is displayed, the level on the button should update to reflect the new best-level Important  - the C&D of the ticket should make a note to mark the cols we reason about as  required  in PAD. These cols - 5360 (TakeStatus), 5365 (BestResponseLevel), 5367 (TooGoodResponseLevel),  5374 (TakeDecisionTime), TBD (RfqExpiryTime) Displaying TOO_GOOD differently on the button: Text is different: e.g. “Take Too Good Level @ <level>. This means the button might grow longer. Color should be different Possibly a warning icon on the button for to…

summary: EU - time-left column on PO should count down differently for ASAP
description_text: When an RFQ goes active (5360 (TakeStatus) != null), the countdown timer logic is different for ASAP and as follows: Time Left Column The PO will have a  TakeDecisionTime (5374)  column. The Timer column (5368) should countdown to the  TakeDecisionTime (5374)  displaying a legend of <todo: time before algo makes a decision>. At the end of this time the Algo may decide to trade, and the RFQ is filled. In this case the timer columns should go blank once the RFQ is dead. The algo may also decide to pass. In this case, the <5368> column should countdown to the  TakeLatestDecisionTime (5393)  value, displaying a legend of <todo>. Scenario how to detect Time left (5368) notes no active RFQ 5360 = null blank active BIN RFQ 5360 != null  and  inquiryType = BIN current Bin logic for timer active Asap RFQ - phase 1 5360 != null  and  inquiryType == ASAP and currentTime <   TakeDecisionTime (5374) countdown. text = <time left> to Algo decision <time left>  =  TakeDecisionTime  - current time active Asap RFQ - phase 1 5360 != null  and inquiryType == ASAP and currentTime >  TakeDecisionTime and currentTime <  TakeLatestDecisionTime (5393) countdown. text = <time left>   to RFQ expiry <time left>  =  TakeLatestDecisionTime  - current time AlgoSummary - RFQ tooltip As part of this effort, we will need to update the AlgoSummary column. See below for details. TODO: review   with ASAP in context, and see if there are any other AlgoSummary changes required BIN - current behavior, no change - FYI Only TakeStatus countdownTime event style timer type Tool tip AwaitingResponse coalesce (manualWaitTime , decisionTime 'Review' (if manualWaitTime) or  'Trade/Pass' in-progress bin rfq-bin RFQ for ${qty} est. at ${level} in progress, collecting quotes. ${countdown} remaining until ${event} Will hit/lift decisionTime - will-trade trade in rfq-will-trade RFQ in progress. Will trade ${qty} at ${level} in ${countdown} unless overridden will pass decisionTime - action pass-in rfq-will-pass RFQ in …

summary: EU - Response Grid sorting
description_text: For ASAP RFQs ONLY, sort the Response grid as follows: SortRank (2094) - smallest value sorts higher. Values may be negative. This is a sample sort order: -3.4, -1.5, -0.6, 0.2, 2.1, 3.3 TransactTime (2036) - earlier time sorts higher CounterPartyShortName (2086) - alpha sort Responses for BIN RFQs should continue to be sorted as is done currently - ( DecisionTimeRank, SortRank, DecisionTimeLevel, ResponseLevel ) Notes That Response updates that specify the DecisionTime fields will NOT have this column because this column is published by COG, whereas the DecisionTime columns are published by the Algo & POG. we do not expect to have partial quantity responses (they will be filtered out by BL ?), so quantity does not need to figure in the sort logic. FYI only related to too-good-level, nothing to code for these: for asap, too-Good should normally be on top, which will happen naturally when we sort on SortRank. If an ASAP RFQ has the <skip-too-good> taskParam set, we will continue to show the too-good level on top. This is unlike BIN where a too-good will sort to the bottom (based on DecisionTimeLevel value). Q&A How to determine if this Response grid requires ASAP logic. A: The PO will include a new (task-param) column RfqType (id tbd) whose value will be ASAP or BIN.  Pick a 20,000 series column for this for now, will definitely change later. Checking for ASAP does this mean we'll have BOND US, BOND EUR NON-ASAP, BOND EUR ASAP. A: NO. For now we have US/BIN, AND EUR/ASAP. In future we'll have US/ASAP. We dont expect to have EUR/BIN. We should use the logic noted above to determine when we want to sort using ASAP logic. Do NOT change the current sort-logic for BIN. Does this mean for BOND US? A: NO. It means use the current sort logic if <not ASAP> - see Q.1 above.

summary: blgw - bind mktx-data jar at runtime instead of current compile time
description_text: Context We have had multiple disruptions in QA and higher envs due to incompatible mktx-data jars in blgw and BL. These disruptions require meetings, updates to blgw, patch/snapshot, and most troublesome is the need for 2 'release-level' blgw versions with different mktx-data jars that we currently cannot support. Requirement Add the ability for a single release-version of blgw.jar to work with different mktx-data jars, assuming there is no breaking change in blgw across the different mktx-data versions. The solution should work for the following deployment / run models local intelliJ local pc, using run.sh docker envs (discuss with   ) pano2cfg envs (QA) (starts the specific uber-jar) PE envs (starts all jars in the lib folder) Implementation sketch Ask chatgpt/claude this, should point you in the right direction. Verify it's not giving you bad info I want an uber jar AND one other jar that is externalized so i can specify different (compatible) versions fo the jar at runtime Notes Assume mktx-data jar is available in a standard folder (lib) at runtime. pano2cfg envs star blgw with java -jar blgw.jar PE envs start by specify a folder for all jars.

summary: MDS: Log subscription added/removed with FeedLogger, not at INFO
description_text: Background See  I pulled an example MDF log, ws-prod001.ny5.pragmafs.com.ny5-ws-mdf-blue.20250917-0615. It was 89 M for the day. MDS logs when a new subscription is created, and when it is removed. Switching this information to part of the feed log does not rob us of much important information, and would shrink that 89M down to 12M. Requirement Reduce the size of the MDF log, by switching log entries from SubscriptionManager, ActivSubscriptionManager, and ActivUnsubscriber to the FeedLog, so that they are not written unless the feed log is explicitly enabled. If possible, add batching to the FeedLogger methods which take a Collection of symbols to log. On startup, we log the symbols being subscribed to, and this is  thousands  of symbols, resulting in huge lines in the log, which are awkward when grepping for symbols. Splitting these single huge log entries into multiple, smaller entries results in more manageable output when grepping, and allows us to skip a lot of data if we have logging enabled for just some symbols.

summary: ADX/XPRO - Import and use @mktx/ui library
description_text:
We should now use the Platform-UI `@mktx/ui` npm library instead of maintaining copied code in our repo. This workaround was originally implemented due to limited access using the Pragma VPN. This includes using the provided design tokens (css variables) and the spreadsheet loader. VPN access issues should now be resolved for most developers, so we can move toward consuming the official shared build directly. Since some developers (Chandra, Rahul, ..) still not fully access the mktx network. The the mktx registry will be proxied by the Pragma artifactory. So nobody should see this error ERR_PNPM_FETCH_401  GET https://marketaxess.jfrog.io/artifactory/api/npm/mktxv-npm-prod-virtual/@mktx/ui/-/@mktx/ui-0.4.1.tgz:  - 401
No authorization header was set for the request. Links Setup guide for local and CI environments: Getting Started – External Usage

summary: (IL-3723) ADX - LimitPx applied to wrong order
description_text: When submitting the  limitpx  change, we’re using the internal grid row  node.id , which is intended for the Rx  rowId . It should match the  TaskId , but that’s not always the case. This could be an  ag-Grid  quirk or a bug in our front-end. The fix is to use the  TaskId  instead of  node.id . Looks like the code on our end is accurate and I cannot find any issues. With the following code I could prove the rx-client provided data is right: The output for rowId and taskId is good. The nodeId is wrong.

summary: Add disrupter as queue between activ receiver thread and mdss consumer
description_text: We are experiencing disconnect from activ. As part of  . we introduce a concurrentlinkedqueue between activ received and mdss processing. we were still seeing disconnect due to gc. As part of this fix we are introducing disruptor as buffer between activ received and mdss processing, also we are introducing Activ HeapmessagePool for lower GC invocation. HeapmessagePool will help us reducing object creation and allow us reuse object. https://support.activfinancial.com/modules/smartfaq/faq.php?faqid=308

summary: ADX/XPRO - Stream layout updates
description_text: ADX uses JSON Patch (PATCH requests) for layout updates. While this approach increases the risk of layout desync between browser tabs, it also presents an opportunity to implement and test layout streaming. Streaming layout updates (JSON Patch) across devices will eliminate synchronization conflicts. This enhancement will also improve the user experience by ensuring that Mobile Workspace modifications made on the Desktop automatically propagate to the corresponding Tablet Workspace. Eventually, AMS and WS should also adopt the PATCH endpoint and receive streamed updates. Layout synchronization will prevent errors when multiple app instances are open—an increasingly relevant concern for ADX, where the app may run in separate windows (e.g., OpenFin).

summary: (IL-3701) Silence unnecessary client errors
description_text: This was initially reported by the Polaris floor support, but the fix impacts all Pano products (EQ, FX, BOND, RATES, Polaris). Testing Polaris (both Cloudflare and Non-Cloudflare) and BOND in JXBrowser is sufficient. Also these errors where harmless. Fixed Autoplay failed error The initial error shown in the browser console has been silenced. This occurred when attempting to play a silent audio file on load when not running as a PWA. This behavior is expected when running directly in the browser. Context:  We play a silent audio file to enable background audio playback in PWA applications. This fix also resolved an issue encountered when running both Polaris UAT and Prod on the same device. Service Worker Registration Error Fixed an error caused by trying to load a non-existent file. This file existed under CRA (Create React App) but is no longer needed with Vite. Missing Column  Metadata Logs were showing multiple  Missing metadata for column map (colId: xxx)  errors due to missing columns in PAD. This can be resolved by updating PAD configuration either: Mark the column as  REQUIRED  if it should not be visible to users (not selectable in the grid). Add the column to the  View Column Set  if it should be visible. Known missing columns found in app-svcs logs: Alert – AlertType (7082) ChildOrder – CrossStatus (7088) ChildOrder – WorkflowDetail (7089) Also, the error  Missing metadata for column map (colId: status)  has also been fixed—this was unrelated to PAD configuration. Not Fixed Manifest Access Error Access to manifest at 'https://pragmatrading.cloudflareaccess.com' This is most likely a configuration issue on the Cloudflare side and requires further investigation.

summary: Pano2 Bonds: Stats for Limit Away, Defensive Limit and Wide Spread are displayed in the AlgoBehaviorSummary column for Cancelled and Done Parent orders.
description_text: Issue:   As discovered in Pano2 Bonds QA version 1.76.0-rc43 - Stats for Limit Away, Defensive Limit and Wide Spread are displayed in the AlgoBehaviorSummary column for Cancelled and Done Parent orders. Steps to re-produce:- Send a Parent order to ADX using any strategy, for e.g. Take. Wait for an RFQ to go out and verify that AR appears in the AlgoBehaviorSummary column (which means that the Algo is Passively monitoring for Contra RFQs and IOIs). Fill the order such that the Status of the Parent order changes from 'ACTIVE' to 'DONE'. Verify the AlgoBehaviorSummary and BondControlIOIStatus columns values. Send another Parent order to ADX using any strategy, for e.g. Take. Wait for an RFQ to go out and verify that AR appears in the AlgoBehaviorSummary column. Cancel the Parent order. Verify the AlgoBehaviorSummary and BondControlIOIStatus columns values. Issue:- For steps 3 and 6, the value AR appears in the AlgoBehaviorSummary column even though the Parent order is no longer active, hence AR does not make sense here. Also, the value in the BondControlIOIStatus column should change from 'Monitoring' to 'Disabled' once the order is Done or Cancelled. Please see the attached screenshots for the same. Notes:- As seen in the screenshot for filled orders, if the NextRFQ time is in the past (for an Active / Done / Cancelled order, i.e. irrespective of Parent order status), then RFQ countdown in the AlgoBehaviorSummary column shows 'Send in 0:00'. If possible, this should be fixed as well. However, as discussed with   , this is less important since the order and thus the RFQ time can be amended while the order is still active. And also, this is less likely to occur. Sree notes Ask   to recreate the bug in the QA env - quickest and most reliable way to see real data that causes the bug. Use the browser debug window, print and capture the response json using "_PRAGMA_DEBUG.getRows()". See  comment on PAN-10471   for how. Review the rules for "AR" in feature   to check if its …

summary: EU - cog - publish Response new/update on receipt of S, AI and Take messages
description_text: Background For US bonds, COG updates the status of a response based on the RFQ and Fill. Logic is pretty simple - new Response = Active, RFQ Canceled -> Canceled, Trade -> winning response = Done, rest = Canceled. Specifically, for US/BIN, SSFR does NOT process the AI messages with status updates to the Response, so COG/Pano never see these status updates. Related, SSFR publishes responses as ExecRpts (yes, raised eyebrow...).  Responses can be identified as (35=8, 39=D, 150=7). For EU, SSFR will start publish status updates from AI on the Response-ExecRpt. COG will need to process these and publish any status update as Pano Response updates. Requirement SSFR will publish Response-ExecRpts for S (New Response), and AI (Response Status update): S mesages are identified by: 35=8, 150=0, 39=D. Note they will not have 297 (status) AI messages are identified by: 35=8, 150=7, 39=D. They  will  be expected to always have 297 and 5627  copied across from the AI message .  With this Pano release, SSFR rules must be added to populate 297 and 5627. RfqTake messages - review and adjust as required Trade/Fill messages - implemented by PAN-10521 COG should process these messages and publish Response new/update. COG should also publish updates for any Responses whose sortRank changed due to the S. To do Do this for both ASAP and BIN. Expectation is that code is the same for both. When publishing Responses, the Dealerid (Cpty) value should be mapped to the Pano display value. This is covered in PAN-10520. The Response object in the cache should include the following fields at least ResponseId - required to discard response Updates (especially cancels) for prior responses from the dealer level - required for sorting status (maybe) - useful for debugging, logging. On receipt of AI: Look up response from RFQOrder cache by responseId If response is found,  extract the status (297), map it to a display value (use a map / enum for the mapping),  update response in cache,  and publish the…

summary: EU - cog - publish response updates from the STP fill message
description_text: For ASAP, the winning response for a RFQ may be a Response that was not seen by TE. In this case SSFR would not have published the winning response, and all the info about the winning response will be in the repeating group of the STP Fill. Starting with EU, SSFR will publish the repeating group of responses on the STP fill. COG should parse the repeating group to extract the winning response, and publish it as a Pano Response. Given the edge case that the winning response may never have been seen before, so publish all fields from the STP for this response. Note that you'll need to parse a repeating group of responses to find the winning response. Do this for both BIN and ASAP. Before implementation, ping    or    to get answers to the following qs What's the AD ticket to publish the repeating group on the STP fill and when will it be available What is the repeating group to check What are the fields in the repeating group How do we identify the winning response Do we need to publish Response updates to the other responses ? A: Yes, publish all response with all relevant fields from the repeating group TODO: Process ALL responses in the repeating group of the Trade / Fill message. For each response, publish all the relevant fields from the repeating group. At least these fields: status (filled/cover?/cxld, etc), level. See if we can do away with the cache of Responses, since all the info we need to publish a ResponseUpdates should be available from the repeating group. This means unwinding the current code that caches (BIN) responses by responseId If we still need the cache, change the key used to cache responses to dealerId (currently responseId). Do this for for both ASAP and BIN. -If a Response Update shows up AFTER we already published the responses from the Fill repeating group, log an INFO with the response details, and drop it - the Fill has the final response values.- Store each new response (S-msg) in RFQOrder cache as a ResponseUpdate object containing re…

summary: Pol -  false warning in browser debugger on PO selection
description_text: Version:  1.75-rc81 (QA Blue) Issue:   Browser debugger WARN seen after a few seconds when switching to a new PO.              Came across while checking out linked PAN-10445.   With the initial checkout with  WS_CLOSE_CONNECTION_MODE ='old'. Logging per Sree while we were checking out the debugger in QA Blue. Screenshot attached. Analysis In  , we added a watchdog timer on the Pano Client to track websocket connection progress. We log warnings if expected events (connected, onSubscribe) are not received in time.  We clear the watchdog timer when all expected events are received, indicating a successful websocket connection. That code has a logging bug - we clear the timer in onNext instead of onSubscribe. As a result, if we never receive an onNext (common when there are no updates after the initial snapshot within the watchdog timeout), we log a false warning. Fix Move the clearTimer code from onNext to onSubscribe. Notes the false warning also gets pushed to app-svc as a client warn, so lot of noise the false warning is for  any websocket connection  that receives no rows within 5 secs after the initial snapshot (no onNext) - so PO, CO, marketdata, dma, ...

summary: Implement distribution strategy for TransFICC proprietary JARs in builds (Tradeplant street-side FIX router)
description_text: Background We need to consume proprietary client API JARs from  TransFICC  to build and test the Tradeplant street-side FIX router. These libraries are not available on Maven Central. Vendor sources: TransFICC Artifactory:  https://transficc.jfrog.io/ui/native/customer-releases/  (requires credentials) TransFICC GitHub:  https://github.com/TransFICC/client-library  (dev team has access; contains build and release artifacts) Problem Decide how our build system should retrieve and cache TransFICC JARs and then implement the approach in our CI/CD and Artifactory, ensuring license/compliance alignment and minimal developer friction. Options discussed Download directly from TransFICC Artifactory during builds. Upload vendor JARs into  artifactory.pragmafs.com  under  ext-release-local  and consume like any other internal dep. Upload vendor JARs to a different (to-be-defined) path within  artifactory.pragmafs.com  and consume normally. Have DevOps own the upload/hosting process inside  artifactory.pragmafs.com  so builds pull from an internal, stable coordinate. — Note: For pano2, we already use a remote repository strategy (mktx Artifactory as a remote) so new versions require only a  build.gradle  version bump.

summary: blgw v1.76 - update MKTX data jar to latest 12.9 version.
description_text: Background When BL is upgraded to 12.9.0, we need to simultaneously upgrade  Pano v1.76  blgw to a version that uses the 12.9.0 mktx-data jar so that it works with BL 12.90. We need to do this because BL 12.9.0 is not backward compatible with mktx-data 12.8.x. Process based on the linked ticket: Get the correct version of mktx-data jar to use from   or SJakka, document in this PAN. Update Pano Artifactory with that Jar version (there's a Pano Jenkins job for this) Rebase this PAN from 1.76. Update blgw (gradle mostly) to use this mktx-data jar version, fix any compile issues (dont expect any) PR and merge to 1.76. Inform QA so they can cut a RC to test against BL 12.9.0

summary: Pol - improve IPs logged by backend apps on API calls
description_text: Sree 9/2 - Lot of these should hopefully be addressed by PAN-10496 and SA-27138. Wait for them to hit prod, and then re-visit this and fix any remaining issues. Need All backend apps log API calls from the Client with a log line similar to that below. This logging is valuable for troubleshooting related to connectivity, requests for a workflow and general troubleshooting. The log lines can be more useful if they allow us to do the following: -done - Isolate/track requests from a single device by  user's IP- Done in PAN-10496 using userid-<d-or-t>-magicNumber -done - Tell if a request is from tablet or desktop- see above {-}done - Tell if a request is via CloudFlare / NYSE Internet / Wifi (Tablet) / Other{-}. see log lines below. CF IPs are inferred and extracted from the  CF-Connecting-IP  header, and logged as "...from [cf:" versus "...from [ip:" for non-CF ips. Note the cf: <IP> - IP is the CloudFlare IP. To see the upstream IP, look at the xff entry. Sometimes this has an upstream IP, other times it's just the CF IP. Not sure why part-done. Tell the user's IP from the log line DONE - for non-CF IPs  this is the from [ip: <IP>. PART done - For CF IPs. Sometimes the xff has more than 1 IP, the first IP is the upstream-from-CF IP. Done - Always show the full x-forwarded-for path see xff in the logs below. If none, then show "-" Still todo Add the following info to the app-svc CLIENT log entries. We currently log these in the WebRequestResponseLoggingFilter, but we suppress that log entry for CLIENT logs, so its not easy to know. toIf we are unable to get this info on the CLIENT log entry, then DONT suppress the WebReqRspLogFilter for CLIENT log entries. This info cf or non-cf , and include the IP xff IPs We have added the nginx-connection-id as a header var -  X-Con-Id  in PAN-10705 . Log this in both the WebRequestResponseLoggingFilter log entry AND the CLIENT logging in app-svc. Log at a fixed place in the log, dont prefix it with a tag i.e. dont do:  conId: X-Con…

Ability to easily know the user/session/desktop-or-tablet from a log entry - especially nginx logs. Ability to easily extract / find / correlate from rx-svc logs all log entries for a specific websocket connection - from the time it is established (connected) till it is closed To do -   Client add a header var  x-sid .  Set its value to <user@firm>-<t-or-d>-substr(UUID, 4). "t" for tablet, "d" for desktop The value should be unique per Client login or browser refresh. It should remain the same for all requests between Client logins or browser refreshes. Include it on every request to all backends, both rest and websocket. To do -   backend app-svc logs - CLIENT log entries - add the x-sid cookie as the sessionId all app logs - WebRequest log entries -  add the x-sid cookie as the sessionId rx-svc logs - websocket events log entries  - add requestId of that request. Client log entries in app-svc.log have changed as follows. the "d" refers to desktop (vs "t" for tablet), and "3648" is a unique identifier to differentiate this session from other session for the same user.  #d2665a refers to the requestId from:

summary: Pol Client ruggedization - publish an empty Datarow as the first row for streaming update (vs snapshot) requests
description_text: This relates to Polaris IL-3631 - where CO subscription requests stop working. This change to assist troubleshooting problems. Context In   we added a watchdog timer in the Client / UI to log a warn if an rsocket connection failed to complete successfully. The warn also includes the stage we were waiting for - connected, and onSubscribe. When we receive both the connection is considered successful. Ideally we want to wait for onNext as well before considering the connection successful. We cannot do that yet because rx and dbsvc do not emit any onNext if there is no data on  update  requests Note - we don't have this  problem in snapshots, because we get an onComplete event for an empty stream that lets us kill the watchdog timer. Requirement rxsvc and dbscv should emit an empty DataRow on onNext for update requests so the client receives an onNext even for empty streams. This allows the client to know that the connection handshake is completed and in good shape, even on empty update streams, while allowing the client to log warns if it does NOT receive an onNext in the allotted time. To do rxsvc, dbsvc Modify rxsvc and dbsvc to emit an empty DataRow as the first row for batchedUpdates. This guarantees at least one DataRow is emitted, even if there is no data for the update UI In rscocket-client, uncomment this line so that the watchdog timer also checks for onNext // wdWaitingFor('onNext');  // re-enable when server publishes an empty frame even if no data

summary: Support custom user configs for Pano Client users, that is specified by Pano Admin and not the user
description_text: Context We want to be able to easily provide user-specific custom configs to the Pano Client. Custom-configs are different than user-prefs. User-prefs are specified by the user, whereas user custom-configs are specified by the Pano admin. They can be used in the Client to enable/disable features, configure tuning params, and any other user-specific tweaks that needs to be specified by admin and not the user. Supported config: Any config found in the .env files (VITE_...) WS_CLOSE_CONNECTION_MODE (simple, old, sure) WS_INACTIVITY_TIMEOUT number WS_KEEP_ALIVE_TIMEOUT number Requirement Backend App-svc should implement a secured endpoint  settings/custom-config  that returns a json of custom configs. Client Pano client will call this endpoint to retrieve custom configs. Implementation sketch PAD entitlement CUSTOM_CONFIG with Param1 = json config. Note: OP is responsible for configuring the json correctly, with configs that are useful to the Pano Client pad should be able to handle multiple CUSTOM_CONFIG entitlements/configs, and deep-merge multiple configs into a single json before delivering to the app-svc. in the event of mutiple configs, pad should merge them in an intelligent order - user configs override firm configs new endpoint in pad - settings/custom-config - returns a json of unified custom configs. new endpoint in app-svc - settings/custom-config - returns a json of custom configs app-svc calls pad endpoint

summary: Pol Client ruggedization - set timeouts and log-warn for connected, subscribed, on-next
description_text: This relates to Polaris IL-3631 - where CO subscription requests stop working. This change to assist troubleshooting problems. To do Add watchdog timers to rsocket connections that log a warning if the timer expires before successfully connected. The warning should specify which stage of the rsocket connection we were waiting on connected called onSubscribe called -defer - onNext called- don't do now, since onNext is never called if there's no data. We should modify rx, dbsvc to always emit and empty datarow so onNext is invoked at least once on the client confirming the handshake is completed.

summary: WS - Group Split : Pending Issues with MaxFloor = 0.
description_text: Version:  1.75.0-rc79 with Nucleus  polaris/127.24.0 : There were some issues reported with MaxFloor = 0 for single and group split respectively in the linked bug tickets:  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-10472  and  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-10480  . The issues reported in these bugs were fixed. However, upon testing various permutations and combinations for this functionality, we found some further issues for MaxFloor = 0 with group split. These are listed below:- Scenario 1:  Select 2 Market orders and open Group split dialog. Select Target as NYSE, strategy as Day, enter a valid qty and enter MaxFloor as 0. Issue 1:  No validation error for MaxFloor. Issue 2:  Split is allowed and is creating a child order with MaxFloor as 0 for the market parent order. Scenario 2:  Select 1 market order and 1 limit order which is not eligible (for e.g. it is Dead) and open Group split dialog. Issues:  Same issues observed as Scenario 1. Scenario 3:  Select a mix of Eligible and Ineligible orders and open Group split dialog. Select Target as NYSE, strategy as Day, enter a valid qty and enter MaxFloor as 0. Issue:  A validation error is displayed. However, the error message is not consistent with the usual message displayed for MaxFloor = 0 for Day and Scaling orders. Please see the below screenshot for the same. Scenario 4:  For Hot buttons with MaxFloor = 0:- Issue: We are able to save a HotButton with MaxFloor = 0 when the InterestType = null (no validation error is displayed for it, Error is displayed for InterestType not null though - as expected). When the button is applied to a Market order, you need to tab out of the field to see the validation error. You can then rectify the value in the field or null it which enables the Split button and allows you to split. Please see the attached screenshots for the same.

summary: WS - Group Split : Validation is missing for Group split: 'MaxFloor of 0 is only permitted when all orders are Limit.' when the selected orders contain Market orders.
description_text: Version:  1.75.0-rc79 with Nucleus  polaris/127.24.0 : Issue 1: Steps to reproduce the bug:- Send 3 High Touch parent orders with the following data Limit order: Side = Buy, Qty=1000, Price = 50. Limit order: Side = Sell, Qty=1000, Price = 100. Market order: Side = Buy, Qty=1000, Price =  null. Select all the 3 orders on parent order grid / summary grid and open the group split panel. Select the Target as NYSE, Strategy as Day, enter a valid qty and enter MaxFloor = 0 and tab out. Expected behaviour:- The validation error 'MaxFloor of 0 is only permitted when all orders are Limit.' should be displayed since one of the selected orders is Market (and is an eligible order). The Split button should be disabled. Actual behaviour:- The validation is not displayed for this scenario. The Split button is enabled. Upon clicking on the Split button, child orders with MaxFloor = 0 are created (including for the Market child order, which is incorrect Behavior). As per  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-6286 , must be positive except for Day and Scaling Strategies where InterestType=Null && OrderType=Limit. Note : When InterestType is not null and MaxFloor = 0, then the validation error is displayed correctly. Please see the attached screenshots for the same.

summary: MDF: Remove potential null pointer exception in logging
Use FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE in MDF, do not use FID_LOT_SIZE
description_text: Sree 8/18/25 - to be done post BBSS decom. Background Per  https://support.activfinancial.com/news-story/view?id=3578  , in September 2025 lot size (FID_LOT_SIZE = 793) will begin to be removed from all tables. As previously announced, two alternate fields were introduced, transaction lot size (FID_TRANSACTION_LOT_SIZE = 1478) and quote lot size (FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE = 1604). We need to ensure that our MDF Depth-of-book code reads FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE before September 2025. This was handled as PAN-10126. Now, see  https://support.activfinancial.com/news-story/view?id=3582  : The SEC has adopted new definitions for "Round Lot" information under the Market Data Infrastructure (MDI) Rules to enhance the transparency for investors and other market participants of quotes and orders in NMS stocks. In accordance with the SEC Final Rule, the following changes will be introduced: Support for a new round lot size value of 40 (in addition to existing allowed values of 1, 10, 100). The dissemination of bid/ask size in number of shares (rounded to down to nearest multiple of round lot size) rather than the number of lots. Currently, the quote lot size (FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE=1604) and the transaction lot size (FID_TRANSACTION_LOT_SIZE=1478) contain the same value (the round lot size). From the effective date, the quote lot size will change to a value of 1 to indicate that the bid/ask size now contains the number of shares. There is a related ticket in Tradeplant for this same work: AD-16799. Requirements Ensure that MDF reads FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE. See linked NYSEPOL-799 for requirements. Ensure that MDF does not read FID_LOT_SIZE. Ensure that MDF obtains the size of a quote from the quote size field (FID_BID_SIZE or FID_ASK_SIZE) multiplied by FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE, and makes no decisions based on the value of just one of those fields. See  https://support.activfinancial.com/news-story/view?id=3582  for the upcoming (November 2025) changes where FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE is expected to always be 1,…

summary: MDF: For depth-of-book, report odd lots as fraction of lot, one lot or more as round lots (rounded down)
description_text: Background For MDF Depth-of-book, sizes are reported in lots. Requirement For MDF Depth-of-book, if a size is between 0 and 1 lot, report it as a fractional lot, as a Double. If the size is 1 lot or higher, report it as an Integer number of lots, rounding down. So if a level has less than a round lot, the quantity will be a decimal between 0 and 1, representing a fraction of a lot. (How this is rounded and displayed in the UI is a display question, probably best tracked as a separate ticket.) If a level has one round lot or higher, its quantity will be a positive integer, obtained by converting the shares into lots using the lot size in our SecMaster (obtained from CLAD), rounding excess shares down to a the nearest lower number of round lots. So, if an instrument has a lot size of 100, a quantity of 99 shares would be sent to the UI as 0.99 lots, a quantity of 100 shares as 1 lot, a quantity of 199 shares as 1 lot, and 999 shares as 9 lots.

summary: Pano2 Bonds: AR stat is displayed in the AlgoBehaviorSummary column and BondControlIOIStatus is remaining as Monitoring for Cancelled and Done Parent orders.
description_text: Issue:   As discovered in Pano2 Bonds QA version 1.76.0-rc41 - AR stat is displayed in the AlgoBehaviorSummary column for Cancelled and Done Parent orders. Steps to re-produce:- Send a Parent order to ADX using any strategy, for e.g. Take. Wait for an RFQ to go out and verify that AR appears in the AlgoBehaviorSummary column (which means that the Algo is Passively monitoring for Contra RFQs and IOIs). Fill the order such that the Status of the Parent order changes from 'ACTIVE' to 'DONE'. Verify the AlgoBehaviorSummary and BondControlIOIStatus columns values. Send another Parent order to ADX using any strategy, for e.g. Take. Wait for an RFQ to go out and verify that AR appears in the AlgoBehaviorSummary column. Cancel the Parent order. Verify the AlgoBehaviorSummary and BondControlIOIStatus columns values. Issue:- For steps 3 and 6, the value AR appears in the AlgoBehaviorSummary column even though the Parent order is no longer active, hence AR does not make sense here. Also, the value in the BondControlIOIStatus column should change from 'Monitoring' to 'Disabled' once the order is Done or Cancelled. Please see the attached screenshots for the same. Notes:- As seen in the screenshot for filled orders, if the NextRFQ time is in the past (for an Active / Done / Cancelled order, i.e. irrespective of Parent order status), then RFQ countdown in the AlgoBehaviorSummary column shows 'Send in 0:00'. If possible, this should be fixed as well. However, as discussed with   , this is less important since the order and thus the RFQ time can be amended while the order is still active. And also, this is less likely to occur. Sree notes Ask   to recreate the bug in the QA env - quickest and most reliable way to see real data that causes the bug. Use the browser debug window, print and capture the response json using "_PRAGMA_DEBUG.getRows()". See  comment on PAN-10471   for how. Review the rules for "AR" in feature   to check if its a UI bug or bad data. For this specific bug, if…

summary: MDF: Enhance feed, client and JSON logging
description_text:
Requirement Minor logging improvements: When MDS FeedLog JSON logging is enabled, include the user ID, subscription type and symbol(s) in the log entry.  This makes debugging easier, by providing useful context. Add user ID and symbol filtering to JSON logging Assign a unique stream ID for each rsocket/flux stream in MDS, include those IDs in the logging. More clearly log when an L2 row is deleted. Developer's Notes I also added some minor clarification to the FeedLogger REST API documentation. ----------------------------------------------------------------
                FeedLog REST API. 
                - all commands are GET, prefixed by /api/internal/feedlog/
                - specify FX symbols WITHOUT the '/' i.e. EURUSD or eurusd
            ----------------------------------------------------------------
                Commands:
                ---------
                symbols/add/{symbol}        adds a symbol for logging
                                            The symbol '*' means "all symbols".
                                            If all symbols are enabled ('*'), adding a specific symbol has no effect.
                symbols/remove/{symbol}     removes a symbol from logging
                                            The symbol '*' means "all symbols".
                                            If all symbols are enabled ('*'), removing a specific symbol has no effect.
                symbols/clear               clears the list of logged symbols
                users/add/{user}            adds a user for logging
                                            Specify the entire user ID (joe_user@marketaxess.com).
                                            There is no wildcard ('*') to indicate all users, you have to specify each.
                users/remove/{user}         removes a user from logging
                                            Specify the entire user ID (joe_user@marketaxess.com).

summary: Rates Unsolicited - #4 Order Ticket - (intelligent) instrument search
description_text: Implement the UI work for feature  . Context: Treasury bonds are issued by the US Treasury on monthly online auctions. On-the-run Treasuries are the most recently issued Treasury bonds (or notes, which are not being traded on our platform). When new On-the-run bonds are issued, the previous On-the-run becomes the Old-on-the-run, and, the previous Old-on-the-run becomes the Old-old-on-the-run. The length of time until the maturity date is often referred to as the term or  tenor  or maturity of a bond. Tenors for bonds traded on the Rates platform are: 2Y, 3Y, 5Y, 7Y, 10Y, 15Y, 20Y, 30Y. The goal of this feature is to provide traders with an UI that enables rapid instrument search for order entry. Implementation Sketch: Bond Search ComboBox Summary Treasury bonds are grouped into categories: On-the-Run ( OTR ), Old-on-the-Run ( OLD ), Old-old-on-the-Run ( OOLD ), Old-Old-Old-on-the-run (OOOLD), and all bonds by  CUSIP/ISIN . Traders search by typing: type + tenor (e.g., `OTR 2Y`) CUSIP/ISIN (full or partial) Or a mix of both (tokens can be in any order) The dropdown should progressively narrow as the user types. "91" → Returns all bonds (all have "91" in ISIN/CUSIP) "2" → Returns all bonds (all have "2" in various fields) "2Y" → Returns only 2Y tenor bonds (exact match) "O" → Returns all bonds (OTR-2Y, OLD-2Y, OOLD-2Y, etc.) "OT" → Returns all OTR bonds (OTR-2Y, OTR-5Y, etc.) "OL" → Returns all bonds except OTRs (OLD-2Y, OOLD-2Y, etc.) "OTR" → Returns all OTR bonds (OTR-2Y, OTR-3Y, etc.) "OTR 2" → Returns OTR bonds that also contain "2" (OTR-2Y, OTR-20Y) "CMM" → Returns bonds with "CMM" in CUSIP/ISIN "4.250" → Returns bonds with exact coupon 4.250 UI State Controlled ComboBox input (text). `options` state for dropdown (initially all bonds). Derived search state from tokens. onChange Workflow Update `options` with filtered/sorted results. Progressive Narrowing as user enters characters in the textbox, tokenize and match each token against cached data (all token must ma…

summary: Pol connectivity - nginx logging improvements to help troubleshoot connectivity issues
description_text:
This relates to Polaris IL-3631 - where CO subscription requests stop working. This change to assist troubleshooting problems. Note: these are mostly logging improvements,  and optional for  Polaris 1.75. It will work fine without these changes. To do Pol Nginx  -  all nginx-templates, PE and non-PE 1. nginx-access log directive DONE - Update the access log-format to this: ######################################
# Logging
###################################### 
log_format ws_custom_${VAR_SUFFIX}
    '$time_local $cookie_sid $remote_addr xff=$proxy_add_x_forwarded_for ::    '
    '($status/$upstream_status) $request_method "$host$request_uri" '
    '$cookie_pragma_zone/$arg_partition flr=$on_floor_${VAR_SUFFIX}/$http_x_on_floor '
    'us=$upstream_addr bs=$body_bytes_sent ssl=$ssl_protocol '
    'con=$connection ($connection_requests) tm=$request_time/$upstream_response_time '; Log X-Session-Id header var on access logs Note: this requires the following Client changes in order to work - PAN-10496) - set X-Session-Id header var cant-do, not supported change timestamp format to match backend logs. Include msec 2. nginx-error logging directive DONE - set error log-level to info and add ```limit_req_log_level info;``` to enable info-level logging in error.log cant-do, not supported change timestamp format to match backend logs. Include msec -not straightforward - log the X-Session-Id header var on access logs- 8/28 - the change below was rolled back (deleted from all templates) in PAN-10508, because it broke Polaris regression health checks. See PAN-10508 for details. 2.3 DONE - Apply the standard set-header directives to ALL locations Remove the following directives from all location blocks and put them at next to the log-format directive, i.e. at the http level ######################################
Header vars to set for all request to backend apps
######################################
proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
proxy_set_header Host $host;

summary: MDF: Not properly parsing trade conditions
description_text: Background See  . Analysis shows that  we are not properly parsing trade conditions . Here is the same trade, trade ID 0x44544553573931410000 of consolidated symbol "IBM.", from this morning's MDF log and from ACTIV's log: MDF : CTS Trade Excluded 2025-08-05 09:54:51.429985866: 0x44544553573931410000: 'IBM' (IBM. [0]): 400 @ $250.6975 at QD (FINRA),  conditions [@F I] ACTIV : 2025-08-05 09:54:51.429985866: 0x44544553573931410000: 'IBM.' 400 @ $250.6975, EVENT_TYPE_TRADE  conditions ['@'] Requirement Identify why we are not properly parsing the trade conditions Fix the problem. Developer's Notes I have re-implemented the code which extracts the trade conditions from ACTIV's data structure. MDF now uses the trade condition extracting logic that we've used in Trade Engine for years. Just for tomorrow's test, I have modified MDF to log trades in this format, in addition to the usual format: 2025-08-05 16:17:03.924  INFO 91272 --- [t thread. Id: 1] c.p.p.m.p.a.UpdateProcessorSipTrades     : ACTIV_TICK 2025-08-05 16:17:03.909483952: 0x54205254374631390000: 'IBM.' 7 @ $250.8, EVENT_TYPE_TRADE_NON_REGULAR conditions [' FTI'] This will be compared with the human-readable trade data extracted from an ACTIV tick file using ActivTickReader (see  ), which produces output like this: 2025-08-05 09:59:49.245570020: 0x54206168433c30390000: 'IBM.' 2 @ $250.51, EVENT_TYPE_TRADE_NON_REGULAR conditions [' F I'] In both of these log entries, the hex number between the timestamp and the symbol is the trade ID (0x54205254374631390000). That is used to uniquely match up trades. The dates and times are from the ACTIV data, and are  not  our receive time, so any clock differences or latencies on our end should not appear in the logs. These two formats are easy to compare with each other, once we extract the top entries and strip out the prefix of "2025-08....UpdateProcessorSipTrades     : " from each line. Then, any differences indicate a bug.

summary: Compare our morning IBM trades with ACTIV
description_text:
Background This morning (August 5th, 2025), Ambrish reported that for IBM, at 2025-08-05 09:55:04, BBSS showed "Last Trade" of $250.60, and EMS showed "Last Trade" of $251.72. We saw 10,143 trades of the consolidated symbol "IBM." on the SIP this morning. They are from the CTS market data feed, so their trade conditions are evaluated according to the CTS Pillar spec  https://www.ctaplan.com/publicdocs/ctaplan/CTS_Pillar_Output_Specification.pdf  , pages 82 and 83. Using this, two of those trades qualify as "Last Trade": 2025-08-05 09:40:33.351315992: 0x50204b37383030300000: 'IBM' (IBM. [0]): 100 @ $251.62 at PA (ARCA), conditions [@4 W]
2025-08-05 09:48:14.944992284: 0x56206071373130300000: 'IBM' (IBM. [0]): 200 @ $251.715 at IX (IEX), conditions [@F W] $251.715 rounds to $251.72 in the UI. So, the UI displayed the same "Last Trade" price that the MDF back-end received: $251.72. After the 09:48:14 trade of 200 @ $251.715 at IX (IEX) (conditions [@F W]), we saw 131 trades with price of $250.59 or $250.60. (I included $250.59, because 250.599 would round up to $250.60, and I am doing a text-based search through the trades.) Each of these has trade condition 'I', Odd Lot, so was excluded from consideration as the Last Trade. So, one or more of these is happening: BBSS is not using the CTS rules for "Last Trade", and have presented one of the odd-lot $250.60 trades as "Last Trade". We did not properly receive the trade data from ACTIV. (I do not think that this is likely, but I should include it. It is certainly possible.) We plan to do these two things: Double-check with the NYSE people who know BBSS, and ask again if all odd-lot trades are excluded from display as "Last Trade". Ask ACTIV to check for trades on the SIP of IBM between 2025-08-05 09:48:14 and 2025-08-05 09:55.59 with price rounding to $250.60, without trade condition 'I'. (I do not expect them to find one, but if they do then we have a problem receiving ACTIV data.) Discuss "Last Trade" with @David Pole…
