summary: Bonds RTS: RTS submit-to-algo with lots of orders (~1000 rts) is failing with a 500 server error in ctrl-svc / ctrlg logs
description_text:
Issue found in Pano2 Bonds QA env with version 1.76.0-rc39 and BL 12.8.0:- RTS submit-to-algo with lots of orders (~1000 rts) is failing with the error: 413 Request Entity too large. Steps to re-produce:- Submit 1000 plus eligible (for ADX) RTS orders (either via Suffix, i.e. solicited RTS orders or via excel uploader, i.e. unsolicited RTS orders). Select these RTS orders in the Staged workspace, apply a strategy, for e.g. Take, modify params if any (optional) and Submit and Start. Expected Results:- The RTS orders should get submitted to ADX successfully, disappear from the Staged workspace and appear in the Adaptive workspace. Actual Results:- The submission is failing with the error '413 Request Entity too large' as seen in the attached screenshot and the orders are remaining in the Staged workspace. Notes - IMP:- we were getting 2025-08-01 13:11:09.499 ERROR 23217 — [or-http-epoll-2] a.w.r.e.AbstractErrorWebExceptionHandler : [74adb98f-1] 500 Server Error for HTTP POST "/api/controlgw/rts/route-to-adx"

org.springframework.core.io.buffer.DataBufferLimitException: Exceeded limit on max bytes to buffer : 262144
at org.springframework.core.io.buffer.LimitedDataBufferList.raiseLimitException(LimitedDataBufferList.java:98) ~[spring-core-5.3.5.jar!/:5.3.5]
Suppressed: reactor.core.publisher.FluxOnAssembly$OnAssemblyException:
Error has been observed at the following site(s): ... As the part of the fix. spring.codec.max-in-memory-size: ${CTRLG_SPRING_CODEC_MAX_IN_MEMORY_SIZE_KB:4096KB} spring.codec.max-in-memory-size: ${BLGW_SPRING_CODEC_MAX_IN_MEMORY_SIZE_KB:4096KB} spring.codec.max-in-memory-size: ${CTRL_SVC_SPRING_CODEC_MAX_IN_MEMORY_SIZE_KB:4096KB}

This quick search strategy will work for both CUSIPs and mnemonics (e.g. OTR/OLD/OOLD). See  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-10451  for details on quick search. Q: What about CUSIP validation? For example, if trader pastes a CUSIP that is not a valid OTR/OLD/OOLD -> the UI will not find the instrument in its cache and will display an appropriate error message - e.g. 'Instrument not found' (TBD) 2. summary: Bonds - Unsolicited RTS: Alerts details are not displayed for Unsolicited RTS alerts.
description_text: Following is the issue for  Unsolicited orders alerts  in Pano2 Bonds QA env for  version 1.76.0-rc39 with BL version 12.8.0 which has Unsolicited RTS enabled:- When unsolicited RTS orders are uploaded via an excel uploader, an alert is displayed for each upload. The details of these type of alerts should be displayed in the Details panel upon clicking on the alert. The issue is that currently, they are not. Please see the attached screenshots for the same. Notes:- This is working as expected in Pano2 Bonds Staging env which is on version 1.76.0-rc34. This issue is only observed for Unsolicited RTS alerts, not for regular RTS alerts, system alerts or user alerts. Also, the workaround is to see the details for any failed upload in the Review screen. This issue was most likely introduced during the implementation of the linked story ticket:  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-10141 . Even though this is reported for Bonds, need to verify if this issue exists in 1.77 for Rates (  ) . If it does, it needs to be fixed there as well.

summary: Bonds RTS: RTS submit-to-algo with lots of orders (~1000 rts) is failing with the error: 413 Request Entity too large.
description_text: Issue found in Pano2 Bonds QA env with version 1.76.0-rc39 and BL 12.8.0:- RTS submit-to-algo with lots of orders (~1000 rts) is failing with the error: 413 Request Entity too large. Steps to re-produce:- Submit 1000 plus eligible (for ADX) RTS orders (either via Suffix, i.e. solicited RTS orders or via excel uploader, i.e. unsolicited RTS orders). Select these RTS orders in the Staged workspace, apply a strategy, for e.g. Take, modify params if any (optional) and Submit and Start. Expected Results:- The RTS orders should get submitted to ADX successfully, disappear from the Staged workspace and appear in the Adaptive workspace. Actual Results:- The submission is failing with the error '413 Request Entity too large' as seen in the attached screenshot and the orders are remaining in the Staged workspace. Notes - IMP:- Found this issue while testing the linked bug ticket:  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-10339  . Even though this issue was found in Pano2 Bonds QA which is on version: 1.76.0-rc39, the issue is also replicable in Staged env which is on version 1.76.0-rc34 (which is the prod version as well). Even though this ticket is raised for Bonds, this should also be tested for rates in 1.77 branch and fixed over there as well if applicable. cc   ,   . discussed this with   and it's likely an nginx issue. With 1000 plus orders, the payload increases significantly and hence one of the params needs to be changed from 120k to 1 mb (Ayub can fill in the details). And we need to test and see if the issue is resolved with this change, if not, need to increase the size and so on. Analysis :  This is failing due to large request payload from nginx.  we have client_max_body_size 128k; Nginx default setting for this client_max_body_size is 1m. we need to take out this config for pano2-common.conf.

summary: Investigate Gradle maxParallelForks Error in Nucleus Build Pipeline
description_text: The Jenkins pipeline for  Pano2_Nucleus  fails during the  Build Nucleus  stage with the following error: Cannot set maxParallelForks to a value less than 1.     This error occurs when running the Gradle build ( ./gradlew clean compileJava ... ) as part of the pipeline. It appears to be related to how  maxParallelForks  is being set within the  apps/build.gradle  file (line ~86). Impact: The build is blocked, preventing new artifacts from being published. Affected job:  Pano2_Nucleus  (example failure:  https://pano2-jenkins.pragmafs.com/job/Pano2_Nucleus/84/console ). Next Steps: Investigate why  maxParallelForks  is being set to 0. Validate if this is related to the Jenkins build agent environment (e.g., CPU count or environment variables). Propose a fix to ensure the pipeline builds successfully. Priority: High (pipeline cannot complete).

summary: (IL-3645)  Filters in PO blotter tabs disappearing (Issue #2)
description_text: Fix IL-3645 Reproduction Steps Use the app on a  tablet  (not the mobile desktop version). Import the layout Quickly switch Workspaces back and forth between  MDM / OT / HT . At some point, the  HT  workspace will lose the filters for the  OPEN  or  CLOSED  grid tab. Resolution This fix specifically addresses the scenario described above. Other, as-yet-unknown paths may still cause grid filters to be removed. The root cause appears to be a  race condition  where  ag-grid  occasionally fails to set filters and emits a  filterStateChanged  event with an empty filter set. The issue is mitigated by checking the component’s  mounted status  before applying filters. This behavior seems related to  ag-grid issue #3457 . Logging added during earlier investigations remains in place to help detect any future occurrences. Additional Fix On  mobile , the grid column context menu was still visible even though it should not be. Because this menu allows users to clear filters, it is now  hidden on mobile  to prevent accidental filter removal.

summary: MDF: Add logging to help investigate L2 issues
description_text: Background There are reports of instances where Pano2's UI displayed L2 (NYSE depth-of-book) data showing the best ask as lower than the best bid. Requirement Gather details. We would like: Screenshot showing the problem Log of an MDF with source logging enabled for the symbol for a while before the event (ideally all day). It is hard to determine how long the logging needs to be enabled for, because this depends on the instrument's volatility. Log of an MDF with client logging enabled for a user who is displaying the L2 data at the time the screenshot was taken. Development Notes This ticket provides enhanced logging only.  There are no changes to behavior in this ticket other than logging. For L2 fixes, see PAN-10377. This ticket makes  no change  to behavior if the FeedLogger is disabled, as it is by default in production.

summary: MDF  L2 depth-of-book and top-of-books are reported in shares, BBSS uses lots
description_text: Summary UAT/PROD reported version: 1.75-rc64. Reproduced in QA version:  1.75-rc72. BBSS displays only round lot L2 data. Pano2 displays odd lots as well. Issues  per the IL description: 1st The L2 data does not match the Polaris ribbon.  (This issue was split into a separate ticket:  ) 2nd the L2 data does not match the BBSS 3rd there are instances where the ask is lower than the best bid.  (This issue was split to a separate ticket: PAN-10378) (See the IL for screenshots with BBSS). QA screenshot attached of one of the same symbols per the IL for ICE. Requirement Display top-of-books and depth-of-book size in rounds lots. See this from PAN-4960: Round Lot Formatting All symbols have a LotSize.  Shares (ActiveBid/AskShares and Bid/AskShares and, in the Header, SharesTraded) are shown in multiples of this LotSize (aka "Round Lots"). IF the shares are less than a round lot, do not display the price level IF the shares are a multiple of a round lot, then display the # of round lots IF the shares are a mixed lot, then display the # of round lots (i.e., "round down")Example: NYSE OpenBook has a bid for 149 shares of MSFT at $514.15. Polaris L2 should show a bid of "1" at $514.15.

summary: POL: Toggle CO grid to show more fields
description_text: Parent Story PAN -  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-10334 NYSEPOL -  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/NYSEPOL-841 Users have asked that we add dPrice and the Participation Rate (POV%) to the CO grid. BBSS does this by using showing two lines for each row - so they have a few extra fields. We will do this by having an expand/collapse button on the CO grid. The button is collapsed by default The button is sticky and will remain expanded (across days) unless the user explicitly collapses it When collapsed the PO/CO grids will remain as today - except that we will add the dPrice into the CO grid. CO.Strategy can be shrunk to 9 chars if necessary to fit this. CO.QtyLive must be 7 digits. LimitPrice must be sized to contain 6 digits (including a dp) - e.g., it should handle both 0.0001 and 2147.48 dPrice is the same as LimitPrice ParticipationRate must handle two digits (percentage). When expanded, the CO grid will gain dPrice and POV%. Since the PO grid is configurable, we can only say that the user will lose sufficient space in the PO grid.

summary: MDF: Always log open, reopen, halt, ssr, and other trading state changes, for all symbols
description_text:
Requirement Modify Pano2 backend component MDF to always log the open, reopen, halt, ssr, and other trading state changes, for all symbols to which it is subscribed. Log this at INFO level. This will not be adjustable through the FeedLogger APIs. Verify with PE before implementing. Development Notes We do most of this already. When there is a change to the trading state of an instrument on its primary exchange, we log it at INFO level. See  MarketDataCache.addTradingStateChange() // Don't pass this to the FeedLogger. We want to always log every trading state change.
            log.info("Added trading state change {}: {}", symbol, primaryState); PrimaryState is an enum, one of: /**
     * We do not yet know the trading state.
     */
    UNKNOWN,

    /** 
     * Either before the open, or after the close.
     * This venue is not currently executing normal trades, although pre-market or
     * post-market trading is allowed.
     */
    NON_REGULAR_TRADING,

    /** 
     * Trading has halted at this venue. It is before the open, or after the close.
     */
    NON_REGULAR_TRADING_HALTED,

    /** 
     * The venue accepts and executes regular trades.
     * This is during regular trading (after the open, before the close),
     * and we are not halted.
     */
    REGULAR_TRADING,

    /** 
     * Trading has halted at this venue, during regular trading (after the open,
     * before the close). because of this halt, this venue is not currently
     * executing trades.
     */
    REGULAR_TRADING_HALTED,

    /**
     * Trading was halted at this venue, during regular trading (after the open, before the close),
     * and was then halted. Part of the resumption of trading at this venue is a quote-only period,
     * during which passive orders are accepted but aggressive orders are not. We are now in this
     * quote-only period.
     */
    QUOTE_ONLY,

    /**
     * Trading was halted at this venue. It is before the ope…

summary: Rates RTS - submit-to-algo with lots of orders (~1000 rts) causes the automatic order-mod by Pano to stop working
description_text:
See Sree comment on 7/18 for Analysis, Production impact for current Bond RTS, and bug-fix. Steps to reproduce / symptoms / description of bug: Note that it is not guaranteed to happen, but very likely with large order batches and a few attempts Submit a large batch of RTS to the algo ~ 1000 orders All of them show up as Tasks/POs in the regular Algo workspace. But only some of them have the order-mod params applied, rest don't. Once an RTS fails to get the order-mod applied, all subsequent ones in the batch also fail on the order-mod All subsequent RTS submits (including hours later), whether single RTS or small batches or large batches - all fail on the order-mods  for every order. Restarting ctrlg fixes the issue, until it happens again. summary: MDF: Write configuration to top of log
description_text: Requirement Delay the subscriptions which occur on startup by 15 seconds, to give the app time to write its initial configuration to the log, and to do other "startup" logging, before we flood the log with data about the initial subscriptions. This makes the app easier to administer, by putting config data in an easy-to-find place (the top of the log), and the 15-second delay at 6 AM will not affect users.

summary: WS: Change fields in Time & Sales Ribbon
description_text: From MDM, remove the Symbol as the user can see the selected row above in the Portfolio grid.  Note: It'd be nice to keep this Symbol on the MDM Desktop as there is more room. On the ribbon title (to the right of the title "Time & Sales, and to the left of the expand/collapse button), we will add  Post and Panel .  These do not need a title and can be quite close together - e.g., they should look like "8 A".  Each field is one character - but we should leave room for two characters just in case. Reorder the fields as follows. Note: rename the fields to make more room as necessary (e.g., LastTradePx -> LastSale; ImbClearingPx -> ImbClgPx) LastTradePx BestBidPx     BestAskPx     Size     Shares (volume, including pre open)     VWAP  (including pre open)     OpenPx (Reopen -> Primary -> Marketwide Open/SIP)     OpenQty <next row>      PrevClosePx (new to Time&Sales ribbon)     ImbSide     ImbTotalQty     ImbPairedQty     ImbRefPx      ImbClearingPx (new to Time&Sales ribbon)      Lot (lotsize, new to Time&Sales ribbon - tagged with a highlight when it is not 100 shares) {*} * https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/NYSEPOL-822 Questions:  What would be the source of new fields (PrevClosePx, ImbClearingPx)? Please refer  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/NYSEPOL-822  for updated requirements. Notes: Post/Panel are aligned with the Title text Column Labels and Values are now center aligned. Refer to  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/NYSEPOL-822  for fields ordering and text. Bid and Ask have been merged to a single column, BidAsk. No color and arrow icon for this column based on previous values.

This PAN is to review, design, and implement these improvements To do Review the following sections of the  BLGW Streaming Behavior  Confluence page Connect/disconnect scenarios and lost messages Further Ruggedization Improve Kafka message processing code Discusss and document changes that make sense, get signoff from Mr. Robot and Pano Dev that the changes are sound Implement

summary: BOND - Ability to add a limit for an RTS within the staged workspace
description_text: Sree 11/4 - Manasi noted we want this, so prioritize this work as high but not urgent Need Currently a user does not have a way to add a Limit to an RTS in the Staged workspace. However as part of the apply strategy order ticket we show the option in Provide for - Use Limit Only. If they enable this and then submit the order to ADX, the algo rejects this param since there is no limit on the order. the user then has to go in again and modify the order to add Use Limit Only This workflow in general is inefficient and involved multiple clicks for a user. Can we allow users to add a Limit within the RTS workspace for RTS which currently dont have a limit . Some things to note Requirements The user should be able to add a limit within the grid by double checking on the limit field like they can in the ADX workspace User should NOT be able to edit an RTS which was submitted with an existing limit. Only adding limits should be allowed. If the original RTS had no limit, the user should be able to update the limit multiple times on the same RTS We need to have some icon or note that this limit being applied is only applicable for ADX The limit should NOT be visible in BL Workstation in the RTS and will not apply outside of ADX if an inquiry to ADX is rejected Implementation sketch UI (branch feature/PAN-10280-ui) Implement validations rules in Reqs above to allow entering a limit px in the grid only when permitted Implement 'enter limitPx in the Staging PO grid' - we have similar logic in the main PO grid. Check with Andrey/Yan for the PAN that does this on the regular PO grid. Try to reuse that code. When the user enters the limitPx, send a OrderMod request to ctrl-svc, similar to what's done for the regular PO edit-limit-px-in-grid. BUT, with the following differences: Include the InquiryId, and any other important fields on the order-mod request (Check with Ayub to determine what's required for blgw) include a flag that indicates this is an RTS order-mod. done Backend    …

summary: Auth0 - add support for Auth0 authentication in Pano
description_text: Requirements - required only for Bonds and Rates. 1. Support Auth0 tokens in Pano 2. Auth0 should support multiple issuer/audience, similar to Cognito 3. Pano for Bonds/Rates should support a mix of Cognito & Auth0 users 4. Should handle both Id tokens and Access tokens Assumptions: 1. The Auth0 token must specify Issuer & Audience 2. Auth0 token must specify (in some claim) the  same userName value  as the corresponding Cognito token. For Cognito tokens, username is specified in cognito:username (id token) or username (access token) claim. 3. Auth0 token must specify (in some claim) the  same ClientId value  as the corresponding Cognito token. For Cognito tokens, ClientId is specified in the  audience  (id token) or  client_id  (access token) claim. Design / implementation sketch 1. Implement Auth0AuthConfig - similar to CognitoAuthConfig 2. Check references to CognitoAuthConfig, do similar for Auth0 3. Auth0 should support multiple pools similar to Cognito 4. Pano should support a mix of Auth0 and Cognito users. 5. Supporting multiple issuer/audience - see CognitoJwtDecoder.decode() for how 6. Supporting both Id tokens and Access tokens - see CognitoJwtDecoder.convert() for how Supported combinations for Pano auth 1. EQ/FX - Okta 2. Bonds - Okta + Cognito & Auth0 3. Pol - Okta & CloudFlare 4. Regression envs - Okta & Fake 5. Not supported - Fake + CloudFlare / Cognito / Auth0 / To do 1. Get a sample Auth0 and matching Cognito token, attach both to this PAN 2. Confirm that issuer, audience, client and username are present on the Auth0 token, and that client and userName match the Pano values - as these will be used to resolve to a user in Pano 3. Code up and test 4. Update C&D on this PAN 5. Update pano2cfg for config for QA envs. Q&A to do

summary: Externalize Pano2 holiday calendar
description_text: Context : Holiday calendar is hard-coded in CalendarService class. Once a year (at least) code change to this class is required to update the calendar. The class logs a warning at half-year mark that it's time to update the calendar: WARNING: 2025-06-27 06:00:05.158 WARN 31469 --- [ main] c.p.pano2.calendar.CalendarService : CalendarService is approaching its last supported date of 2025-12-25; alert developers Work : Externalize the calendar by making it a configuration setting. PE should be able to update the calendar without requiring any code change, just like they might update a config/env setting for another Pano component. Provide support for multiple calendars by country-market - e.g. US-EQ (US Equity), US-FI (Fixed Income), UK-EQ, UK-FI, etc. Calendar should support full/half holidays, normal/half-holiday close times.

summary: MDF: Update javadoc to clarify that we check trade conditions for NYSE and non-NYSE trades
description_text: Background MDF currently checks for NYSE trade condition characters when evaluating trades. We need to also check non-NYSE trade conditions, for trades occurring in other venues. Requirement When checking for trade conditions (extended hours, odd lots, conditional trades), use NYSE trade condition characters and non-NYSE trade condition characters. These are documented on page 44 of  https://www.utpplan.com/DOC/UtpBinaryOutputSpec.pdf Development Notes The trade conditions for NYSE trades (  https://www.ctaplan.com/publicdocs/ctaplan/CTS_Pillar_Output_Specification.pdf  , the "Sale Condition" field description, page 82, as of Version 2.11a, June 6, 2026) and non-NYSE trades (  https://www.utpplan.com/DOC/UtpBinaryOutputSpec.pdf  , section 3.14.2, "UTP Sale Condition Matrix", as of version 1.9a, June 2025 ) are both covered by the ACTIV spec (  https://support.activfinancial.com/feed-info/feeds/detail?id=396  ). There is no logic change required for this ticket.   The changes in the PR are only documentation changes and a rename of  NyseTradeCondition.java  to  TradeCondition.java . These changes clarify to readers that the trade conditions being checked apply for all venues as provided to us by ACTIV, and are not NYSE-specific.

summary: MDF Cleanup: Remove Subscription Manager Subscribe/Unsubscribe APIs which do not take user ID
description_text: Background PAN-10242 overloaded  SubscriptionManager.subscribe()  and  SubscriptionManager.unsubscribe()  to take the user ID, for logging. All non-test invocations were modified to call the new methods. To minimize the scope of the change, tests were left as-is. Requirement Remove the   SubscriptionManager.subscribe()  and  SubscriptionManager.unsubscribe()  which do not take the user ID. Update tests as necessary. Other than a little bit of logging, there should be no changes to the behavior of MDF as a result of this PR. This is just cleanup to make the code cleaner and better documented.

summary: Early conversion of symbol from Pragma symbology to ACTIV symbology
description_text:
Background When a market data snapshot is requested from MDF, the symbols are converted from Pragma symbology (where a space is used as a suffix delimiter) to ACTIV symbology (where a slash is used). While this has no effect on most symbols, this converts 'BRK B' to 'BRK/B'. A lookup of the symbol in Secmaster then fails, because 'BRK/B' is not in SecMaseter. This lookup is unnecessary, because if ACTIV is configured as the market data provider, the ACTIV data provider already translates the symbol. If ACTIV is not used as the provider (such as for FX deployments), we should not be converting to ACTIV symbology. We do not see this bug in FX deployments because FX symbols do not have spaces in them. This issue only occurs for snapshot requests for symbols with spaces. Example log excerpts: 2025-06-18 15:01:15.846  INFO 54338 --- [tor-http-nio-44] c.p.pano2.mdf.web.MarketDataService      : Requesting Best Bid / Best Offer snapshot for user fdemarco@baycrestpartners.com, ins
trument(s) [A, ABBV, ...(many symbols omitted)..., BEN, BF B, BG, BK, BLDR, BLK, BMY, BR, BRK B, BRO, ...(many symbols omitted)...] Symbol conversion of "BF B" to "BF/B" and "BRK B" to "BRK/B" occurs. 2025-06-18 15:01:15.848  INFO 54338 --- [tor-http-nio-44] c.p.p.m.s.SubscriptionManager            : Added first client Best Bid / Best Offer subscription to [A, ABBV, ..., BEN, BF/B, BG, BK, BLDR, BLK, BMY, BR, BRK/B, BRO, ... ]. Then, SecMaster lookup fails: 2025-06-18 15:01:15.848  WARN 54338 --- [tor-http-nio-44] c.p.p.m.p.activ.UpdateProcessorSecurity  : Secmaster data not available for BF/B
2025-06-18 15:01:15.848  WARN 54338 --- [tor-http-nio-44] c.p.p.m.p.activ.UpdateProcessorSecurity  : Secmaster data not available for BRK/B Requirement Remove the conversion of symbols from Pragma to ACTIV format from the MarketDataSnapshotter. We should  never  convert a symbol in MDF, String symbols should always be in Pragma symbology. For ACTIV, only in the ACTIV data provider code, we create  SymbolId …

2025-06-18 16:03:57.215  INFO 54338 --- [criptionManager] c.p.p.m.p.activ.ActivUnsubscriber        : Unsubscribed from symbol: RDDT (ACTIV RDDT.NBI [0]) for request type IMBALANCE
2025-06-18 16:03:57.215  WARN 54338 --- [criptionManager] c.p.p.m.p.activ.ActivUnsubscriber        : Attempt to unsubscribe from L1_QUOTE RDDT. [0], but there is no open subscription.
2025-06-18 16:03:57.215  WARN 54338 --- [criptionManager] c.p.p.m.p.activ.ActivUnsubscriber        : Attempt to unsubscribe from IMBALANCE RDDT.NBI [0], but there is no open subscription Requirement Identify the cause of the warning, and address it. Developers Notes When the ACTIV subscription for L1 data of an instrument is ended, the current code is attempting to remove it twice. This does not occur for other instrument types. description_text: Bug blgw app.yml specifies a value for kafka.producer-props.[transactional.id] This value needs to be different for Credit vs Rates, or else there is a collision in Kafka between Credit & Rates messages. The value is currently hardcoded and so the same for Credit and Rates, causing Kafka Transaction errors. To do Parameterize this value in app.yml, no defaults, must be specified in env-settings. [transactional.id]': "${BLGW_TRANSACTION_ID}" Default to the following value in any props / env-settings for credit: "producer-credit" for rates: "producer-rates" Merge to release/1.77 Repeat the code change in branch PAN-???? that uses the mktx-12.8.0 jar. create an RC or snapshot jar for use in UAT, QA, ULT provide the jar to PE Q&A See Q&A for

summary: Bonds blgw - must be able specify different Credit vs Rates values for param transactional.id
description_text: Bug blgw app.yml specifies a value for kafka.producer-props.[transactional.id] This value needs to be different for Credit vs Rates, or else there is a collision in Kafka between Credit & Rates messages. The value is currently hardcoded and so the same for Credit and Rates, causing Kafka Transaction errors. To do Parameterize this value in app.yml, no defaults, must be specified in env-settings. [transactional.id]': "${BLGW_TRANSACTION_ID}" Default to the following value in any props / env-settings for credit: "producer-credit" for rates: "producer-rates" Merge to release/1.76 Repeat the code change in branch PAN-10164 that uses the mktx-12.8.0 jar. create an RC or snapshot jar for use in UAT, QA, ULT provide the jar to PE Q&A Can we go live in Prod for RTS & Unsolicited without this fix A: Yes, but we need this in place before Rates RTS is in Prod Huh, why ? A: There's a kafka transaction-id conflict. Prior to this fix the value for the transaction-id is hardcoded, and the same for Credit and Rates. That is bad. We dont have RTS Rates in prod yet, so there's no conflict with the current hardcoded value. What are our options if we dont want to upgrade Prod now  A: These 1. Fix the final value for this variable to be the same as the current value for Credit only (Rates will specify a different value). That way when we upgrade later, the value IS configurable, but its the same as the earlier hardcoded value, so effectively no change. Not pretty, but effective. 2. Specify the new value (producer-credit) in a bespoke application.yml file in prod. This file can be removed when we upgrade What happens in UAT - that has RC34 that already has the old value for this setting. What happens if we change it. A: Changing the value as an EOD TU should be fine as we'll start with messages. We may see some errors, but they should be fine. How sure are you, will you test it in QA first A: Not 100pct sure, and yes we'll test in QA first. Did you test in QA and confirm that all is well…

summary: Migrate Protractor test cases to Playwright
MDF has different Trade Condition filters than BBSS, so provides incorrect 'Last Trade' and 'Open'
description_text: Background "Last Trade" data is provided to the Pano2 UI by MDF. MDF filters trades by trade condition, excluding odd lot trades (condition 'I'), extended hours trades (condition 'T'), and contingent trades (condition 'V'). If BBSS uses different criteria to exclude trades from its 'last trade; display, its 'last trade' data may not match Pano2's. Requirement Determine the rules used internally by BBSS to select trades for display as 'Last Trade'. Apply them to MDF. QA Notes Summary We use different trade condition rules when checking for "Last Trade" and "Opening Trade at Primary Market" for the CTS and UTP Market Data Feeds. Data for each symbol comes from either the CTS or UTP feeds, not both. NYSE symbols are on the CTS feed, Nasdaq on the UTP feed. For symbols listed at other exchanges, check the ACTIV details on the feeds,  https://support.activfinancial.com/feed-info/feeds/detail?id=88  . As of this writing (July 24, 2025) the following exchanges publish their data on the UTP feed: 24X_NATIONAL_EXCHANGE (24) CBOE (W) CBOE_BYX (BY) CBOE_BZX (BT) CBOE_EDGA (DA) CBOE_EDGX (DX) FINRA_ADF (QD) IEX (IX) LTSE (LT) MEMX (MB) MIAX_PEARL_EQUITIES (EP) NASDAQ (Q) NASDAQ_BX (B) NASDAQ_ISE (Y) NASDAQ_PSX (X) NYSE (N) NYSE_AMERICAN (A) NYSE_ARCA (PA) NYSE_NATIONAL (C) NYSE_TEXAS (MW) So, we will need to test: "Last Price" for the CTS feed "Last Price" for the UTP feed "Opening Price at Primary Market" for the CTS feed "Opening Price at Primary Market" for the UTP feed "Reopening Price at Primary Market" for the CTS feed "Reopening Price at Primary Market" for the UTP feed Other market data follows the same rules for the CTS and UTP feed. Our L2 data is NYSE-specific, so it all comes over the UTP feed. Details This PR implements trade condition evaluation specific to the CTS and UTP market data feeds. This provides filtering to determine which trades should count as the "last trade", the "opening trade at primary market", and the "re-opening trade at primary market", with d…

summary: ADX - Display indicative levels for ADX strategies - UI
description_text: Implement this feature:  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-10199 Details of new stats for Pano are here:  https://confluence.pragmafs.com/display/RES/Bond+Indicative+Limit+Stats Related info - may be useful but the feature spec should be considered the main source of requirements Quant spec - see attached email from 11 Jul 2025. Quant ticket related to this: QU-3092. It provides good background on the feature, so make sure to review it. Original spec Pragma link to spec Mktx / VDI link to spec Important Quant note on IOI_RESPONSE_LIMITS stat: This stat will initially be a single price - e.g "1.2345". Later, it will be a list: (price/qty,price/qty, ...), and we will need to pick one price depending on the order quantity. We will  NOT support this now . However to prevent breaking Pano when TE starts publishing the new format, implement this now: Option A - Client - treat the column as a string, extract the first number delimited by an  optional  "/". Option B - POG - treat the column as a string, extract the first number delimited by an  optional  "/". override the type of this stat and make it a Double when publishing the schema To do UI - implement the feature PAN. Broadly: logic to derive the value of synthetic columns formatting for columns formatting for tooltips make the new columns required at startup Q&A We have 11 new stats - do we expose all of them as new columns in the UI or only some of them A:  Only some. However the ones we do not display will be used to populate new synthetic columns that are displayed. The feature PAN details this clearly. What is the exact set of synthetic columns need to be derived in Pano. What are the rules to derive them, and are they all exposed as PO columns. A:  See the feature PAN. Is there a change to the default layout with these new cols. What is the plan for existing users A:   Yes, I will give you this soon. ((ROSIE TO DO))

summary: Unsolicited RTS: Potential Caching issue with JX Browser in BL while upgrading to a new Pano2 RC or version, due to which the version does not get updated in JX browser immediately upon upgrading.
description_text:
Steps to replicate:- In QA env, when a new RC or Pano version is available, update Pano2 to it and re-start the env. For JX browser, execute the below additional unix commands to copy over files to MKTX facing nginx in order to complete the update:- scp -rp /home/pragma/pano2-bond-qa/client nginxuat002.ny5:/home/pragmaqa/pano2-bond-qa/ scp -rp /home/pragma/pano2-bond-qa/client-pad nginxuat002.ny5:/home/pragmaqa/pano2-bond-qa/ Re-open BL app / JX browser if already open. Doing so should update the RC version to the intended one. However, it is observed that the version in the user menu in Pano2 opened via the JX browser remains unchanged. Notes:- This issue is observed for BL 12.7.1 while upgrading to Pano 1.76.0-rc34 in QA. We tried an nginx re-load when this issue occurred, it did not resolve the issue. The next morning, it got resolved by itself, not sure what was done overnight to resolve it, maybe a BL re-start? Confirmed that nginx was not re-started. Fix:- Add a no cache policy on these 2 files: /ssl/index.html /ssl/loader.js Testing if it worked. Generate and deploy the nginx.conf, do a nginx reload/restart curl -I  https://yourdomain.com/ssl/index.html Expected output (the  -I  flag tells  curl  to only fetch headers) HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-eval'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';
Cache-Control: no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate, proxy-revalidate
Pragma: no-cache
Expires: 0

summary: Route to RatesAlgo from RTS with Strategy Vwap and its basic params are getting ClassCastException
description_text:
QA Findings Submit RTS order to be displayed in RTS Staged window Right click and apply strategy. Select Strategy as VWAP and enter the basic param details and click on Submit button Expected: RTS should be routed to Rates Algo with above order params being persisted Actual: NPE is thrown in Ctrl-Svc logs Development Notes ( ) Apparently the UI passes an integer value for these parameters if the user types in a numeric value without a decimal point, so "7" is being serialized as an Integer but if the user types "7.0" it is being JSON serialized as a double. Control Gateway should perform the conversion, and should accept (for example) "79", 79, 79., or 79.0 as valid values for LIMIT price. The UI should not be required to understand. Requirement Control Gateway should receive the JSON string (below is an example, with whitespace added): body={
"requests":[
    {
        "modifyAndStart":false,
        "orderId":"18340048",
        "params": {
            "2038":"VWAP, TWAP, POHV, Taker, Provide",
            "3015":"ratesvwap",
            "3026":1749828900000,
            "3027":1749839400000,
            "3066":79,
            "3093":7,
            "3094":7,
            "3497":1000000,
            "3841":"false",
            "5000":"UNSPEC",
            "5006":"Sell",
            "5077":"US91282CNC19",
            "6003":"RATES",
            "rowId":"18340048"}}]} Control gateway should parse the params as a  Map<Integer, Object> , convert to  Map<Integer,String>  by calling toString on each value, and use  TaskParams  from TE to properly parse the various task param values from Strings. If a parameter is invalid or cannot be cast (such as a LIMIT_PRICE of "whatever"), the exception thrown should clearly state which task parameter (identified by name and number) cannot be cast, specify the type to which the cast was attempted, and specify the invalid value.

summary: BOND Take and Provide Indicative Pricing
description_text: As an Adaptive user, I would like to view my parent order's potential (indicative) Take or Auto-R response level before starting the parent order for trading. I would also like to see my live prices in all venues. Where to Display Workspaces The take limit can be calculated once the order is in TradeEngine; therefore, indicative prices can only be shown in the Adaptive workspace and not in Staged. What and How to Display Used the same series as AlgoSummary since these are for display only: RfqExecutionReference 20002 RfqRespondingReference 20003 LMRespondingReference 20024 Take Tactic: Create a new synthetic column  RfqExecutionReference  (6xxx series) as follows: Description Condition RfqExecutionReference Rendering Take tactic is live TAKE_STATUS (5360) non-null AND CHILD_ORDER_SUMMARY row exists for  venue-name.rfq TAKE_LIMIT (5361) normal Take tactic planned in future CHILD_ORDER_SUMMARY does NOT have a take row AND NEXT_RFQ_TIME (5376) non-null TAKE_LIMIT (5361) Periwinkle / dotted outline Take tactic in past CHILD_ORDER_SUMMARY does NOT have a take row AND NEXT_RFQ_TIME (5376) is null AND LAST_TAKE_LIMIT (5388) not null OR Status = Expired\ Cancelled\ Done (optional, up to dev implementation choice) LAST_TAKE_LIMIT (5388) grey Create a new dislpayable column  RfqReferenceType  (6xxx series) as follows Alternately reuse the TAKE_CONSTRAINT column, but remap it's values for display as follows TAKE_CONSTRAINT Value Rendering Client Client Limit Variation CP+ Offset Model Take Model Limit Note: We do not need a separate column for the raw values. Auto-R:   Create a new synthetic column  RfqRespondingReference  (6xxx series) as follows: Description Condition RfqRespondingReference Rendering Auto-R child order is live CHILD_ORDER_SUMMARY - row exists for  venue-name.respond  AND IOI_RESPONSE_LIMITS is not null IOI_RESPONSE_LIMITS normal Auto-R order planned in future CHILD_ORDER_SUMMARY - row does not exists for  venue-name.respond  AND IOI_RESPONSE_LIMITS is not nu…

summary: RTS Window --> PO Grid --> ShortName should be displayed as OTR-10Y etc.
description_text: Send RTS order and verify shortname in the RTS PO grid. Expected: Short Name should be the same as OTR/OLD/OOLD-Tenor derived from the sec master Actual:  Short name is like UST 4.125 5/32 Context : US Treasury bonds are auctioned every month. When this happens, the new bonds are called 'on the run'; bonds that previously used to be 'on the run' become 'off the run'. It's a market convention used by traders. On the UI, instead of displaying the bond's TCM (<ticker> <coupon> <maturity>, i.e. short name) - e.g. UST 4.125 5/32 - we should display:  <instrumentState>-<tenor>. <tenor> for a bond with 10 year maturity will be  10Y , the tenor for a bond with 15 year maturity will be  15Y , and so on. The <instrumentState> will be  OTR  for 'on the run' bonds and <OLD> for 'off-the-run' bonds. <instrumentState> for on-the-run bonds will be  OTR . And for off-the-run-bonds it will be  OLD . For example, the short name for the on-the-run 10Y UST will be  OTR-10Y . And, the short name for the off-the-run 10Y will be  OLD-10Y . Note: old-off-the-run bonds (bonds that used to be off-the-run but have been bumped back 1 more generation) are not allowed (yet) on the platform. When they are allowed, their <instrumentState> will be <OLD1> in the security master. Since business wants <OOLD> instead of <OLD1>, we will have to either change it in the security master or transform it on the fly on the POG or UI. Dev Work : BLGW - from uigateways/POG repo copy Rates security master loader classes, tests, and sample data into pano2/BLGW In pano2/BLGW - use the ported sec master to enrich Rates orders before publishing to Rx Long term - once the uigateways repo is merged into pano2 repo (TBD), duplicate code can be removed from uigateways

summary: blgw - devise a plan to allow a release version of blgw to target different BL versions
description_text:
Bacgkground/context See PAN-10164 and   for context/details Requirement Devise a plan to support the following scenario - the ability for a blgw release version (e.g. blgw-1.76) that can work with 2 adjacent versions of BondLink (e.g. 12.7.1, 12.8.0). Some thoughts Ensure the later version of mktx-jar is backward compatible, e.g. mktx-jar 12.8.0 works with BL 12.7.1. Then blgw can just use the later (12.8.0) version of the mktx-jar Possible, a burden since no breaking changes are permitted. blgw binds to both version of mktx-jar, and we specify the runtime version of mktx-jar to use via config (env-settings). Ideal but challenging ? Let's assume that the mktx interfaces used by BLGW have not changed in a breaking manner- e.g. if BLGW is using a class called Side, then the new JAR still has that class (otherwise, a ClassNotFound exception will occur at runtime). In other words, the JARs are  binary compatible . Further, let's assume that mktx-data dependencies have not changed - e.g. new mktx-data.jar does not pull in dependencies that were not in previous version. By the way, if these assumptions do not hold then mktx-data cannot be updated without re-compiling BLGW. If these assumptions hold, then a way to make BLGW work with multiple mktx-data JARs would be to have the BLGW startup script 'link in' the correct mktx-data jar as follows: BLGW startup script creates a symlink as shown below based on config - e.g.  current version  is 11.5.0. Important: stored-versions folder should not be on the classpath, otherwise a random mktx-data JAR might get loaded. BLGW is compiled using the current version. Create a switch-version.sh script to do the following: stop BLGW, remove all mktx-data JARs from lib folder, create symlink as shown below to point to the  target version  of mktx-data (e.g. 12.8.0), verify there is exactly 1 mktx-data JAR in lib folder, restart BLGW. deployment/
├── blgw.jar       
├── lib/
│   ├── stored-versions/         # Archive of all versions
│…

That work is already done in a separate PAN-10164 and related feature branch, however for 1.76, the main branch is BL 12.7 compatible, and the PAN-10164 branch is BL v12.8 compatible Note for QA & PE : This PAN makes blgw on the 1.77 release branch compatible with BL v12.8.0, since that's where most of the testing is happening When we are ready to release to Prod against BL 12.7.2, there should be another PAN to make blgw on the 1.77 release branch compatible with  BL 12.7.x. When Rates Prod BL is ready to upgrade to 12.8.0, there should be ye another PAN to change blgw on the 1.77 release branch again, to make it compatible with  BL 12.8.0.

description_text: Context See  https://marketaxess.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Pragma/pages/4268949831/BOND+entitlement Requirement In blgw, populate the traderId on the FirmTrader column 3648 for all PO and Alerts updates. To do Check wherever blgw is updating the Client (3644), or ClientTrader (3646), or Firm (3647) columns on RTS POs or Alerts that get published to Pano. For each of these, make sure to set the FIRM_TRADER (3648, set to TraderId), and if not already set, the FIRM (3647, from config) as well. Make this change in 1.76, and cherrypick to 1.77 Testing Configure CSFR with the groovy rules to set the FIRM_TRADER col. See TU-36622 for the specific change that is  already done in Prod for Mktx CSFR . The change is in csfr's translators.groovy. See  here - config/transation/translators.groovy   for the Prod PR that made this change for TU-36622 Set Entitlements Set VIEW_FIRM_TRADER Entitlements. See Note #1 below Remove VIEW_ CLIENT_TRADER.  See Note #2 below for context. Remove VIEW_DOMAIN, VIEW_ALL entitlements Test the following show up as expected Staging workspace related Regular RTS Staging workspace - RTS generated from Unsolicited orders Alerts for RTS Alerts for Unsolicited Regular ADX Workspace related (POs and Alerts from TE) Parent Orders Regular Alerts Notes With this PAN, the VIEW_FIRM_TRADER entitlement should be sufficient in order to see all RTS / Unsolicited related messages - RTS-POs and RTS-Alerts, as well as Algo related Orders and Alerts. Earlier we needed the VIEW_CLIENT_TRADER entitlement to see Algo orders, because these orders populate the CLIENT_TRADER col, but do NOT populate the FIRM_TRADER col. Now that we have added the Groovy rules to CSFR to populate the FIRM_TRADER, we can remove the VIEW_CLIENT_TRADER, and use just VIEW_FIRM_TRADER for both RTS related and Algo related entitlements. The CSFR groovy rules  are already in Prod - see TU-36622 See this Confluence page for the additional details:  https://marketaxess.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Pra…

summary: Where unsolicited order upload failed, send WARN on alertLevel
description_text: Where unsolicited order upload failed, send WARN on alertLevel so that the user sees a more urgent level of alert than INFO. On failed order upload attempts where # failed >0, set the AlertLevel = WARN. Therefore is one or more of the order upload attempts do not pass, then the entire alert has an alertLevel of WARN. Example of msg coming from IS where the order could not become an RTS: "Headers": null, "Message": { "id": "999e1d2e-647a-44e3-affa-508e6f19fd3f", "bondlinkInquiryId": null, "groupId": "c90e3852-d1bb-4450-abea-50ad43e8c901", "resultSummary": "Order could not be submitted.", "timestamp": 1749144449710, "companyId": 10035, "traderId": 50448, "traderShortName": "bragill", "companyShortName": "Blackrock", "bondShortName": "AAPL 2.200 9/11/29 c29", "side": "Sell", "orderQuantity": 2000000, "limitLevel": 21, "errorMessage": "Settlement date is in the past", "adx": null

summary: MDF: Subscription reference counting bug
description_text:
Background See IL-3571. On June 4th, production problems were reported with BRK B imbalance data received from production MarketDataFeedApplication v1.75.0-rc59 (Java 17.0.4.1) on ws-prod003.ny5.pragmafs.com. Floor traders reported that they stopped receiving updates for this instrument at about 2:01, and it did not resume until 2:35. This MDF is failing to properly track its subscriptions. When the first user requests imbalance data for BRK B, MDF sends a subscription request for BRK B to ACTIV, and logs: 2025-06-04 14:35:03.133  INFO 34269 --- [tor-http-nio-62] c.p.p.m.p.a.UpdateProcessorNyseImbalance : Subscribed to imbalance of BRK/B.NBI (table 0) As additional users add subscriptions or drop them for BRK B, MDF tracks the count of users who want BRK B data. When that count reaches 0, one minute later we unsubscribe, and log a notice like this: It logs:
2025-06-04 14:36:08.538  INFO 34269 --- [criptionManager] c.p.p.m.p.activ.ActivUnsubscriber        : Unsubscribed from symbol: BRK/B (ACTIV BRK/B.NBI [0]) for request type IMBALANCE The one minute delay is to avoid dropping subscriptions for transient issues, like when a user drops their subscription for (F, BRK B) to add a subscription for (BRK B). In that case, we don't want to lose any data which might have arrived in the meantime. So, if no user wants data from BRK B, we start a 1-minute timer, and at the elapse of 1 minute if no user still wants that BRK B data we unsubscribe from ACTIV. Apparently, there was at least 1 user connected who wanted BRK B between 2 PM and 2:30 PM. This is the user or users who reported the bug. So, we should have never unsubscribed from BRK/B imbalance during that time. However, we saw the following in the production log from the event: 2025-06-04 14:01:20.994  INFO 34269 --- [criptionManager] c.p.p.m.p.activ.ActivUnsubscriber        : Unsubscribed from symbol: BRK B (ACTIV BRK/B.NBI [0]) for request type IMBALANCE
....
2025-06-04 14:35:03.133  INFO 34269 --- [tor-http-nio-62…

summary: AMS - allow users to see END_USER_ALERTS, if entitled
description_text: As part of ADX-724 we are introducing a new category of alerts called End user Alerts. These alerts will be visible to the end users . We want this ability to be controlled through an entitlement in PAD called ENABLE_END_USER_ALERTS. Required Refactoring for RTS Routing RTS alerts currently use the END_USER alert category, which causes conflicts with existing features. Additionally, alerts for unsolicited orders are categorized under UNS_RTS. Both alert types are currently visible to all users who have the DISABLE_SYSTEM_ALERTS and ENABLE_RTS_ROUTING entitlements. What need to be done Introduce a New Alert Category named WORKFLOW. All alerts under this category will be sent to  all users , regardless of whether they have the DISABLE_SYSTEM_ALERTS entitlement. Add a New Alert Field SubCategory( 6024 ). This is needed to distinguish between different types of WORKFLOW alerts, such as RTS and unsolicited order alerts. Modify BLGW alerts to use: AlertCategory = WORKFLOW AlertSubCategory = RTS or UNS_RTS (depending on the type of alert Pano TODO pad-svc - add a new entitlement ENABLE_END_USER_ALERTS, no params rx-svc publish the new category of alerts (tbd from TradePlant : eg END_USER) only if the user has then entitlement enabled rx-svc to filter non END_USER/WORKFLOW alerts for users with DISABLE_SYSTEM_ALERTS unwind the code where users where seeing END_USER when DISABLE_SYSTEM_ALERTS and ENABLE_RTS_ROUTING where set.

summary: Replace ACTIV field FID_LOT_SIZE in MDF with its replacement FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE
description_text: Background See  https://support.activfinancial.com/news-story/view?id=3578 ACTIV is removing field FID_LOT_SIZE in September, 2025. Requirement Remove all reference to FID_LOT_SIZE. Replace them with FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE (1604). Per  https://support.activfinancial.com/news-story/view?id=3578  , "FID_QUOTE_LOT_SIZE will represent the number to multiply bid and ask sizes by to calculate the number of shares. If an instrument is quoted in shares, then this field will be set to 1. If an instrument quotes in lots, then this field will contain the same value as FID_TRANSACTION_LOT_SIZE." The following applications are affected: MDF, MDSS QA follow up dev notes (6/27/25): From   :   WS reads the lot sizes from Secmaster.  If the lot size is not available in secmaster , then we read lot size from ACTIV. But, lot size should be in secmaster for all of our securities. We log the quote and trade sizes, which is (number of lots) * (size of lot, as obtained from Secmaster if it is in secmaster and secmaster is available, or from ACTIV if this deployment is not using secmaster).

summary: AMS EQ / FX / Bonds / Rates: Issue with pausing orders for a user that has only VIEW_DOMAIN entitlement and no other VIEW_ entitlements.
description_text: Issue as observed in  EQ QA  for version  1.75.0-rc59  and  Bonds QA  env for  version 1.76.0-rc28:  Pausing orders for a user that has only VIEW_DOMAIN entitlement and no other VIEW_ entitlements is displaying an error and logging the user out. Steps to re-produce the issue:- Login with a user that has only VIEW_DOMAIN entitlement and no other VIEW_ entitlements. User can have all other standard entitlements other than the view entitlements. Pause an active order. Expected Behavior:- The order should get paused. Actual Behavior:- An error is displayed in a red banner on top as seen in the attachments. And the user gets logged out of the application. Notes:- This issue is observed in EQ / FX in 1.75. And in 1.76 for Bonds. Hence, it's most likely there for Rates as well and needs to be fixed for all the AMS asset classes for 1.75 and the fix needs to be merged upto 1.76 for Bonds. This issue is only occurring with VIEW_DOMAIN entitlement, and it doesn't matter if the entitlement is in lower or upper case. VIEW_ALL, VIEW_INSTANCE, VIEW_CLIENT_TRADER etc. work without any issue, able to pause and start orders with these entitlements. It's only VIEW_DOMAIN which causes the issue. The workaround is to change the value of PANO_DOMAIN in server-config.xml in the TE config folder from lower to upper case and re-start the TE. For e.g. if the PANO_DOMAIN value was mktx, changing it to MKTX worked. Analysis A few related bugs, different apps and code paths for each bug, but both bugs the same root cause - the domain stamped on the PO (and Route) is the value extracted from the TE INSTANCE_NAME, and not the configured PANO_DOMAIN. Related to this PAN i.e. pausing and any other actions on orders don't work. This domain associated with the order is the domain-extracted-from-instance. If this does not match the user's VIEW_DOMAIN entitlement (likely set to PANO_DOMAIN), boom!. A workaround is to set the VIEW_DOMAIN entitlement to the same value that is extracted from the te-insta…

summary: Bonds: Sharing of User Alerts is not working when shared using the icon on top.
description_text: Issue as observed in Bonds QA env for  version 1.76.0-rc26:  Sharing of User Alerts is not working when shared using the icon on top. Steps to re-produce the issue:- Open the User Alerts panel. Check if user alert rules exist, if not, create at-least a couple. Click on the Share icon on top (next to the Search Rules input). Enter the email for the user to share with, select the rules to be shared and click on Confirm. Expected Behavior:- Selecting / dragging rules to select them to share should work and Confirm button should be enabled. Clicking on Confirm should close the dialog and share the selected rules with the provided user. Upon logging in with that user and opening the User Alerts panel, the shared rules should be visible. This is described in the feature ticket  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-2920   which has been implemented as mentioned in the corresponding story ticket:  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-9969 . Actual Behavior:- Selecting / dragging rules to select them to share is not working and as a result, clicking the Confirm button is doing nothing and the dialog remains open. As a result, sharing User Alerts using the Share icon on top is not working. Notes:- Selecting individual rules in the User Alerts panel and clicking the row level icon and sharing using the dialog that opens is working. It's sharing using the top icon that's not working.

summary: Rates RTS - pog - include RTS specific columns in the PO-schema response
description_text:
Background POG serves up the PO schema as a combination of  TradeEngine TaskParams & TaskStats. It gets these using TEAPI. RTS POs however have additional columns that are not task-params/stats. We need to include these cols in the PO schema served up by POG To do: Get the list of additional columns from here ShortName(2000, "ShortName", ColumnType.STRING),
    UnderlyingTicker(2001, "UnderlyingTicker", ColumnType.STRING),
    Coupon(2002, "Coupon", ColumnType.DOUBLE),
    Maturity(2003, "Maturity", ColumnType.STRING),
    IssueDate(2004, "IssueDate", ColumnType.STRING),
    AmountOutstanding(2005, "AmountOutstanding", ColumnType.DOUBLE),
    Ccy(2006, "Ccy", ColumnType.STRING),
    Product(2007, "Product", ColumnType.STRING),
    LiquidityScore(2008, "LiquidityScore", ColumnType.INTEGER),

summary: Rates RTS - update mog to support RTS Routing
description_text: Requirement Pano automation / regression testing will NOT add blgw or ctrlg to the app-stack under-test. In order to support automation testing of RTS functionality, AND developer e2e flow using mog, we need mog to mock blgw and ctrlg functionality for RTS Routing To do - mog generate RTS in sim-code, publish as RTS PO. Would be nice to have blgw spit out RTS PO json to a file that we can then use as samples for generated RTS. Alternately hand-craft. accept submit-rts with order-params, publish RTS-delete, publish vanilla PO, amended PO with order-mod params. Implement ctrlg functionality. Keep it simple, dont need to do the full ctlg workflow where it caches the order-mod params, waits for the PO and then submits the order-mod. Instead in mog just do this on accepting the s: publish RTS PO on a different PO stream than regular PO - believe we already did this for some other feature in Pol?, so should be able to reuse/copy the approach Implementation MOG API for RTS Testing MOG_SIM_MODE mode needs to set INTERACTIVE. Publish a new RTS from MOG Request:  POST: localhost:8083/api/blgw/sim/rts/new Response: success Action: This request will publish a RTS order to UI. Invoke a route to adx from browser Use client browser to invoke route to adx vi apply strategy Send RTS update from MOG Request:  POST localhost:8083/api/blgw/sim/rts/updates Request body:  for: success submit {     "2018":"44558",     "2030":"DNE",     "6019":"INFO" } for Terminal error {     "2018":"31971",     "2030":"DNT",     "6019":"ERROR" } for Transient error {     "2018":"31971",     "2030":"RTS",     "6019":"WARN" } Response: success Action: it publishes Alert and PO order based on updates params Success submit – it sends a delete for RTS. this RTS should disappear from RTS grid – It publishes a PO order  – no alert Terminal error – it sends a delete for RTS. this RTS should disappear from RTS grid – it publishes a ERROR alert Transient error – it publishes a WARN alert Here is request details: s…

summary: Bonds RTS: Apply Strategy is disabled for eligible rows in Summary view in the Staged workspace.
description_text: Issue as observed in Bonds QA env for  version 1.76.0-rc11 which has RTS enabled:  Apply Strategy is not disabled for ineligible rows in Summary view in the Staged workspace. Steps to re-produce the issue:- Send a couple of ineligible orders via BL OMS such that they appear in the Staged workspace in Pano2 Bonds which has RTS enabled. Select these orders in the Summary view and right click on them. Expected Behavior:- The 'Apply Strategy' context menu item should be disabled for these selected ineligible orders in the Summary view. Actual Behavior:- The 'Apply Strategy' context menu item is enabled for these selected ineligible orders in the Summary view. Clicking this menu item opens the order ticket. Selecting a strategy from this order ticket displays a blank order ticket. Notes:- Workaround for this issue is to select orders from PO grid and apply strategy. Or select Summary grid rows and click a hot button and apply it.

summary: MIN_NUM_RESPONSES_TO_IGNORE_LEVEL_LIMIT being 0 rather than null
description_text: User Story : As a user, if I set my MIN_NUM_RESPONSES_TO_IGNORE_LEVEL_LIMIT parameter to 0 I would like the value to be read as null so that I can easily start my parent order and begin trading without any errors. Context : An issue has been identified where a legacy client who does not set MIN_NUM_RESPONSES_TO_IGNORE_LEVEL_LIMIT will see their param set to 0 in the order ticket. This is because when BondLink sends the value to Pano (through both the BLGW and the PO FIX), it is incorrectly setting the value as 0 rather than null. This change on their end will be completed to submit null rather than 0 but until then we will alter the logic such that a 0 is read as null. There is no rationale to a client wanting to set their MIN_NUM_RESPONSES_TO_IGNORE_LEVEL_LIMIT to 0, as this would mean the limit is ignored every time, regardless of the number of responses. Therefore, the value of 0 is not valid. Change : When MIN_NUM_RESPONSES_TO_IGNORE_LEVEL_LIMIT is set to 0 by the client, strip out this param from being sent down to TE on the API call (Sree: note this was done in the UI and not ctrlg, so its the UI that checks and strips out the param from the request sent to ctrl-svc , ctrlg, teapi, te) QA Testing Note : This will impact both Parent Orders coming through BondLink submission (FIX, Automation Manager, and Right-Click submit) and orders coming through RTS Routing (Staged workspace). Param id - 3848

summary: WS: 1.75 MDM: Fallback from 'primaryOpenPx' to 'OpenPx' in UI when displaying "open price"
description_text: Background Recently, we updated the back-end Market Data Feed application to provide  primaryOpenPx  as the price of the trade received from the instrument's primary venue which is marked, by trade conditions, as being the opening trade.  On startup, the back-end Market Data Feed applications subscribes to receive (among other data) all trades on NYSE of NYSE-listed instruments. This provides us the  primaryOpenPx  for all NYSE-listed instruments. By other means, using code which has been in we obtain the  OpenPx  for any instrument (in our security master file) that a user requests by asking Activ for the opening price. If a user requests market data for a NYSE instrument, the UI receives the  primaryOpenPx  (and, if the instrument was reopened, the reopen price), and everything is good. If a user requests market data for a Nasdaq-listed instrument that trades on NYSE, the back end was not subscribed to its trades on NASDAQ, so does not have its primary open price, so does not provide  primaryOpenPx , even though it can provide  OpenPx . Requirement Modify the UI to follow the rules outlined in   for displaying "OpenPx", for all cases in the UI where the opening price is displayed using data from the Market Data Feed.

summary: Enhance trader and order data in the BLGW
description_text:
Update BLGW based on the new fields needed: RequestViewModel Name from IS Dev Note Param ID ccy This is an existing param ID (3034) for currency. Please place the IS field  ccy  into the existing param ID. 3034 companyShortName This is a new param ID and new data. Please map the new param ID to the new IS field. i.e. "ABN_US" 2042 companyFullName This is an existing param ID (3404). Please map this new IS field into this existing param ID. i.e. "Russell IS" 3404 traderFullName This is a new param and new data. Please map the new param ID to the new IS field. This is the full name of the trader, i.e. "Lucas Ferreira" 2044 traderShortName We are changing the name of traderName to traderShortName 3405 adxEligibleStrategies This field is going to include spaces now. Pano impact: We would like to show the list as sent by BLGW in panorama so that it is a better user experience. Map the value directly into the param (2038). Order ticket impact: We must ensure that the spaces added to the string are accounted for in BLGW so that the Order Ticket strategy picker may still be able to read and pick up the strategy names and show those in the dropdown.  i.e. "Provide, Seek, Take"   IMPORTANT A simple code change needs to be done in the javascript code.  In the file `ams/client/src/app/parent-order/parent-order.utils.ts`    This code export const flattenRtsEligibleStrategies = (rows: RxData[] = []) =>
  uniq(flatten(rows.map(row => row[Ams.AdxEligibleStrategies]?.split(','))))
    .map(blgwStrategyToTicketId)
    .filter(Boolean); Needs to be changed to export const flattenRtsEligibleStrategies = (rows: RxData[] = []) =>
  uniq(flatten(rows.map(row => row[Ams.AdxEligibleStrategies]?.split(','))))
    .map(strategy => blgwStrategyToTicketId(strategy?.trim())) // HERE"S WHAT CHANGED
    .filter(Boolean); 2038 Note for ccy (3034): Please remove all mapping for 2006 as currency in the BLGW and testing harnesses/simulations.

summary: Unsolicited RTS - Support for both ISIN and CUSIP
description_text: Context : See PAN-8891 for detailed business context. Each uploaded bond is uniquely identified by its security identifier, which is expected to be either a CUSIP or ISIN.  Reject uploaded bonds with invalid securities with reason 'Invalid SecID'. A CUSIP (Committee on Uniform Security Identification Procedures) number consists of  9 characters  alphanumeric. The first six characters identify the issuer, the seventh and eighth identify the specific issue, and the ninth is a check digit. Example, 90131HAR6. Be aware that traders sometimes upload CUSIPs without the 9th check digit, but we don't need to worry about it for now. An ISIN (International Securities Identification Number) is a 12-character alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify securities worldwide. It consists of a two-letter country code, a nine-digit numeric identifier, and a single check digit. Example, US90131HAR66. Implementation BondLink (BL) Integration Server (IS) supports already ISIN and CUSIP populated in the same Identifier field. The simple change is to rename "CUSIP" references to "Identifier" in the Unsolicited RTS code in both BE and FE

summary: AMS apps - expose log-level via an env-var, similar to WS apps
description_text: Issue For AMS apps ONLY, there is no easy way to enable DEBUG logging, useful for QA - currently the only way to do this is via a JVM arg. For WS, all apps expose the log-level via env vars (<app_name>_LOG_LEVEL). Requirement We want to expose log-level for AMS apps, similar to what we do for WS apps To do Expose log-level for each app using an env var, similar to what is done for WS (see ws/<backend>/app/..../application.yml) Env var should be optional, default to INFO level if var is not defined (same as WS) update all the local default properties file in ams/ to add these vars, but commented out. Again see the local default props file in ws update pano2cfg repo to add the vars commented out in ALL/common-env-vars. Note that pano2cfg is only used by AMS, so there is no "see ws" for this Update C&D on this PAN with the new vars. Check with Ayub for any q's on configs, vars, etc.

summary: blgw - document streaming behavior for different startup sequences and disconnect scenarios
description_text: Document the following aspects If blgw starts before rx-svc, does is subscribe to IS right away or only after rx-svc connects ? If the former, then does it start receiving messages from IS ? are these messages q'd ? if so, what happens if the message count exceeds the back-pressure buffer size are these msgs sent to rx when it connects later If rx-svc connects to ParentOrders , receives POs, and disconnects after a while, are any POs during the disconnect period held back and sent to rx on reconnect, or lost ? If rx-svc connects to ParentOrders, disconnects, and reconnects - does it receive any new messages after the reconnect or is the channel dead ? If rx-svc connects to  Alerts , receives alerts, and disconnects after a while, are any Alerts during the disconnect period held back and sent to rx on reconnect, or lost ? If rx-svc connects to  Alerts , disconnects, and reconnects - does it receive any new Alert messages after the reconnect or is the channel dead  Note: Alert behavior may be different than PO behavior, so test both.

summary: blgw - blgw alerts channel fails to publish following a disconnect

Following the rx-svc bounce, rx-svc successfully reconnected to blgw, and received RTS POs. However it did not receive any Alerts. We then attempted to troubleshoot the Alerts channel by connecting to it from curl. The expectation is for the connection to remain open, and Alerts to be written to the console. summary: POL:  Feature updates to Start / End times for Split and Amend dialogs
description_text: This is a Story created to fix IL-3510 for which some feature changes came up to the way Start/End times are used for the Split and Amend dialogs, with    and  . See below points of the requested fixes/updates. ------------------------------------------------------- Split dialog action for the Start/End times: Splitting either via a hot button or once the algo has been selected in the drop down, start and end time auto populates. User cannot enter a start time earlier than the current hour. (e.g. if the user is trying to split at 10:05:00 he cannot split with a start time earlier than 10:00:00, but also if a user is trying to split at 10:56:00 the user still cannot split with a start time past 10:00:00) Users cannot enter a start time later than end time or vice versa. The latest eligible split  start time  should be 15:59:59 and the latest eligible  end time  should be 16:00:00 (if the user enters a time past these parameters, a red dialog should be displayed underneath the rejected time box – split button grayed out) If splitting via a hot button with pre-set  start  and/or  end time , display the values saved on the hot button and display a message under the timebox (also grayed out split button) if the order is not eligible to split using the times entered. Make time fields mandatory. Do not add a trailing zero (9 becomes 90) if the user enters only one digit in any of the boxes, instead will add the zero in front (9 become 09) CO Amend dialog for the Start/End times: IF the starttime <= current time, when the user brings up the amend dialog, display the current time on the start time box. IF the starttime > current time, when the user brings up the amend dialog, display the original CO time on the start time box. Display the original start time or last amend time (just the time without any writing) underneath the start time box for reference. Same time constraints apply as in the PO split. Known issue:  When splitting PO with a different end time than 4:00PM, s…

summary: Unsolicited RTS: Summary of issues for the initial round of testing - Front end.
description_text: Following is the summary of issues found by Charan/ me for the initial round of testing for the  Unsolicited uploader  in Bonds QA env for  version 1.76.0-rc19 which has Unsolicited RTS enabled:- Uploading unsupported files / Paste errors:- It tries to load unsupported files first, which takes time and then later throws an error. I think we should reject them upfront. Likewise, if paste is done incorrectly, for e.g . header is not pasted, then it gets stuck in a loop. Yan - Instead of showing "Unsupported file format" we will show "Unsupported content". Since the same error occur when pasting from the clipboard. 'Add Items' button is present in the Adaptive workspace. It should be removed since it doesn't work from this workspace and is not expected to. Having it here is confusing. And for some uploads which fail with the error: "Invalid Order Request", the Alert Review screen does not show anything in the Alert column for the row that failed upload. Please see the attached screenshot for the same. As per  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-8891  , the Review button in Alerts should be present to display a Review dialog only for the failed uploads. Currently, this is available even if all the rows are uploaded successfully. Please see the attached screenshot for the same.

summary: RTS - Share Grid Tabs
description_text: Need: As we roll out new features and onboard clients it’s a big benefit for client engagement if we can push grid tabs to users - either standard tabs for new functionality, or something we customize for their use case. Pushing a whole workspace isn’t great because it then requires the user to then move grid tabs around - which our end users don’t know how to do and don’t really want to learn about. Sketch: Mimic the capability that exists for workspaces, but allow it for a single grid tab. TBD: We can do it first to a matched name, and if there isn’t to the default. OR we could copy the grid to all the workspaces of the same type. ((DOUBLE CHECK WITH TRADE DESK)) (We may separately want to lock down the 2 standard workspaces; tbd separately.)

Currently the stats are wiped out after the RFQ is no longer active. The work is being done as part of  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/QU-3082 In order to ensure that the user doesnt get confused as to which RFQ the stats apply, we want to gray out the columns if TakeStatus is not populated ( the RFQ is no longer live). summary: Bonds RTS: List columns are not displaying the correct values in the Summary grid when the underlying parent orders contains both lists as well as single orders.
description_text: Issue as observed in Bonds QA env for  version 1.76.0-rc19 which has RTS enabled:  List columns are not displaying the correct values in the Summary grid when the underlying parent orders contains both lists as well as single orders. Steps to re-produce the issue:- Send a single RTS order from suffix and verify that it appears in the Pano2 Bonds Staged workspace with the values for the columns 'BLListName', 'BondLinkListId', 'BLListId' as blank. Send a list from suffix and verify that it appears in the Pano2 Bonds Staged workspace with the values for the columns 'BLListName', 'BondLinkListId', 'BLListId' populated for the list orders based on what was set in suffix. Verify the column values for these columns in the Summary view for a grid tab that has not been aggregated by any of the list columns. For e.g. check in the 'Eligibility' tab in the Summary view. Expected Behavior:- The value 'Mixed' should be present for each of these columns in the Summary view since the summary row contains a mix of blank and non blank values for the underlying parent orders for these columns. Actual Behavior:- The values from the list based orders are populated for these columns instead. Please see the attached screenshot for the same. Note:- For a summary view which is aggregated using one of the above list columns, the values are populated correctly.

summary: Throw an 400 for User with No externalId send  "route to adx" or "rts submit" request

Which is sometime user forgot to setup in PAD. As Current implementation does have any way to know about this requirement from UI. So We now want a specific exception message from backed control services. So that UI can use this message to show why "route-rts-to-adx" is failed. summary: SSR/HALT/Resumed alerts are not functioning properly
description_text: Issue: Multiple SSR / Halt alerts in the same security update the first alert in the instrument with a new time and new description. One problem caused by this is that if the user has cleared the first alert, than the alert remains cleared even when it updates. Same alert is getting updated instead of creating new alert for a security. Halts due to Limit Up Limit Down do not include the text "Limit Up Limit Down" in the Alert Text Reason is not parted properly. TO DO: As the part of fix. we are creating uniqID for each alter. and set to alert's rowId and alertId(6016). As we saw that halt reason is not parsed properly. we are going to fix that. and add halt reason as the part of alert details(6021) as well as alert title text. Note  that we are not setting alert's group.  no longer groups by symbol Note:  take a look at the below link how the reason is parse For halt reason for nyse:  https://support.activfinancial.com/feed-info/feeds/detail?id=88#Utp_Trading%20Status%20Reason For halt reason for NSDQ:  https://support.activfinancial.com/feed-info/feeds/detail?id=78#Cta_Trading%20Status%20Reason

summary: Fix IL-3486 - suppress ctrlg warn "Not Found a RTS mod request"
description_text: Context ctrlg uses the 99430/BL_INQUIRY_ID to identify Pano-RTS orders and then amend them with it's cached values for this BL_INQUIRY_ID. If it doesnt find the cached values, it logs the error noted in the IL and below. This logic is buggy because TE can receive RTS orders with BL_INQUIRY_ID from non-Pano sources - and ctrlg wont have any cached values for them. See IL-3486 comment also. To do Suppress this warning in ctrlg, it is not required Not Found a RTS mod request, inquiryId:24643908, taskId: ny5-tebond-mktx-dev1.1dgddc834742 The only downside of squelching the warn is that ctlg will no longer warn for a pano-rts-order where it cannot find the amend-details - this is very unlikely. Even if it does happen, the only downside is the PO is not populated with the additional fields, and not started. The user will know because the PO shows up in ADX with none of the expected fields populated, and they can apply the hot-button manually and start it.
