Steps to re-produce the issue:- Click the Hot buttons toggle icon in the Staged workspace in Pano2 Bonds workspace header. Click it once more. Expected Behavior:- Toggling the button on and off should open / close the Hot buttons panel. Actual Behavior:- The Hot buttons remains open and does not close upon toggling. Notes:- The toggling is working as expected in the Adaptive workspace.

summary: Missing side on several orders in 1.75 Pano2
description_text: when we see a task for the first time, mark it as 'not yet published' if we get an task param/stat update, and ALL entitlement fields are NOT present in the task, dont publish the update if we get an task param/stat update, and ALL entitlement fields ARE present AND the task is marked as 'not yet published', then publish all fields of the task. if we get an task param/stat update, and ALL entitlement fields ARE present AND that task is previously published, then publish only the deltas Edit

summary: Bonds RTS: Apply Strategy is not disabled for ineligible rows in Summary view in the Staged workspace.
description_text: Issue as observed in Bonds QA env for  version 1.76.0-rc11 which has RTS enabled:  Apply Strategy is not disabled for ineligible rows in Summary view in the Staged workspace. Steps to re-produce the issue:- Send a couple of ineligible orders via BL OMS such that they appear in the Staged workspace in Pano2 Bonds which has RTS enabled. Select these orders in the Summary view and right click on them. Expected Behavior:- The 'Apply Strategy' context menu item should be disabled for these selected ineligible orders in the Summary view. Actual Behavior:- The 'Apply Strategy' context menu item is enabled for these selected ineligible orders in the Summary view. Clicking this menu item opens the order ticket. Selecting a strategy from this order ticket displays a blank order ticket.

summary: Bonds RTS: LiquidityScore column needs to be of type Integer in PAD.
description_text: As reported by Frank / Rosie, the values in the 'LiquidityScore' column in Pano2 Bonds with RTS enabled has values formatted with a single decimal point. They want the values to be formatted without any decimal points, i.e. they want the column to be of type Integer in PAD. Notes:- Using any of the existing formatters in PAD does not resolve the issue. Hence, a data type update is needed for this column (column 2008) which requires a code change as it cannot be done in PAD. Validated this in Bonds QA env for version 1.76.0-rc11.

summary: Bonds RTS: Symbol value is not populated in the Staged workspace thus causing the Exact Symbol search to not work.
description_text: Issue as observed in Bonds QA env for  version 1.76.0-rc11 which has RTS enabled:  Symbol value is not populated in the Staged workspace thus causing the Exact Symbol search to not work. Steps to re-produce the issue:- Send an RTS order via BL OMS such that it appears in the Staged workspace in Pano2 Bonds. Observe the value for PragmaSym column (column 5165) in PO grid for this order. Perform an exact symbol search in PO grid in this workspace for the symbol used by turning the exact symbol search on (by clicking the diamond icon next to the search icon in the PO grid header). Perform an exact symbol search in the same manner at the workspace level as well. Expected Behavior:- The symbol value should be populated for this RTS order in the PragmaSym column (column 5165) in the PO grid in the Staged workspace in Pano2 Bonds. Exact symbol search should work for this symbol value for both the PO grid and the workspace. Actual Behavior:- The value for this column is blank in the PO grid in the Staged workspace. Hence, Exact symbol search is not working for either the PO grid or at the workspace level in the Staged workspace. Notes:- Need to check with   if it's a mapping issue and with   if it's a Bond Link issue. Based on that, the issue should be fixed as applicable. Also, once a strategy is applied for one of these RTS orders and submitted, the PragmaSym column value is populated in the PO grid in the Adaptive workspace for this order. Which causes Exact symbol search to work for orders in the Adaptive workspace.

summary: Bonds RTS: Notes are not updated for RTS orders in the Staged workspace.
description_text: Issue as observed in Bonds QA env for  version 1.76.0-rc11 which has RTS enabled:  Notes are not updated for RTS orders in the Staged workspace. Steps to re-produce the issue:- Send an RTS order via BL OMS with a Note set to "test" via FIX tag 6122. Send a list order via BL OMS with a Note set to "test" via FIX tag 26122. Observe the values for the Notes column (Column 3020) in the PO grid in Bonds QA env - Staged workspace for these RTS orders. Expected Behavior:- The value "test" should be present in this column for both the orders. This is based on the mapping present for this column in the sheet 'CanAPI RequestViewModel UI Map' in the excel  https://marketaxesscorp-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/g/personal/dmechner_marketaxess_com/EUoLCQWAFKlIkXPKMs4w6roBj2a70TNK6KlzIF2iuib3AQ?wdOrigin=TEAMS-MAGLEV.p2p_ns.rwc&wdExp=TEAMS-TREATMENT&wdhostclicktime=1743086550719&web=1  (This mapping was provided by Rosie and specifies the Pano2 columns for RTS orders in the Staged workspace in Pano2 Bonds and where these values are mapped in BondLink). Actual Behavior:- This column is blank for a single order (35=D) as well as for lists (35=E). Please see the attached screenshots for the same. Notes:- Need to check with   if it's a mapping issue and with   if it's a Bond Link issue. Based on that, the issue should be fixed as applicable. Also, once a strategy is applied for one of these RTS orders and submitted, the note value "test" is populated in the PO grid in the Adaptive workspace for this order.

summary: Bonds RTS: VIEW_INSTANCE entitlement is not working for the Staging workspace.
description_text: Issue as observed in Bonds QA env for  version 1.76.0-rc11 which has RTS enabled:  VIEW_INSTANCE entitlement is not working for the Staging workspace. Steps to re-produce the issue:- Send an RTS order via BL OMS such that it appears in the Staged workspace in Pano2 Bonds. Observe the instance for this order in the PO grid. It should be "RTS". Remove any other VIEW_ entitlements in PAD for this user and add the entitlement - VIEW_INSTANCE = RTS (or rts in lower case). Refresh Pano2 Bonds UI. Expected Behavior:- Since, for any Staged order, the Instance is "RTS", the RTS order should be visible in the Staged workspace. Actual Behavior:- The staged order disappears from the Staging workspace after the entitlements update and application refresh. Please see the attached screenshots for the same. Notes:- As discussed with   , BLGW is sending instance id as "RTS" in caps. And Pano processes entitlements with lower case. Hence BLGW needs to send the instance as "rts'. Whether the VIEW_INSTANCE is updated to "rts" or "RTS" in PAD, it doesn't make a difference and does not resolve the issue. Need to confirm this logic with   or   and determine if the change needs to be made in Bond Link side or Pano side. In addition, need to check with   and   if this is a realistic production scenario before prioritizing this fix.

summary: WS:   1.75 MDM - T&S section missing OpenPx
description_text: Version:  1.75-rc41  (Reported by UAT). Issue:  OpenPx is missing for the MDM > Time & Sales section.  (See screenshot with compared to 1.75-rc28 which shows the value populating). NOTE Current UI logic to display  OpenPx  is to use  reopenPx  or  primaryOpenPx  field values. The  openPx  field does provide the value but it's not used for display. const openPx = reopenPx || primaryOpenPx; Also,  OpenPx  label in the UI will get renamed to  PriOpenPx  per   request. Developer's Notes Logging was added to record the incoming trades during the open of April 23, 2025. Trades such as the following were seen: 2025-04-23 09:30:00.023573504 Instrument: RSG trade condition " O I" exchange: Q 25 @ $106.92 This contains two trade conditions: O (Market Center Opening Trade) and I (Odd Lot trade). This is the opening trade at the market center for RSG, but it was an odd lot (25 shares). We are currently excluding all odd lot trades, which causes us to miss this opening trade. Requirement Process all open or re-open trades. Otherwise, process all trades except odd lot, conditional, or extended hours trades. This does not affect any of the volume calculations performed by MDSS. This applies only to "last trade" and "open" or "reopen" information provided to the UI by MDF.

summary: RTS - blgw improvements related to VirtualListId & adxEligibleStrategies
description_text: New requirements added to PAN-8892 Please review the requirements, including the the  DONT-DO Deferred to Later  with Rosie before starting on this Requirement / Implementation sketch adxEligibleStrategies  ** BLGW currently converts adxEligibleStrategies to a comma-delimited list of strategy values, e.g. "Take,Provide,SomeOtherStrategy" when populating the Pano field 2038. This list sometimes includes non-adx-eligible strategies ("SomeOtherStrategy" in the example). When we populate 2038, we should drop any non-adx-eligible strategies. Using the example here, 2038 should only contain "Take,Provide", and not the "SomeOtherStrategy". Consult a config value for the list of adx-eligible strategies, so that no blgw code change is required if the list of adx-eligible strategies changes Unrelated small bugfix -  in application.yml - change the log file name to  blgw.log  (currently mog.log) DONT-DO, deferred to later (check with Rosie this is still dont-do-deferred-to-later (note from rosie: yes, defer.) Requirement The default layout for Summary View and Parent Order View includes the following tabs: List View - Group by 3870, unless null. If null then show the individual item with an artificial value of (bondLinkInquiryId) . Implementation sketch 3870 is normally populated by VirtualListId. If VirtualListId is null or empty, then populate 3870 with bondLinkIquiryId (same value as 2018). The goal is that 3870 published to rx-svc is  never  null or blank

summary: Unsolicited RTS - add support in blgw
description_text: Implement blgw support for Unsolicited order features specified in To do: Rename branch feature/  with the existing code to feature/PAN-9875 .., (or create a new branch off the 8891 branch, and then delete that brancu) Target release branch is release/1.76 Added by Sree 4/3 following discussion with Yan set the alertCategory to UNS_RTS <finalize naming with Yan> for Unsolicited Alerts. For reference, the AlertCategory for RTS Alerts is "END_USER" If the alert includes error rows, set the eventType to "Review" (finalize naming with Yan) The errors for a failed order is a map of (colId, errorText). An error that is NOT col-specific. will go against a pseuduo-column in this error map named "row" Developer notes the unsolicted order from blgw to  IS  must include groupId and id. These will need to be generated either by blgw or ctrl-svc/ctrlg. We should decide and generate both these ids from the same app - blgw or ctrl-svc or ctrlg. blgw should cache pending requests before it submits them to IS.  The cache is keyed by groupId, and value is a "?RequestObject?. when blgw receives extCmdStatus from IS for a submitted order, it uses this map to find the corresponding requestObject and the order. When extCmdStatus for all orders in a cached RequestObject have been received, publish an Alert for this request. AND delete this request from the cache. The RequestObject is also associated with a timeout. On expiry, the RequestObject should publish as a alert to rx-svc. Any orders that have not yet received a extCmdStatus on timeout should be included in the 'failed orders' list on the alert. check with UI developers if they need a means to distinguish alert type for solicited vs unsolicited RTS Alert TBD - what is the shape of the Alert-with-list object in blgw ? in rx-svc ? How does it get deserialized/reserialized in rx-svc. TBD - in blgw, we want to be able to distinguish between solicited vs unsolicited RTS extCmdStatus. If easy, provide this info on the extCmdStatus. Other…

summary: Bonds RTS: Staged orders are not displayed for a user not having any other VIEW entitlements other than the VIEW_ALL entitlement
description_text: Issue as observed in Bonds QA env for  version 1.76.0-rc7 which has RTS enabled:  Staged orders are not displayed for a user not having any other VIEW entitlements other than the VIEW_ALL entitlement. Steps to re-produce the issue:- Login with a user in Pano2 Bonds for a user that has VIEW_ALL entitlement in PAD (along with ENABLE_RTS_ROUTING and any other entitlements required to view RTS orders and perform various actions on them). Send an RTS order via BL OMS such that it appears in the Staged workspace. Verify that it appears in the Staged workspace in Pano2 Bonds. Modify the Entitlements in PAD for the same user: Remove the VIEW_ALL entitlement and add another VIEW entitlement such as VIEW_CLIENT_TRADER or VIEW_FIRM such that the entitlement should match the previously sent RTS order. Refresh the application and wait a few seconds. Expected Behavior:- The RTS order should be displayed in the Staged workspace. Actual Behavior:- The RTS order disappears from the Staged workspace once the VIEW_ALL entitlement is removed and the application is refreshed. Please see the attached screenshots for the same. Note:- This issue is only observed for RTS orders. Once a strategy is applied and RTS orders move to the active 'Adaptive' workspace, the orders are displayed in this workspace even for a user without the VIEW_ALL entitlement.

summary: RTS - ctrl-svc - add loggedInUserBondLinkTraderId to rtx-to-adx request from UserProfile.exernal_id
description_text: Background IS expects the submit-to-adx request to include the traderId on the order for authentication. The traderId on the order is the value of col 3646 (traderId) on the inbound RTS. Requirement Include the traderId on the submit-to-rts request from ctrl-svc to ctrg to blgw. For proper authentication, the traderId should NOT be supplied by the Client (from 3646), as that can be spoofed by a malicious client. Instead, this will be implemented as follows: To do ctrl-svc should enrich the submit-to-adx request with the requestFirmTrader - it should use User.external_id for this value. This will require the user_code to be setup in pad-svc to match the user's 3646. Pano entitlement-api might need to be expanded to include the user.externalId - it currently does not include this field. The Client should NOT include 3646 on the submit-to-adx request. If it does this currently, ctrl-svc should NOT use this value as it can be spoofed, it should use the value from User.external_id as noted in 2) - if the above is not quick, and using the entitlement-cache 3646 is quick, or if asking the ui to specify 3646 is quick, do one of those as a temp workaround, and follow up with the fix noted above Notes: We do not want to use the 3646 value from the entitlementCache that ctrl-svc gets from blgw for the following reason - BL only supports submission by the owner of the order. Specifically it does NOT support for example, trade-desk or a supervisor submitting a request on behalf of the order-owner - this flexibility is only available in Pano. To avoid any surprises from BL clients ("how was someone else able to submit my order ?!!") , we want to be conservative - an adx submitted by anyone other than the order owner (e.g. user.traderId =  rosie), will be rejected by current BL validation (because rosie != order.3646). If we want to allow this capability of trade-desk or a supervisor to submit orders on the user's behalf, then we should remove the current IS check of user.trader =…

summary: RTS - remove te-api listener for RTS in control gateway and move to POG
description_text: Requirement : Control Gateway today connects Conc vi te-api and attach taskListener to all type of task to get blgw taskId. And we don't have any way to remove taskListener. This kind of implementation in control services put burden on concentrator. Implementation Since POG already connects to CONC. and Gets all task related info.  we want to add an end point on POG for blgw rts feed. POG will publish all blgw taskId and InquieryId which Control Gateway can consume. that way no new burden to CONC. Steps Remove te-api listener for RTS in control gateway Implement a new endpoint /api/rts/feed for RTS data (<taskId, InquiryId>) in POG POG publishes blgw data(<taskId, InquiryId>) when it receives a task param update with TaskParam.BL_INQUIRY_ID set. Control gateway subscribe to POG for RTS data(<taskId, InquiryId>) On data in control gateway, check inquiryId in the data waiting RTS. if inquiryId is in waiting queue then send a apply RTS mod request with taskId received in data row  (<taskId, InquiryId>) Q&A What are the implications of this change for the end-user none

summary: MDF: Fix ACTIV Content Gateway conflation
description_text: Background MDF currently implements conflation in the application, receiving updates from ACTIV as fast as they happen. This wastes bandwidth and CPU resources. We want to support conflation at the application (for non-ACTIV data sources) and at ACTIV. Requirement Support conflation at ACTIV and in the application for MDF. Development Notes If conflation occurs twice: once at ACTIV and once in the app, and each of the two conflations uses the same period, the average total conflation is 1.5 times the period. This means that we want the app conflation and the ACTIV conflation to be 2/3 of the conflation interval specified in the config file. If ACTIV is not being used, the app will conflate to the specified conflation interval, not 2/3 of it. Logging like the following occurs when a conflated subscription is created: 2025-02-27 11:03:45.975  INFO 29399 --- [t thread. Id: 1] c.p.p.m.p.activ.UpdateProcessorNbbo      : Subscribed to BBO of F. (table 0) CONFLATION_TYPE_TRADE conflated to 500ms Upstream (ACTIV) conflation is enabled or disabled for subscription types as follows: Subscription Type ACTIV Conflation enabled Basic (Trade State and Price Indication) No Depth of Book No NBBO (and NYSE Imbalance) Yes Composite Depth of Book No Price Indication No Security Definitions No Top of Book Yes NYSE Top of the Book No

Client want to see last applied hotbutton details for a particular order. 2. This can be a behaviour for last 1 or 2 days since older ones might not be relevant. 3. This can be shown as a column in the PO grid. 4. Client will not see this info across different devices since it's stored on the client side only. 5. Render hot-button column with similar colour same as hotbutton. Dev Approach:   Save in local-storage to persist across sessions. Clean-up old records from local-storage. Add new record in local-storage as soon as the hot button is applied to the order. Update record if applied hot-button changes. Note:  As per discussion with Abhay, this change has been included for all(EQ, FX, Bonds, Rates) Current Implementation:  1. Last Applied Hot Button for an order persist even if the order is modified after that later on. 2. Order and Hot Button Mapping Persist for Max 2 days. (similar to cleared alerts behaviour) 3. Ways to clear hot button from order:     * Remove hot-button from the hot-button panel     * Update to different hot-button     * If none of the above 2 step is taken. The mapping will be cleared after 2 days of applying for the   order. This happens because we keep record of mapping for max 2 days only in local-storage. 4. The mapping is stored in the browser only. No backend API is called to save this info…

summary: POL Alerts for SSR/Halts and NYSE Price Indications for all symbols, not just NYSE-listed
description_text: PAN-9095 implemented Alerts from MDF for SSR Halts, etc. The initial feature specified just NYSE symbols. We just found out that this is required for all symbols, not just NYSE symbols. Extend the work done in PAN-9095 to support all symbols We want to provide: LULD and trading halts/resumes, and short sale restrictions, from the SIP (the consolidated feed, NYSE CTA A, B, and C), for all instruments traded on NYSE. NYSE's price indications for all instruments trading on NYSE. Development Notes We are modifying WS MDF as follows: on startup, WS MDF will request an ACTIV subscription for NYSE trading state information (halts and resumes, with reason text like LULD) for all instruments in the SecMaster file, whether or not the instrument is listed on NYSE. on startup, WS MDF will request an ACTIV subscription for NYSE price indications for all instruments in the SecMaster file, whether or not the instrument is listed on NYSE. We assume that the WS Security Master File will only contain NYSE-traded instruments, and will not contain any instruments that are not equities traded on NYSE. If it does, then we will waste CPU and network resources subscribing to the trading state and NYSE price indications for instruments which do not trade on NYSE.

summary: BondLink Integration Alerts
description_text: Sree 3/13 - QA heads up - this feature was implemented against this PAN, and not the related story PAN-9596. Summary: Given the data flow between BLGW and Integration Server, users must receive alerts on problem orders so they can have awareness of and remediate potential issues as they occur. Receiving Data from IS to BLGW: BLGW must listen to ExtendedCommandStatus to receive data sent from IS. Pano Alerts ExtendedCommandStatus messages with status SUCCESS should be silently dropped in blgw and no alert published or other action taken for these. For ExtendedCommandStatus messages with status FAILURE, blgw should publish  Pano Alerts  on a new  alerts  SSE endpoint. rx-svc will connect to this endpoint to receive RTS related alerts. We will need a new type/category field on these alerts that indicates these system alerts should be published to all entitled users. Current Pano only publishes user alerts to users. Key data formatting on ExtendedCommandStatus includes: ParamId Pragma Name ExtendedCommandStatus Name Type 1. Description 2. BLGW Processing {*} * 6016 AlertId Id String 1. Unique alert Id. May be synthesized from content of the alert if no native unique notification id exists. 2. Map AlertID to 6016 6017 AlertText ResultSummary String 1. Indicated the main error reason (failure, ineligible) 2. Map alertText to 6017 6018 AlertTime Timestamp Date The time the alert was raised. 6019 AlertLevel Retriable JSON 2. Map to 6019: WARN if Retriable=Y ERROR if Retriable=N 6020 AlertCategory String 1. Application-specific; displayed on the first line of the alert item. 2. Hardcoded for “END_USER” to 6020   (Pano Note: this is likely a new field in Alerts). 6021 AlertDetails errorMessage traderShortName companyShortName String 1. Additional text about the notification, displayed in a panel suitable for multi-line text. 2. Map alertDetails to 6021 like this     <errorMessage> <traderShortName>@<companyShortName> 6022 AlertEventType String 1. - 2. Hardcode to “New” "rowId…

Discuss with Mr. Robot, QA, and confirm ** Scenario mog support for playwright blgw publishes RTS playwright calls the mog pass-thru endpoint with a valid RTS PO. mog publishes to rx-svc user submits rts to adx existing (implemented by other RTS mog PAN) mog behavior - publish rts-hide, publish vanilla-po, publish amended-po. playwright can validate all 3 on the UI. blgw publishes rts-error status and alert playwright calls the mog pass-thru endpoint with a valid RTS-PO-Status-update message, and valid RTS-Alert message. mog passes thru and publishes these msgs to rx. summary: RTS - mog - publish status updates and Alerts for RTS errors or status changes
description_text: Sree 3/13 -    check that when we publish a WARN Alert, we do NOT publish anything for the RTS PO, make sure RTS PO's adxEligible status is still true, so that this RTS can be resubmitted. Background An RTS that is displayed in Pano can become ineligible for submit-to-adx, or permanently-canceled. The following are some use cases RTS was submitted to ADX or some other action in BondLink, so no longer available for submit-to-ADX from Pano (is this already handled by PAN-????) RTS was submitted from Pano, and rejected due to a temporary condition - for example, risk check. If it is tried later/again after the temporary condition is resolved, it will succeed RTS was submitted from Pano, and encountered a condition/error in BL that caused BL to cancel this RTS completely. It is effectively dead, and can no longer be submitted. In each of these cases blgw/mog needs to publish a RTS PO status update with the related fields. It also publishes an Alert for the RTS error. To do Discuss with Mr. Robot team the different scenarios for the RTS errors, including transient and terminal errors. In sim mode, publish the appropriate RTS PO status update and alert for each of these cases. These RTS Alerts endpoint in mog should be different than the regular alerts endpoint (if we did this in WS, otherwise dont bother) Add config options to specify the frequency at which to publish each type of error - either per/minute or per/rts pos published. Allow this option to be enabled/disabled for simulated mode (tbd - what about file-replay mode) update pano repo properties files with the config options update pano2cfg repo bond envs env-settings with config options

summary: RTS - rx-svc - design and implement 'hide/delete PO'
description_text: Context When an RTS is submitted to ADX, IS will send a RTS-routed message to blgw, and this should trigger the RTS from being removed from the Staging are PO grid in Pano. At present, rx-svc sends delete-row to the Client for a PO, and the Client deletes the PO from the grid.  HOWEVER , the current rx-svc implementation is to handle WS Assign-order functionality, where the PO is (deleted) for one trader only (the 'assigned-from' trader). Whereas we need functionality that deletes the PO for EVERYONE. To do 1. Discuss with blgw devs and finalize the PO cols & values used to specify a hide/delete 2. Come up with a design for rx-svc to delete the row from it's cache, and publish a delete-row message to all subscribers, implement 3. Add unit and integration tests 4. Test with a real Pano client to make sure it works.

summary: RTS - blgw - publish RTS Alerts
description_text: Sree 3/13 - note for QA. This work was done in branch feature/PAN-9647 ( the feature PAN). So you wont see this PAN-9596 in the git report, there is no code/PR against this PAN. But you will see PAN-9647 in the git report, and a PR against that PAN. Background When blgw receives a submit error (on the command status channel) from IS, blgw needs to publish a status update for the RTS-PO . Additionally, blgw also needs to publish an Alert to rx-svc. The Alert is a different message type than the RTS PO. blgw will expose a 2nd streaming endpoint similar to the PO endpoint, this one to publish alerts. rx-svc will connect to this endpoint to receive alerts To do  implement alerts as specified in feature PAN-9647

summary: AMS Order Ticket - Handle eligible orders and RTS strategies
description_text: Handle strategy eligibility in the Order Ticket: The strategy selection button in the order ticket will be enabled based on the content of the adxEligibleStrategies field in the selected orders. The eligible strategies will be the union of eligible strategies across all the Staged orders in the selection (so if one order is eligible for (Take) and another is eligible for (Provide, Seek) then all three strategies (Take, Seek, Provide) will be enabled in the selection button). The If there is a multi-selection and not all selected orders are eligible for the selected strategy a summary area at the top of the order ticket will display "(4 ineligible orders)".  This is updated any time the strategy choice changes. Note the initial selection might include orders that are not eligible for ADX at all; these should be counted in the "ineligible" count in the order ticket. In the event that no order selected are eligible for adx, or the eligible strategy set for the selection is empty (probably should never happen but...), clicking the hotbutton should have no effect. !!! IMPORTANT This change also affects EQ, FX, Rates. What will change: where we will: Show the same "(4 ineligible orders)" as RTS if some orders are not eligible. Only the eligible orders will be taken into account to populate the order ticket Ineligible orders will no longer be sent to ctrl-services

summary: AMS - Hotbutton updates: single click opens order ticket
description_text: This is a change to general AMS behavior for all asset classes (EQ, FX, BOND, and RATES). The hotbutton feature  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-4067  has been be updated to reflect this functionality (new stuff in bold in the feature). To summarize, the work is: Similar to Polaris, summary and parent_order views in AMS will now display an orange highlight around the grid to indicate which selection is "active." (But a child order selection should not change the active selection highlight in the summary or parent order views). Clicking on a hotbutton will open the order ticket (if it's not already open) and apply the contents of the hotbutton (instead of editing the hotbutton as happens today in AMS). If some of the selected orders are Live and applying the HB modifies one of the order strategy, we should show the modal dialog stating some orders needs to be paused. This is already implemented when the Order Ticket is open on HB apply. Just make sure it still work on the single-click apply. Also this should never show for RTS since no orders are live. The order ticket should (other than the above note) be opened and populated the same way as it would be today if "Amend…" had been chosen and the hotbutton clicked for the same selection. Right-click Edit... option will be the only way to edit the hotbutton.

summary: RTS - Client - display RTS Alerts
description_text: Background RTS Alerts are generated by blgw in the following failed-to-submt-rts scenarios: failed to submit, and the RTS is dead. an alert is published with: 6019 (AlertLevel) = ERROR 6020 (AlertCategory) = END_USER see feauter   for other alert fields a delete PO is published to delete the RTS from the workspace failed to submit, RTS is temporarily ineligible, and can be tried again later an alert is published with: 6019 (AlertLevel) = WARN 6020 (AlertCategory) = END_USER see feauter   for other alert fields an update PO is published with details of the failed-to-submit. To do - Client Implement Client portion of feature A. Display alerts Subscribe to Alerts with "6020=END_USER", and display them Add a clickable link on the Alert for the related RTS PO. This will take you to the related RTS PO. For Error alerts, where the PO has been deleted, this should be a no-op B. Alert badges on workspace Not in scope: confirm with DM/Rosie Add a 'unread alerts' badge to the RTS and  Pano workspaces badge shows only for the inactive workspace Update the RTS badge when we're in the ADX workspace and receive one or more RTS alerts Update the ADX badge when we're in the RTS workspace and receive one or more ADX alerts C. Entitlements Users  without  DISABLE_SYSTEM_ALERTS will see RTS alerts Users  with  DISABLE_SYSTEM_ALERTS and  with  ENABLE_RTS_ROUTING will see RTS alerts Users  with  DISABLE_SYSTEM_ALERTS and  without  ENABLE_RTS_ROUTING won't RTS alerts

summary: RTS - pog - include RTS specific columns in the PO-schema response
description_text: Background POG serves up the PO schema as a combination of  TradeEngine TaskParams & TaskStats. It gets these using TEAPI. RTS POs however have additional columns that are not task-params/stats. We need to include these cols in the PO schema served up by POG To do: Get the list of additional columns from here <todo> Include them in the PO special columns Add / update unit tests to verify these columns are served up in the PO-schema request Update the PO schema-csv-files in Pano repo with the updated column list. Two ways to do this: preferred - run the schema-dump-script against a full Pano env that includes a) latest TEAPI, and b) POG with RTS columns. The script generates the csv schema - not preferred - manually update the schema-csv with the new RTS columns Validate with local-dev setup - spin up Pano and verify rts cols are written to the db from csv files Notes: pog is not used in mog test envs. In these envs, the PO schema is obtained from csv files in the jar resources, hence the need for the schema-csv files.

summary: AMS / WS: When a column is removed from the Column picker once columns are pinned, one of the pinned columns is getting unpinned.
description_text: Steps to reproduce the bug (with AMS/WS release/1.74):- In Parent Order Grid, Right click on "Status(5000)" column header, then click "Pin Column Left" from the menu to pin the column to the left side of the grid Following the same steps, Pin "AssetClass(6003)" and "Strategy(5007)" to the left side of the grid. So that we will have 3 pinned columns. They are  Status, AssetClass, and Strategy in the order respectively Open the column picker and remove "ArrivalPrice(3147)" from the selected columns, click on Submit button to close the column picker window Expected Results:- Upon clicking on Submit button on the column picker:- The pinned columns count or their order should not be changed Actual Results / Issues:- The right most column "Strategy" in the grid is getting unpinned Notes:- This issue is also present in the Summary Grid Different columns can be used for pinning in the grid and column picker deselection Please see the attached screenshots and recordings for the same.

summary: prometheus monitoring for NFS mount on Mac mini
description_text: Background :  NFS mount disconnects have been happening more frequently. This seems to be an issue with the driver on the mac's. there isn't much we can do on our end to resolve this. the only solution we can implement right now is to have monitoring in place to alert the devops team if the nfs mount is not there. Ideally we want to have a pipeline to go and try reconnecting the NFS mount if a disconnect is detected. Requirements: setup monitoring for the nfs mount on the Mac minis. use node exporter and a custom script to run the check. have prometheus scrape for the metric setup slack alerts in prometheus if we get an NFS disconnect. Try to create a pipeline to attempt reconnecting the nfs mount via terminal if a disconnect is detected.

summary: NPM deps - Upgrade React to v19
description_text: While working on the ADX/XPRO 2.0 UX alignment, compatibility issues were identified with React 17. No clean or maintainable workaround exists, so we will migrate to React 19 to ensure compatibility with modern libraries and dependencies. This migration requires replacing several deprecated or incompatible libraries: react-beautiful-dnd : Not compatible due to the removal of React.findDOMNode. Use @hello-pangea/dnd as a drop-in replacement. While other drag-and-drop libraries exist, this option minimizes migration effort. NPM Trends:  https://npmtrends.com/@hello-pangea/dnd-vs-react-beautiful-dnd ] react-resize-detector : Replace with  @react-hooks/resize-observer , which offers equivalent functionality. NPM Trends:  https://npmtrends.com/@react-hook/resize-observer-vs-react-resize-observer react-transition-group : Not compatible due to the removal of  React.findDOMNode . Can be replaced with  framer-motion , but this change is extensive and must be implemented carefully. NPM Trends:  https://npmtrends.com/framer-motion-vs-react-transition-group-vs-react-transition-group-v2 PAD will remain on React 17 for now due to Material UI dependencies, as upgrading would effectively require a full rewrite.

summary: RTS - ctrlg - implement submit-RTS-to-ADX + order-mod (the task)
description_text: sree 1/22/25 - a rough first cut by Ayub is in PR. Implemented as noted below Background We need to implement the functionality to allow clients to submit and RTS and amend (and possibly start) the resulting Task/PO in a single step, instead of a) submit the RTS, b) wait for and find the corresponding Task in the PO grid, and c) amend and submit the PO. The way we do this is as follows: Client submits the RTS with the inquiry id and order-mod params to Pano ctrl-svc. The following steps are then done by one or more pano backend apps: cache the order-mod params submit the RTS to BLGW, which forwards it to IS. (IS / BL create an ADX Inquiry and submit to Pragma via FIX. TE creates a task, and publishes the task, params) waits for the task to show up in Pano backend. Once it does apply the cached order-mod params and submit an order-mod (or modify-and-start) to TE via TEAPI. See  Confluence here  for an analysis of the different options to implement this functionality, and the option to implement in this PA To do Implement Pano-only Option B.2 - (ctrlg and TEAPI)  on the Confluence page. Details below. Most of the work in ctrlg, minor work in ctrl-svc blgw add an action endpoint to the BondLinkGateway /rts/route-to-adx (or whatever better name devs decide). It should take a list of rowIds like similar control endpoints. Translate the request to the CanonicalAPI endpoint call to route an RTS order to ADX, and publish it over Kafka. ctrl-svc forward the rts-route-to-adx call from the client to ctrlg might need to relax entitlement depending on what's available in the message. ctrlg Workflow Part 1 - process the route-to-adx received from ctrl-svc Add a new endpoint  "/parent/rts-route-to-adx"  - accepts a list of inquiryIds, and a set of modParams. Alternately reuse the current orderMod endpoint, but extend the message body to support route-to-rts When ctrlg receives a request from ctrl-svc on this endpoint: make a 'route-to-adx' call to BLGW with the set of InquiryIds c…

summary: RTS - blgw rugeddization changes
description_text: Background This PAN is for ruggedization changes that were skipped so we could get a functional version out soon. DM comments from an earlier PR: just wanted to make sure that when you do that you spend some cycles on the reactor mechanics: do we have the right kind of sink, are we handling backpressure right, what triggers subscription, error handling, etc. When I was learning reactor I ended up making some little toy applications to make sure I understood how it would behave in some of these secnarios. Just wanted to put it on your radar To dos: Make sure blgw connects to IS only after it is connected to rx-svc. This way any messages it gets from IS can be forwarded to rx-svc. In the event of an rx-svc disconnect, blgw should also disconnect from IS or otherwise stop receiving updates from IS. This is required as blgw does NOT cache RTS inquiries from IS. If blgw connects to IS and receives RTS orders before it is connected to rx-svc, it will discard these orders and they will not display in Pano, which would be bad Review the type of sink used - multicast, backpressure Error handling on streaming failures Scheduler - do we really need a boundedElastic ?

summary: MDF: New subscription type between MDF and ACTIV for Price Indication & Trade State only
description_text: Background Currently, MDF receives price indications and trading state from ACTIV as part of its NBBO subscription. (NBBO Subscription from MDF to ACTIV.) Motivation We want to be able to subscribe, at startup, to receive price indications and trading state for all NYSE-listed instruments. We do not want to receive the "firehose" of other NBBO-associated data (trades and quotes) for all of these instruments as a "side-effect" of getting the price indications and trading state. We do not want to put unnecessary load on the Activ CG, or ad unnecessary network bandwidth between ACTIV and our MDF. Requirement Modify MDF so that it has a new type of subscription to ACTIV: BASIC_DATA. This will include the fields necessary to calculate the trading state, and the fields necessary for Price Indication. This subscription type will push trading state and (if available) price indication for a symbol into the cache. To avoid the risk of causing a problem with the current NBBO data subscription, do not remove these fields from NBBO data. This will cause some redundancy of data, but trade state and price indications change rarely (compared to quotes and trades). Modify the pre-subscribe feature so that it pre-subscribes to the new BASIC_DATA type. Development Notes I have created a new subscription type: BASIC. This just obtains trading state at the primary (halted, regular trading, quote-only, etc) and price indication data for the symbol. This subscription is not currently for use by clients, it is intended to be created at startup only. In WS, the StartupSubscriber bean is constructed. This reads the SecMaster from CLAD, and reads the config to determine which (if any) exchanges we should subscribe to, and then it opens a BASIC subscription for all instruments from secmaster with the specified exchange as their listing exchange. This data is stored in two new collection-like classes held as members within the MarketDataCache: a PriceIndications and a TradingStateChanges. The t…

summary: RfqTake - calculate the TakeTimeLeft (5683) column on PO
description_text:
Background The TakeTimeLeftSec (5368) column in PO  is a countdown timer of how much time is left to action the Rfq. At present, this column is a task_stat, and is  published by TE every 1 sec. This approach of publishing time-left from TE has several problems (homework assignment - figure em out).  With TE v8.85, TE will publish 2 additional stats TakeManualWaitStartTime, and TakeDesisionTime, from which TakeTimeLeft can be calculated  in Pano UI , and we dont need TE to publish an update every 1 sec for this column. There is some additional complexity introduced due to the fact that TE will continue to publish the TakeTimeLeftSec task-stat every 1 sec, and we do  not  want the TE-published value to override the new logic described in this PAN. This is a high priority and needs to be implemented in 1.74 as soon as possible. This PAN is only the  UI  work - calculate the value for TakeTimeLeftSec (5368) from the new task-stats (columns). Related backend work is done in To do - UI  (this PAN) Calculate an updated value for the  TakeTimeLeftSec (5368) col.  The col value is calculated from TakeDecisionTime (5374), and TakeManualWaitStartTime (5375) as noted below. Setup a timer to update the value every 0.2 ~ 0.5 secs as per the rules below. The scheduled updates (timer) begins on receipt of either the TakeManualWaitStartTime column or the  TakeDecisionTime , whichever is first. The scheduled updates (timer) ends when NOW > TakeDecisionTime Note there are 2 separate countdowns - BIN_TIMER countdown, and Manual Override countdown. Use visual cues in the column to differentiate between the BIN_TIMER countdown, and Manual Override countdown. Value calculation for TakeTimeLeftSec : BIN_TIMER_PREFIX = 'bin: '
MANUAL_OVERRIDE_PREFIX = 'ovr: '

if (TakeManualWaitStartTime is specified AND TakeManualWaitStartTime > NOW ):   // BIN TIMER COUNTDOWN
     value = BIN_TIMER_PREFIX + format ("00:00", TakeManualWaitStartTime - NOW)
     cellStyle = BIN_TIMER_STYLE

else if (T…

When user open 'Trade History' window - we display top 2 or 5 from all combined responses from 2 RFQ's. We should display 4 in summary and 10 in expanded. Q:  When we  select the PARENT ORDER , which of these do we want to show in the Trade History chart: 2 responses in summary mode / 5 responses in expanded mode, OR 8 responses in summary mode / 20 responses in expanded mode Answer / Required behavior / bug fix:  We want to display 8 responses in summary / 20 responses in expanded mode.

summary: Dockerize AMS: Enhance docker form AMS adding new features
description_text: Background: The AMS system is transitioning to a Docker-based infrastructure to better mimic the production environment and support multiple isolated testing environments. The backend components of AMS (including mog, pad-svc, app-svc, rx-svc, ctrl-svc, mdss, and mdf) need to be encapsulated within a single Docker container. This approach simplifies the deployment process while ensuring all backend services are co-located, facilitating easier management and testing. Goals: Dockerize the AMS backend by consolidating all backend components into a single container. Ensure the container runs efficiently with all required dependencies (JDK, Git, etc.). Enable communication between the AMS backend container and the Postgres database container via environment-specific configurations. Facilitate local and integration testing by using Docker Compose to manage interactions between backend and database containers. Pull Jars at docker building  process If JAR files does not exist try to pull them at bootstraping process Non-Goals: This task does not include Dockerizing the database or frontend components, which are handled separately. SSL certificate management or inclusion is not part of this task. Implementation Notes: Create a Dockerfile for the AMS backend container that includes: JDK installation Git for fetching necessary repositories (pano2cfg) Startup script ( ams.sh start ALL ) to initialize all backend services within the container Ensure the container can link to the Postgres database container using the  DATABASE_URL  environment variable managed within existing configuration files. Validate the Dockerfile locally by building the image and running the container to ensure all backend components are functioning as expected. Update the project's CI/CD pipeline to include the build and push of this AMS backend container to the Docker registry.

summary: Dockerize AMS #2 - create a Pano AMS docker stack with uigateway apps
description_text: Building on PAN-8935, this next Pano AMS Docker is meant for TradePlant developers to use, to connect to their TradePlant stack. Main differences between this one and PAN-8935. This container needs to start pog, cog, ctrlg, edg instead of mog. pog, cog, edg need to connect to external networks - usually localhost, but potentially other hosts as well. pog, edg will connect to TradeEngine or CONC running on the developer's PC cog will connect to the Tibco rvd, which typically runs on a swdev server. the start command will need to be start pog, cog, edg, ctrlg also. ams configs may need to be modified so rx-svc, ctrl-svc connects to pog, cog, ctrlg, edg instead of mog. Most likely no config changes required for this i.e. a no-op. Implementation notes spend some time discussing and decide which model we want to use for this container: Option A PAN-8935 model - git repo, build in the container, run the built jars, or we will need to pull the uigateway repo as well, and build these jars as well. The location of these built jars will be different than the pano repo built jars ams.sh will need to be modified to start / stop the uigateway repo apps, which will be in a different location than the pano repo apps. can run with any version / branch of pano - just checkout that branch and build. Option B pano2cfg model - run.sh, env-settings, pull down jars from artifactory and run these jars. No pano2 or uigateway repos to pull, no builds. What we want to do here i.e. connect to TradePlant is more aligned with this option compared to Opt A we will need an nginx instance as well, as we wont have the pnpm reverse proxy available. rather than using ams.sh, we should use pano2cfg run.sh as the start script for this option. We can use get-jars.sh to pull down the jars. This option is more lightweight from a CPU/memory perspective - no build / compile, packaged Client presumably runs less hot (tbd). Not easy to run a specific branch of Pano as this option does not build, the jars need…

summary: POL Show SSR / Halts InstStatus
description_text: Implement feature NYSEPOL-350 MDF will monitor the Activ SIP data and publish alerts to Pano when any symbol moves to Short Sell Restricted (SSR) or to Halted or Open or Limit Up/down InstrumentStatus (note: I do not know the exact text of the Activ InstrumentStatus). These alerts are not order-linked. This means that all users will see them regardless of whether they have orders in the stock that has the alert.  It also means that the Target icon on the UI will not appear for these alerts.  No alert is generated when the symbol transitions to any other InstrumentStatus. AlertText AlertDetails AlertCategory [Symbol] Short Sell Restricted same as AlertText INFO [Symbol] Halted same as AlertText INFO [Symbol] Limit Up/Down same as AlertText INFO Requirements mdf - create mock Activ-events for these alerts mdf - For WS only (not AMS): Pre-subscribe to receive trade state for all NYSE-listed instruments. (See PAN-9156). When a NYSE-listed instrument halts, raise an alert. When the instrument resumes trading, cancel the alert. Expose an RX-services compatible "drop" to publish these Alerts, for consumption by RX-services and DB-Writer. rx-svc - app.yml config changes to receive Alerts from multiple sources - drop and mdf e2e - test to see these mock Alerts in the UI mdf - code and logic to receive the real events from Activ, and replace the mock Activ-events (maybe Jamie) e2e  - test with real Active Development Notes / Suggestions The following are suggestions only. Create a new component within WS MDF, which receives notification of changes to the trade state of NYSE-listed instruments. When one of them halts, it should raise an alert. It should clear this alert when the instrument resumes trading. Create a drop to publish these alerts. Do this in a way which minimizes changes to other MDF classes. Do this in a way which uses trading state data from the currently-configured data provider (ACTIV, mock or puppet), so that halts can be 'injected' using the mock or puppet …

summary: POL Handoff Workflow - CLAD UI Changes
description_text: Implement the CLAD component of the Handoff feature:  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PF-52 . See the Feature for details, terminology, GWT, etc. Overview "Handing off" an order means that the "parent order" has been handed off to another Firm to cover - whether on the Floor or an Algo Provider. Handoff orders begin the lifecycle as a standard HighTouch parent order entered via FIX or via the UI.  When the order is split, it may move to a "Handoff" HighTouch order. There are two paths for this: The order is split by a Routing Rule flagged as a "Handoff Routing Rule" The order is split by the new Handoff Split or Handoff Group Split dialog. Technically, we represent this as splitting a child order with a new field (WorkflowDetail) set to Handoff.  Since the idea is to "Handoff" the parent order, the child order must have identical terms and parameters to the parent (same Strategy, Qty, Price, LiquidityInst, etc). Any amend and cancel is automatically propagated to the child order to keep the "Handoff" synced.  In this way, our technical solution ("back-to-back") models our business need (handing off order). Changing Inter-Firm Routing Targets Currently, a Firm has two category of Targets: Inter-Firm Routing (i.e., to other Floor Broker Firms), and everything else (e.g, NYSE, Clearpool, etc). Per  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-6808  , All floor broker firms show up as Targets All other Targets (e.g., Clearpool, NYSE) must be entitled in the CLAD | System | Firm Targets page. Note on Target/Strategy Since Polaris does not support ParentOrder.Strategy of POV/VWAP/TWAP, we will not (yet) support it for Handoff.  For this reason, in both Routing Rules Strategy field, we will only support the values of Day, Open Close.  This is true whether the Handoff checkbox has been checked. In addition, to limit potential errors while introducing Routing Rules and Handoff, we will limit the Target to just Inter-Firm Routing (other Floor Brokers). Note: As of 9/17/2024, Polaris…

summary: Implement smart lookup for ask and bid at MdsDataProvider in MDSS
description_text: Background MDSS SimpeDB expects both bid & ask to be specified in its update calls. However for Rates, MDSS receives single sided quotes from MDS (just bid or just ask). At present, MDSS Rates caches the latest bid & ask and when it receives a new bid (new ask), it calls SimpleDB with "new bid/old ask (old bid / new ask). This is inefficient as most of the time the new bid (or ask) is likely followed immediately by a new ask ( or bid). Ideally MDSS should wait for a while to see if a new  ask  follows within a few msecs after the new  bid   (or vice versa). To do (9/25, discussed with   ) -SimpleDb should be able to handle single sided updates - see if you can pass null, etc for one side- -Does not - any modification will fall back to reusing the prev-bid/ask - effectively doubling the number of data points. This is because the core TimeSeries element Cumulative???? has both a bid and ask and always expects them both.- -If not, check SimpleDb code - it should already be doing something to handle single sided updates.- Does not. See above. If no, then discuss. Back to the Todo (orig) below: To do (orig): Main Concepts WaitType : The class uses a state-driven approach to handle the waiting for the other side of the quote. Two types of wait modes are defined: `WAIT_FOR_ASK`: The system waits for an ask price after receiving a bid. `WAIT_FOR_BID`: The system waits for a bid price after receiving an ask. Note: Default state: Initially, the system assumes `WAIT_FOR_ASK`, expecting an ask after receiving a bid. Dynamic switching : If the time difference between receiving bids and asks (`bid2AskDelay - ask2BidDelay`) exceeds a certain threshold (`WAIT_FOR_BID_THRESHOLD_MSECS`, default 2ms), the system switches from `WAIT_FOR_ASK` to `WAIT_FOR_BID`. This suggests that the system may be optimizing based on observed market conditions or network latency. Detailed Behavior In `WAIT_FOR_ASK` mode: If an ASK is received: The system publishes the rate quote with the received ASK an…

summary: RfqTake - POG is publishing the take_time_left stat (5368) at 3 sec intervals instead of the expected 1-sec interval
description_text:
The requirement is that the TAKE_TIME_LEFT_SEC (5368) task_stat is published every 1 sec, so we can show a countdown on the Client (PO grid). The bug:  PO is publishing this stat only every 3 secs (instead of 1 sec). We checked both the batched and non-batched feed and see the same results - every 3 secs, occasionally  every 4 secs. See attached logs. Analysis Not clear if this is a POG issue, TEAPI issue or TE. Issue. If TEAPI or TE issue To do See if TE is throttling publish to 3 secs - this appears the most likely case (see if   ,  ,     can look into this) If not TE, then check TEAPI & POG. Fix the bug IF TE or TEAPI, create an AD to fix the issue, and link here. Attachments: All shows take-time-left task-stat published by POG for each task. First file is the POG non-batched endpoint, second file is batched endpoint po-take-time-left-non-batched.txt - non-batched - buggy - publishes only every 3 sec curl http://pog-host:pog-port/api/parent_order/feed -o /home/schan/pog_non_batched.txt
grep '"5368":' /home/schan/pog_non_batched.txt | sed -E 's/.*"rowId":"ny5-tebond-mktx-dev1.([^"]+).*"5368":([^,]+).*/\1  \2/' > /tmp/po-take-time-left-non-batched.txt po-batched-take-time-left.txt - batched - buggy -  publishes only every 3 sec curl http://pog-host:pog-port/api/parent_order/feed/batched -o /home/schan/pog_response.txt
grep '"5368":' /home/schan/pog_response.txt | sed -E 's/.*"rowId":"ny5-tebond-mktx-dev1.([^"]+).*"5368":([^,]+).*/\1  \2/' > /tmp/po-batched-take-time-left.txt po2 - configured with correct server param.txt - batched with server-param configured for 0.5 secs correct - publishes (mostly) every 1 sec

summary: Bonds - modify the Client bootstrap index.html to load the Cognito JWT early
description_text:
Background Pano for Bonds is hosted on old Centos hosts and also accessible from the Internet via MarketAxess Infra To protect against security attacks, especially given the old Centos, MA F5 Firewall checks that every request, including static resources specifies a valid JWT. This stops any attacks at F5, protecting the Centos hosts. F5 checks for the JWT in the auth header, or in a cookie. However, a number of static resources are loaded BEFORE the JWT is available to Client code and registered as a cookie, and these would fail the F5 check. So F5 excludes these resources from the JWT check. This is an manual and error prone process as these 'bootstrap resources' can change with an upgrade and F5 rules need to be updated each time - an onerous process. Addl Background Worth reviewing these to get the big picture, and related context Overview - https://confluence.pragmafs.com/display/PMO/Secure+ADX+Panorama+Access - see the comment trail and email. This page is a how-to to test the Cognito feature in the absence of a MA Workstation (that is the usual use case)  https://confluence.pragmafs.com/display/PAN/MarketAxess+Cognito+-+testing+with+Postman Requirement Modify index.html (ideal) or some other script that is loaded VERY EARLY - and add the JWT as a cookie named  X_Panorama_Authorization . This way these are the only static resources that need to be excluded-from-JWT-validation by F5, all other static resources requests will include the JWT cookie, and can pass the F5 JWT validation Here's a sample request for static resources with the JWT as a cookie GET /node_modules/.vite/deps/chunk-MU2BHGCH.js?v=17a1cda3 HTTP/1.1
Accept: */*
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate, br, zstd
Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.9
Cache-Control: no-cache
Connection: keep-alive
Cookie: X_Panorama_Authorization=Bearer%20eyJraWQiOiJMZ0puNVJHZUx2X3VfREFITXZVVm83dFYzNm5tY1JMcElXTWVSVDFIM1hNIiwiYWxnIjoiUlMyNTYifQ.eyJ2ZXIiOjEsImp0aSI6IkFULjk4bjhVWUI3d29ONm5sRFRFV1NBLXJNVzdEQjBHSFJJZFFvNWNha2U5…

summary: AMS mog - support pad-sync, load schemas from resource folder
description_text:
Need It'll be nice to have the latest table schemas from COG, POG available in a mog based environment, and allow pad-sync to pull this schema in. mog supports this at present but with file based schemas that are not predictable, they may be missing completely, or old versions. If we always have the schemas in mog, we can also do a pad-sync to update the schemas in Pad DB. To do Make the schemas always available in mog, so we can do a pad-sync in a mog env. M Make the schemas consistent for a given version of mog. Make it easy to update the schemas Log the version of schemas used in mog log. Implementation sketch Keep the latest schemas in the mog  jar  so pad-sync for a given version / branch is always consistent like it would be for a cog, pog setup. Config change: change the default for MOG_LOAD_SCHEMA_FROM_FILES to false. This signals mog to load schemas from the resources. This only applies to mog envs, otherwise not relevant Updated schemas will have to be manually copied into ams/mog.../resources/schemas/ folder. Schemas can be obtained from a cog + pog env by running this command. It will pull schema's from the env's running cog/pog. # 1. start an env with COG and POG that have the TEAPI version you want. MAKE SURE YOU'RE RUNNING COG & POG
# 2. export schemas to the download subfolder

> ./run.sh download_schemas 

# 3. scp all files in the download folder to your local PC
# 4. IMPORTANT - delete any existing files in ams/mog/....resources/schemas folder, or we'll have dups.
# 5. Copy the downloaded files to ams/mog/...resources/schemas/ folder. Notes: Anytime we have a new TEAPI or hardcoded column changes in COG or POG, we should update mog..resources/schema/ with an updated set of schema files When we copy schema files, also copy the pad-sync-schema.info file that is generated. This file has the schema version and some other info, and is logged when mog starts

summary: RfqResponse - sort responses correctly - by DecisionTimeRank, or SortRank
description_text: Requirement Responses in the response grid should be sorted by the DecisionTimeRank if available, and if not available, then sorted by SortRank. Implementation This is implemented in related PAN-????. Implementation logic is as follows: Code block using sortRank & DecisionTimeRank. These values are used to decide top 2 or 5 responses to show. If these values are not present, we will need another differentiator to get top 2 or top 5 responses. --------- const isDecisionPending = rowsData.every( row => row[ResponsesColumns.Status].toUpperCase() === 'LIVE' && !row[ResponsesColumns.DecisionTime] && !row[ResponsesColumns.DecisionTimeRank] ); if ( isDecisionPending ) { priceColumn = ResponsesColumns.Level;  rankColumn  = ResponsesColumns. SortRank ; timeColumn = ResponsesColumns.ReceivedTime; } else { priceColumn = ResponsesColumns.DecisionTimeLevel;  rankColumn  = ResponsesColumns. DecisionTimeRank ; timeColumn = ResponsesColumns.DecisionTime; } rowsData = take( sortBy (rowsData, row => row[ rankColumn ]), maxAllowedResponses ); ----------

Require a confirmation dialog / click to dbl check intent Before moving the user, check there are no FK relations to the user in other tables. If there are, show the user the problems, and do NOT move the user. Only if there are no FK references to the user, then move the user to the new company. Test the Client UI with a 'moved' user, make sure it works fine Backend implementation: Develop a new endpoint for user move to a company : /user/{userId}/move/{toCompanyId} If user has no role assigned then update user with new company id If user has assigned a role of current company. then it should move user. return response: HTTP response code 400(BAD Request) with message "User is referenced in user-role. Remove role for user, then try again

summary: RfqTake POG COG edge cases
description_text: Review the following edge cases and make sure COG, POG do the right thing Order Event What to do Inquiry Canceled back Responses:  set status to Canceled RFQ Take order: let also set status(when inquiry order got canceled, take order will also got cancelled/rejected status) Inquiry Rejected Responses:  set status to Canceled RFQ Take order: let also set status(when inquiry order got canceled, take order will also got cancelled/rejected status) Inquiry Timeout- no response We are doing anything on timeout. Complicated. Take Canceled back Responses:  No change in status RFQ inquiry Order:  No change in status Take Rejected Responses:  No change in status RFQ inquiry Order:  No change in status Take Timeout - no response We are doing anything on timeout. Complicated. if for whatever reason the RfqTake results in a reject or cancelBack, Algo may try again for second best quote. (so in COG we should not mark responses as canceled for a RfqTake Reject or Canceled) If no ack is received for the RfqTake before the time expires (edge case), Algo will end the Rfq and publish a decisionTime alert with only header, and no responses. Also, the TAKE_STatus will get published as Passing, then null Note: the simplest then might be to check for TakeStatus = Passing, and cancel the Rfq and Responses whenever we see this.

summary: Bonds - document the list of static resources to exclude from F5 JWT validation
description_text:
1.74 packages using Vite. The static resource root and maybe path-files have changed compared to 1.72. Prod Bonds web access is protected (infosec) by a Firewall. The firewall expects the JWT to be present on every request, except a specific list of static resources - requests for these static resources do NOT need to specify the JWT. Requirements: Come up with a new list of static resources to exclude from JWT validation for 1.74 If there are more than 10 items, provide a NARROW set of patterns (e.g.  /assets/vendor-ag-grid-*.css)  that captures the list of files to exclude, and fewer than 10 items in the file-pattern list. The patterns MUST be narrow - /assets/* wont cut it from an Infosec perspective Make sure the exclude list is stable - it is not acceptable for the list to change every few releases. Firewall current configuration for static resources that are excluded from JWT check i.e. request for these patterns are  not  required to include a authorization JWT *.wav, *.png, *.js, *.js.map
/favicon.ico, /mktx.ico, /logout.html, /unauthorized.html, /asset-manifest.json, /version.txt, /manifest.json, /index.html
/static/css/, /static/js/, /static/media/ Partial list of 1.74 static resources that were requested without a Jwt and were denied. summary: Display RTS Inquiries in new AMS workspace
description_text: Work In Progress This assumes the backend BLGW work is done and parent order rows representing BondLink inquiries are reaching pano. Create separate non-deletable workspaces named "Staged" and "Adaptive" with hard-coded workspace filters (the names and filters can't be changed, and the workspaces can't be deleted or reordered by the user). The filter for Staged is (TaskId is null); the Adaptive workspace filter is (TaskId not null). Skipped for now:  Show only Status = STAGED orders in the Staged workspace, so they disappear when routed to any protocol (ADX or otherwise). (Note ADX eligibility is already guaranteed by backend.) (TO CONFIRM): Except, we should display inquiries that were routed to ADX but were rejected by TE. How can we set up the workspace filter so that we can still see them in the Staged workspace, see that they are dead, and hopefully see the reject reason. And consider two different kinds of rejects, one that created a task (rejected by strategy? does that happen?) and one rejected before a task was created. This new workspace will be based on a new ENABLE_RTS_ROUTING entitlement: For a user with the entitlement but without the Staged and Adaptive workspaces in their layout, rename their single workspace to "Adaptive" (and change its filter and make it non-editable), and create a new "Staged" workspace as described above. For a user without the entitlement but with the Staged and Adaptive workspaces in their layout, hide the "Staged" workspace and leave the Adaptive workspace back to All as and make it a normal workspace. Status (5000) has a new possible value of STAGED, representing orders staged outside of/upstream of TE. For now show no status icon for STAGED orders, same as UNSPEC. (Note, this is based on the assumption that the difference between RTS and in AMS but no strategy applied is an implementation detail that's not important to users; if that changes we can create a different icon). Enable the hotbuttons (actually it should be displ…

summary: RTS Routing to ADX - Order ticket
description_text: ControlServices Endpoint Add a new endpoint `/parent/rts-route-to-adx`. An optional query parameter "start" can be provided to automatically start the parent order. Standard row-level entitlement checks parent order control. RTS parent_order rows will include standard entitlement fields used in our bond envs. Require the user have entitlement ENABLE_RTS_ROUTING. Log an audit trail as usual (for PASS or FAILED) and forward the call to the BondLinkGateway endpoint above. The action should be"RTS_ROUTE_TO_ADX". Order Ticket Create a context menu action in the Staged workspace "Apply Strategy..." that will open the order ticket with no fields populated. When a hotbutton is pressed with an RTS order selection, the order ticket should be opened for the selected RTS items with the hot button parameters applied. The order ticket should offer action buttons "Cancel", "Submit" and "Submit and Start" similar to what we have for non-RTS orders. Submit action will hit `/parent/rts-route-to-adx` control-services endpoint with the regular OrderRequestParams. Same for "Submit and Start" with an extra parameter `/parent/rts-route-to-adx?start=true`. TODO: how do we get the max qty for Take?

summary: POL PoP timer reset on any tablet action
description_text: Josh @ NYSE emailed us a change in requirement for PoP:  NYSE Reg and NYSE Business are ok with proceeding to allow “mouse moves” in order to validate Point of Presence for traders as opposed to having to click a box.   We confirmed the following solution with him (and Charlie Caccese @ NYSE). Currently, the Polaris desktop and tablet shows a “User Location” button at the top right of the application. On the Polaris desktop, this button is view-only – the user can see their User Location Status (On-Floor, Off-Floor, On-Floor-For-Day - aka Break Glass) but cannot click the button to change status. On the Polaris tablet, the user can also click this button, which will reset their PoP status timer to 90 minutes. We will change the behavior on the tablet so that any action within the Polaris tablet has the same effect as clicking the User Location button, i.e., resetting the PoP timer to 90 mnintues. Note: It is acceptable if we limit the scope "action within the Polaris tablet" to: Any action (mouse movement, click, etc) on the tablet itself Any action within the Polaris application on the tablet Specifically, a click within the Polaris application on the tablet As usual, let me know if there is a better (easier to implement, better UX) "action scope. GWT GIVEN the User.UserStatus is OFF_FLOOR WHEN the user logs into the desktop and takes an action within the Polaris application THEN the User.UserStatus continues to be OFF_FLOOR GIVEN the User.UserStatus is OFF_FLOOR WHEN the user logs into the  tablet  and takes an action within the Polaris application THEN the User.UserStatus changes to ON_FLOOR and the PoP Timer resets to 90 minutes GIVEN the User.UserStatus is ON_FLOOR WHEN the user takes an action on the desktop THEN the PoP Timer is not reset GIVEN the User.UserStatus is ON_FLOOR WHEN the user takes an action on the tablet THEN the PoP Timer is reset to 90 minutes

AMS support Add a new property to the  settings  endpoint called  product  -  api/settings/product . Possible values are the values for ASSET_CLASS -  EQ, FX and   BOND WS support - do whichever is easier Return "POL" No support for WS - endpoint should not be called, behavior undefined. However, WS app-svc should start fine without needing any additional config changes. CLAD support Return "POL" Test report with failed tests: https://pano2-portal.pragmafs.com/test_results/pano2-mac01/AMS/Build-1194-devregr-feature-PAN-8497-regression-chrome-2024-08-05_14_57_20/e2e-report.html

summary: Bonds:   Ctrl-svc action logs out user.
Integration tests - fix duplicate ports, unstable db-svc tests
description_text: Fixes/improves a few issues: Port clash during integration tests - all these application-test.yml use port 7080. This causes integration tests in the pipeline to randomly fail if 2 integration tests that use these ports are running at the same time: rx rsocket, mdf mgmt, mdf rest, mdf rsocket, db-svc rsocket db-svc InstantMessageSvc integration tests scheduled-executor tries to access the Docker DB after it is shutdown. Continues forever, and Jenikins kills the run after ~ 1.5 hrs db-svc SycnMessageSvc integration test sometimes fails to start at all, hangs indefinitely - 1.5 hrs for Jenkins to kill the job. To do: For 1 change the ports for mdf and db-svc. Leave rx-rsocket alone. Ideally use the same ports as the local properties check the new ports do not conflict with any other application-test.yml ports check and fix ANY OTHER conflicting ports across all application-test.yml For 2 shutdown the executor thread at the end of the tests paranoia - add a gradle directive to timeout db-svc (only) integration tests if it takes longer than 5 mins - this will FAIL the run, so not great, but at least releases the env for the next run imm instead of 1.5 hrs. IF this still continues to fail (5 min timeout), we should disable it for now. For 3 disable the SyncMessageSvc test - so the pipeline does not fail. We have not changed db-svc recently, and dont care about it for AMS that is our focus for now. Created PAN-8834 to re-enable this test

summary: Fix unstable / flaky integration tests
description_text: No code for this PAN, work was done in branch bugfix/ Fix failing integration tests in Regressions pipeline PointOfPresenceServiceTest.attestation_from_another_zone_prevents_timeout() this one failed because a db operation (not sure if testcontainers or mocked) took more than 300 msec. This test checks timeouts, and is time sensitive. 300 msec looks like plenty of time, but guess not enough on the pipeline. Just flipped two sleeps (1200 msec, 500 msec) -> (500 msec, 1200msec) so the DB operation now has 1 sec (vs 300 msec earlier) to complete. Total time for the test, and test functionality remains unchanged.

summary: POL Handoff Workflow - Polaris UI Changes
description_text: Implement the Pano component of the Handoff feature:  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PF-52 . See the Feature for details, terminology, GWT, etc. Overview "Handing off" an order means that the "parent order" has been handed off to another Firm to cover - whether on the Floor or an Algo Provider. Handoff orders begin the lifecycle as a standard HighTouch parent order entered via FIX or via the UI.  When the order is split, it may move to a "Handoff" HighTouch order. There are two paths for this: The order is split by a Routing Rule flagged as a "Handoff Routing Rule" The order is split by the new Handoff Split or Handoff Group Split dialog. Technically, we represent this as splitting a child order with a new field (WorkflowDetail) set to Handoff.  Since the idea is to "Handoff" the parent order, the child order must have identical terms and parameters to the parent (same Strategy, Qty, Price, LiquidityInst, etc). Any amend and cancel is automatically propagated to the child order to keep the "Handoff" synced.  In this way, our technical solution ("back-to-back") models our business need (handing off order). Creating Handoff Orders Handoff Split Dialog Pano will add a new order action (desktop: right click; tablet: button) to bring up the Handoff Split modal dialog, covering both single order and multiple orders. This is similar to the Assignment dialog except that it allows the user to handoff (split) the order to an external target rather than an internal user/team. . Title: Handoff Split Order banner:  Identical to Assign Order. Parameters. Target. For the tablet action, this button will go after Assign.   https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-8435  has been updated to show this. Eligibility A parent order is eligible for Handoff if and only if its Qty = QtyStaged.  In other words, it has no fills; and it is not currently split anywhere.  We follow the same pattern for enabling and disabling this action as we do for the "Split" action. In brief: If a single parent …

summary: Rates - implement Rates PriceFormatter
description_text:
Review the attachments - they contain useful info Sketch Implement a function RatesPriceParts getRatesPriceParts(price, precision) price is a double upto 9 or 10 dp precision represents the denominator of the min price increment - can be 8 or 16, and represents 1/8 or 1/16 price increments RatesPriceParts {
  int bigFig
  int smallFig
  int numerator
  int denominator
  int precision // copied from the arg - reqd for PriceEditor
  int isExact
} Talk to Sree about implementation details of the function i.e. how to determine the price-parts from the input args. Will be based on Chris Good's XLS. In the meantime, hardcode sample values from Chris's XLS, so you can test the formatter. Use the RatesPriceParts to display the price as described in Chris's XLS and screenshots. Sree Notes from Price Formatter meeting   todo - review for accuracy Price e.g. 100.123456789 is composed of 3 parts for display - BigFig, Smallfig, Fraction Big figure is 3 digits (100) sometimes 2 (99). Denominator is 16  (so n/16) for on-the-runs, and 8 (so n/8) for olds. Sequence is ‘ ‘ (0/16), 1/16, 1/8,  3/16, ¼, 5/16, 3/8, 7/16,  +  (for ½ or 8/16), 9/16, 5/8, 11/16, ¾, 13/16, 7/8, ‘ ‘ (small figure bumps up) When you bump up  past 15/16, bumps up small figure, fraction goes to ‘ ‘. When you bump up small figure, bumps up the big figure up Editor needs to mirror these increments including showing “ +”  when the fractional part is effectively 0.5 AvgTrade price may not be an exact n/16. For these, find the closest n/16 (n/8 for olds), and print a “*” next to the price to show its not exact Prices are usually up to 9 decimal places. If we showed a mid price i.e. (bid + ask)/2, it would be up to 10 decimals, otherwise 9 is the usual

summary: Implement Purge Task in Pano2
description_text: Background Linked IL-3094 was for an issue where a task (aka Parent Order) was rejected with an exception due to bad config and was in a bad state. The task was dead, but was still spinning and consuming CPU. TE has a "Purge Task" API that would have killed the task and resolved the issue. However we were unable to invoke PurgeTask because: Legacy Panorama has PurgeTask, but the task was not visible in the list of POs Management Console (MC) only has options to Purge all Tasks on a TE, so not useful Pano2 does NOT have a PurgeTask feature. Requirement Implement Purge Task. Use the TEAPI call. We should be able to Purge a task even if its not visible on PO grid To do: UI add a PO command "Purge Task" Add an alternate way to type in a TaskId to Purge. Useful if the Task does not show on the PO grid Need confirmation to make sure a) they really want to do it, and b) they selected/entered the correct PO/taskId Call ctrl-svc new PurgeTask endpoint. Params is just the taskId, maybe a reason. Same entitlement as ForceOut - only avail to Pragma personnel. BE ctrl-svc - accept call from UI, send to ctrlg. Log at WARN level, including who issued the request. ctrlg - call teapi. Log at WARN level, including who issued the request. ( this will need to done by Ayub or Jamie ) if easy to provide feedback to the UI on the request status, do that. Otherwise dont
