summary: POG: Make rowId to include instanceId, InquiryId and ResponseId,  make changes to publish LMResponse as new response quote
description_text: Scope of this ticket. POG current response data implementation generates rowId from teinstanceId, inquiryId and quoteId. but COG we changed to use teinstanceId, inquiryId and responseId to generate rowId. So we need to change POG logic to use same way to generate rowId. Rx-svc use rowId to merge data from COG and POG. POG needs to publish new response when it receives decision alert with LMResponse as response Update response schema to include inquiryId(20117) Update COG response to publish inquiryId(20117) Update POG response include pragmataskId( 99998) and   inquieryid(20117) We also need to do this  confirmed (7/25 DM/Sree discussion on Slack) The DecisionTime alert may sometimes include an  pseudo-response  for the LiveMarkets (LM) Quote. We call it a pseudo-response, because it looks like a Response, and includes all the other fields that are in the DecisionTime alert, but it is not a real Response to the RFQ Inquiry. Instead it is a LM quote that the Algo considers (to hit) along with the regular RFQ Responses. We want to show this LM quote along with the other responses in the UI. The LM psuedo-response will look just like the other responses in the DecisionTime alert with the following differences: The LM  psuedo-response  will have these values: dealer="LiveMarkets", response id = "LMResponse", and quote id = "LMQuote" There are no COG updates to rx-svc for the LM response. The only updates are from the DecisionTime alert row. So the Client will never see a LM Response until POG sends response updates from a DecisionTime Alert This means rx-svc and client do not have any other fields for this pseudo-response - significantly, they dont have the chainId, which is required by both rx and the Client. POG todo POG should look for any LiveMarkets response by checking for e.g.  id=LMResponse  , and if present, publish it like the other Responses on the DecisionTime Alert. Because this LM pseudo-response was not previously published by COG, POG will need to prefil…

summary: MDF - add a Mono endpoint to support Marketdata download feature in WS Client
description_text:
Context Related   is to support download of Portfolio view Marketdata from the WS Client. However the PV only subscribes marketdata for the currently active tab (e.g. S&P 500) and not all PV tabs, whereas the requirement is for the download to include data for symbols across all tabs. This PAN is to support the WS Client feature to download marketdata across all PV tabs Requirement Add an authenticated endpoint in MDF that accepts a large list of symbols - 500-3000 symbols and returns the marketdata for these symbols in a single compressed snapshot. No streaming. Implementation sketch / to dos Finalize shape of request and response with Yan. Subscribes to these symbols using the normal subscription mechanism in place for MDF Waits N seconds - in the event the symbol was not previously subscribed, for subscriptions to complete and marketdata to flow into the cache. N is  configurable in mdf application.yml using a new  optional  env-var MDF_SNAPSHOT_WAIT_DURATION, and defaults to "5s" (5 seconds) If MDF_SNAPSHOT_WAIT_DURATION is not defined. Retrieves the marketdata for each of the subscribed symbols from the cache. Returns the result set in a Mono. For symbols that did not have marketdata, discuss with Yan and do one of the foll: return the list of "no-data" symbols in a separate property return empty rows for "no-data" symbols Note that from the client perspective, this is a REST (not streaming) request. So the endpoint should return a Mono and not a Flux. Notes Slack discussion with DP, YP, Sree on 7/18/24 DP: One Firm raised a good problem on the portfolio download.
Since we limit the portfolios to 500 symbols each, some Firms will have 3 or 4 portfolio tabs, so they can look at 1500 or 2000 symbols.
And, of course, they want to be able to download all three or four portfolios every 30 minutes, rather than just one of them.I'm still thinking about what to do here (any ideas?), but wanted to flag this potential challenge , as I know you were about to start worki…

summary: MDF: POL Price Indications during Halt
description_text: Background Please see PAN-8319 Definitions and References https://www.ctaplan.com/publicdocs/ctaplan/CQS_Pillar_Output_Specification.pdf Pre-Open Indication.   Prior to the open, the NYSE CQS market data may contain the likely opening price (Trading Range).  This is sent only by for symbols that are dislocated - i.e., likely to open far from their closing price.  Typically there are fewer than ten such symbols each day. Price Indication .  Similar to the Pre-Open Indication, except sent for symbols that during a halt, when they are within 3 minutes of the open. Identifying Indication Messages Indication Logic to identify the Indication message Price IF CQS.SecurityStatus in [M,P]  (Regulatory Halt    AND BidPrice and/or OfferPrice is populated    THEN CQS.BidPrice/OfferPrice =  Price Indication prices (Trading Range) Ideal Solution Implement the "Price Indication" portion of PAN-8319 Two new permanent tabs in the Portfolio grid: "Pre-Open Indications" and "Price Indications". All (and only) symbols that are Price Indications (as defined above) appear in the Price Indications grid. A new "Price Timestamp" captures the time the symbol was added to the Price Indications portfolio. Users can choose to view these timestamps in the Portfolio grids. Pricing GWT GIVEN AA, IBM, BA and BRK.B are symbols    AND AA, IBM have a CQS.SecurityStatus in [M,P] and CQS.BidPrice/OfferPrice are not populated WHEN the SIP provides a BidPrice/OfferPrice for IBM THEN only IBM automatically appears in the Price Indications portfolio GIVEN IBM is in the Price Indications WHEN the SecurityStatus changes to another value    OR the BidPrice and OfferPrice are no longer populated THEN IBM should disappear from the Price Indications portfolio Acceptable Solution Perhaps the tricky part in the Ideal Requirements is automatically creating portfolios that automatically show Pre-Open and Price Indications. Per a May 10th, 2024 call with Charlie Caccese, we do not have to do this.  It is sufficient fo…

summary: POL NYSE Opening/Reopening Trade - backend work
description_text: Requirement Implement feature PAN-8645 - backend work only Deployment and Monitoring Considerations On startup, the  ws  deployment of the market data feed (MDF) will subscribe to ACTIV trade notifications for all NYSE-listed instruments in SecMaster. This will very probably significantly increase the network bandwidth between ACTIV and MDF, and put more load on the ACTIV content gateway. This will also increase the CPU load of the MDF application near and during market hours, because the application listens to pre-market and after-hours trade messages as well as regular trade messages. Finally, this will increase the memory needed by the application, to hold trade data for all NYSE instruments, although this increase is not very large. I recommend that we load-test this in non-production environments, and verify that we are comfortable with the bandwidth and network load, and that the application memory seems sufficient. A new Prometheus metric has been added, "ws.market.data.trade.symbol.count". This reports the number of instruments for which ACTIV trade subscriptions are currently open. The old metric "ws.market.data.nbbo.symbol.count" will now report the number of instruments for which ACTIV quote and trading state subscriptions are currently open.

summary: SortRank - UI should display Responses sorted by DecisionSortRank, SortRank, and sort null values at the end for numeric fields
description_text: 9/1:    - do this instead of  ). This is what we want, and also may be a more generic solution (todo.3) See feature  , section  Sort Rank . Also see PAN-8627 &   for the backend work to support the UI work Client TODO Sort the Response Grid by (DecisonTimeRank, SortRank) - smallest to largest. Note that the values may be negative, so -100 sorts ahead of -10. use the SortRank of a row only if it's DecisionTimeRank is null/empty. If its not possible sort by DecisionTime and SortRank, and we're just using default grid sort behavior, then make the default sort DecisionTimeRank Regardless of whether we do 1) or 2), make sure NULL sorts at the END for ALL numeric Response columns - currently it sorts at the top. This inculdes the foll cols - SortRank, DecisionTimeRank, Level, DecisionTimeLevel Discuss with Yan and do this for ALL grids i.e. NULL sorts at the end for numeric columns. If it is not a small effort, create a separate PAN for this step. Note The original plan (see feature  ) was to merge RawSortRank (published by COG) and DecisionTimeRank (published by POG) into a single SortRank field in the backend so the UI work could be simple - just sort by the combined SortRank field. However that was too difficult to do. So we now have 2 fields - SortRank (old RawSortRank) published by COG, and DecisionTimeRank published by POG, and hence more complicated sorting logic to be done by the UI (or not at all).

summary: SortRank - cog, pog work to publish SortRank related columns
description_text: See feature  , section  Sort Rank,  and PAN-8628 for the related Client work. Modified Todo (8/26/24) COG Publish a field  SortRank  for Response. Calculated as follows: Start with Level. Note: for quotes responding to a PRICE Buy order, the lowest offer is best. Multiply by -1 if order Side is Sell Multiply by -1 if PriceType is SPREAD Add 2_000_000 to SortRank. This way a response with a DecisionTimeRank will always rank higher than a Response with only a SortRank and no DecisionTimeRank - which is what we want POG Publish the  DecisionTimeRank  (2092) from the RfqAlert. (nothing to do - this is already implemented)

summary: POL Download MDM Portfolio once every 30 mins
description_text: We will implement this as: Users must have a entitlement (ENABLE_PORTFOLIO_DOWNLOAD) to be able to download all unique symbols across all tabs in the Portfolio grid. We will download all available columns (not just the displayed columns) We will order the columns as:  'symbol', 'instrumentStatus', 'imbSide', 'imbClearingPx', 'imbRefPx', 'imbAuctionPrice', 'imbPairedQty', 'imbTotalQty', 'imbUnpairedSide', 'imbUnpairedQty', 'bestBidQty', 'bestBidPx', 'bestAskQty', 'bestAskPx', 'lastTradeVenue', 'lastTradePx', 'lastTradeQty', 'prevClosePx', 'openPx', 'closePx', 'post', 'panel', 'listingExchange', 'reason', 'timestamp' We not allow a Firm to download the grid more than once every 30 minutes (when a user at FirmA downloads, then we do something like send a message to the UI of all other users to ensure they can't download for 30 mins) The FIrm will tell us which user(s) they want entitled for downloading. When the user cannot download the grid (due to entitlement or timer) then the download icon is grayed out. There is no requirement to audit when a user attempts to download and whether it is successful. GIVEN a user is not entitled to download the Portfolio grid WHEN the user attempts to download a Portfolio grid THEN they cannot click the download icon GIVEN a user is entitled to download a Portfolio grid      No user at the Firm downloaded the grid in the last 30 minutes (e.g., it is 1:46 pm and the Firm last downloaded it at 1:15) WHEN the user attempts to download a Portfolio grid by clicking the download icon THEN  all symbols across all portfolio tabs  are downloaded with  all columns GIVEN a user is entitled to download the Portfolio grid      A user at the Firm has downloaded the grid in the last 30 minutes (e.g., it is 3:55pm and the Firm last downloaded it at 3:30) WHEN the user attempts to download the Portfolio grid by clicking the download icon THEN they cannot click the download icon Notes Q:  Why are all symbols across all portfolio tabs? summary: FXRates - add the ability to save/restore/delete multiple layouts
description_text: Background During the Demo Manasi noted that it would be nice to be able to quickly switch to a mode that shows just 1 or 2 price panels / symbols. Requirement Support multiple user defined layouts. Layouts actions are done at the Container Panel level. Allow these actions: select an existing layout save the current layout save-as a new layout Delete an existing layout. Layouts should save the following: Layout Name the list of symbols and their order If the save was done on a popped-out Container panel, then dimensions AND position of the panel. The current view of each price panel - just TOB or with depth Any MDS, Venue selection Show or hide column headings. Do NOT save the popped-out / docked status. Layout changes will not pop-out / re-dock. That is a user action All the changes saved in pop-out mode should be visible in docked status and vice versa. Notes: If the Container is not popped out, then keep the last popped-out attrs during a save.

summary: Ability to freeze columns in Panorama
description_text: Today, in the summary grid, we have the ability to group the rows by columns (max 2). When a summary tab has a column that is grouped, the column is pinned to the left and the rows are aggregated based on the selected column(s). Several users have asked for the ability to freeze/pin a column(s) to the left (specifically in PO grid) so that when they're scrolling to the right, those columns are always visible (similar to how excel freezes panes). We'd like to have the ability to give a user to right click on an individual column and pin it to the left. The right click menu option can be "Pin Column" and pinned columns are always kept visible to the left. They can be moved/re-ordered within the pinned columns set. Right click on a pinned column should show a new option called "Unpin Column" which would then bring the column back as the first column amongst all unpinned columns. If we need to enforce a limit on how many columns a user can pin, let's go with 5. Ideally we also show (in all themes) a subtle difference in the columns that are pinned.

summary: AMS - attempt #2 - enable PAD column-sync from a more recent TEAPI than POG's teapi
description_text: First attempt at this by PAN-6602 was a fail. See   and email in Comments for why. This PAN is a 2nd attempt at this feature. Implementation sketch: The solution we are considering (still to be tested) is to dynamically load the teapi.jar from the filesystem, instead of binding it to pog/cog at build time as is done today. This  changes the Pano deployment workflow   for PE/Devops, and introduces the following changes: Need to specify the teapi.jar version somewhere that pipelines and PMT can read. As part of a regular Pano upgrade, pipeline/pmt will need to pull down tepai.jar from Artifactory, and drop it next to pog/cog.jar. Have a separate  upgrade-teapi-jar-only  process for PE to update the Pano env’s teapi.jar (only) when required. Needed for PE, can skip for non-prod Pipelines. See email in comment for addl. details. Q&A Q:  Why do we need to upgrade Pano to be able to see the new task-params/stats ? A:  pog/cog need to know the all the task-params/stats – type info, etc. The task-params/stats are defined in teapi.jar, and pano POG, COG are built with specific versions of teapi.jar. When there are new task-params/stats we need to build a new pog/cog with the new version of teapi.jar. Q:  Why can’t we do the same thing for pog as we did for pad-svc i.e. load the new task-params/stats from a schema file ? A: We cannot fix this by doing the same thing for pog that we did for pad-sync i.e. load the columns from schema/config files. This is because the logic to retrieve column values is embedded deep in the teapi codebase, and not pog code, and we do not want to mess with that. Q: Will PE/Devops need to support both old and new deployment models ? A: I think the new is backward compatible. Rollout the teapi.jar even if the Pano version is older and pog/cog have embedded teapi.jar. They’ll just ignore the file based teapi.jar ** Q:  If we don’t have a non-prod pipeline for upgrade-teapi-jar-only, how will QA test an old Pano with a new teapi.jar A: QA should manua…

summary: Standardize the way log files are configured in AMS and WS application.yml
description_text:
Background In AMS, we specify log directories (e.g. APP_SVC_LOG_DIR) whereas in WS, explicit log files are used (APP_SVC_LOG). It would be good to standardize this. Similarly, for all apps. (See placeholder PAN-9951 for SDLC steps to take the code thru to merge) To do Discuss with PE ( ) to pick one convention for both. Preference is AMS config, but WS may have some complications due to zones. If the decision is to leave as is, close out this PAN as wont-do, and add a rationale/justification. Discuss with Devops ( ) for any pipeline coordination required Update AMS xor WS to match the other's convention Update configs in pano repo - either AMS or WS. If AMS was updated to match the WS format: also update UIGateways entries/cofigs to match the WS format Update AMS configs in pano2cfg repo - do all envs EXCEPT the qa envs that are managed by QA i.e. dont touch any <star>-qa folders. # AMS sample application.yml logfile entry:
file.name: ${APP_SVC_LOG_DIR}/app-svc.log

# WS sample application.yml logfile entry
file.name: ${APP_SVC_LOG} Considerations on picking a convention 1. Fixing the logfile name (AMS) is less confusion 2. AMS needs to support pano2cfg, WS needs to support docker, both need to be able to run from IntelliJ 3. WS has to handle logfiles for multiple zones. The folder structure for log files in PMT is different than in Docker for multi-zone setups 4. WS has to handle logfiles for multiple rx-svc partitions 5. Need to think about WS nginx for multizone setups

summary: Group Split - Ineligible Orders
description_text: Overview For Group Split, we do not rely on the UI to prevent selection of ineligible orders or entry of invalid values. For example, Group Split does not prevent a user from selecting Dead orders, risk-failed orders, splitting Open/Close orders to a different order type.  To show these ineligible orders, we introduce an Ineligible Order Pane. Requirements Events Pano should scan all selected orders, identify ineligible orders, and populate the Ineligible Order Pane on the following events: Events Comment Split Order(s) right click menu Hotbutton click Basically, the same event as Split Order(s) Modifying Strategy in the Split dialog We only need to revalidate "Strategy mismatch" in this case, but am assuming it is little cost and slightly better UX to revalidate everything Ineligible Orders Ineligible orders are defined as orders that meet at least one of the following criteria: Ineligiblity Type Where ... Priority Dead ParentOrder.OrderStatus = Dead 1 Pending ParentOrder.PendingStatus not NULL 2 Rule 122 There are existing live Child Order(s) for this parent order Any of the child orders have a FIrmTrader that does not match this user. 3 Risk-failed ParentOrder.RiskStatus = "Failed" 4 Working ParentOrder.OrderStatus = Live and ParentOrder.QtyStaged = 0 5 Mismatch SplitRequest.Strategy != ParentOrder.Strategy, where ParentOrder.Strategy in (Open, Close) 6 An order may be ineligible for multiple reasons: for example, it may have a Strategy Mismatch and be either Dead or risk-failed.  In this case, we show the lowest priority ineligibility type in the grid. Layout The Ineligible Order Pane is basically a parent order grid showing these ineligible orders. Ideally, the Ineligible Order Pane has all features of the parent order grid (dynamic updates, sorting, filtering, searching, modifying columns) except that it does not support actions (Cancel, split, assign, etc). However, these features are nice-to-have.  Pleas don't implement any where there is a a significant cos…

summary: Improvements to help PE operate and troubleshoot Pano2 in Prod
Pol - market hours override
description_text: Currently Polaris market hours are hard-coded in class CalendarService based on the published NYSE holiday calendar. In the event of an error, an unexpected early closing (e.g. a 9/11 or hurricane) we may want a way to override this pre-programmed calendar without doing a code release. This doesn't seem urgent since break-glass is available for day-of situations, but adding the story to the backlog for prioritization anyway.

summary: Montage
description_text: Summary See the red below for Post-Phase 2 items. Phase 3 requirements have been pushed to  https://jira.pragmafs.com/browse/PAN-7174  .  Phase 3 means "nice-to-have, don't ever have to do". This Feature includes Phase 2 (must be in production in mid-September 2023) and "post-Phase2" (must be done shortly after Phase2) items. AS A user IN ORDER TO understand price formation for an IPO I NEED TO see the NYSE Depth in Polaris AS A user IN ORDER TO understand the current market when trading a symbol I NEED TO   see t he  Exchange BBO in Polaris I NEED TO   see the NYSE Depth in Polaris I NEED TO   see my own orders by pricepoint overlayed on the Exchange BBO / NYSE Depth Polaris has a depth object (Montage) that shows the market data for either the SIP (Consolidated) or NYSE (NYSE L2 feed).  It also overlays the orders for this user onto the depth object.  This is used for both IPOs and day-to-day trading. Like the MDM generally, we are building this just for the NYSE Floor Brokers.  We don't believe it has value beyond this, so we are keeping the requirements tight to what the FBs require.  The FBs require it because not all of them pay for Retuers/Bloomberg; and even those who do can't see the data in their tablet.  Also, the Montage is intended for trading - for example, it shows orders overlayed on market data.. The Montage is shown within both the Market Data Monitor (MDM) and the Parent Order Details (POD). Requirements Data Depth is used to show two discrete data sets: Depth view Feed Phase Description TOB (Top of Book) SIP Phase2 SIP data is used for the Depth and for the L1 data. Depth NYSE L2 Phase2 NYSE L2 data is used for the Depth and for the L1 data. We will always show 14 rows. The Montage (as explained below) can show the 14 best bids; the 14 best offers; or the 7 best bid and 7 best offers. . Display type Feed Phase Description Non-cumulative SIP or L2 Phase2 Displays the qty at each pricepoint. Cumulative SIP or L2 Post-Phase2 Displays the qty at each…

summary: POL Market Data
description_text: Background and Context In this context, market data refers to data published by exchanges in real-time. And also to a real-time view of the trades within Polaris (LiquidityFinder). Brokers use this market data to: Determine how to execute an order (e.g., when splitting an order, what limit price to use on the child order) Discuss symbols with a client (e.g., the closing imbalance for a particular symbol helps the client understand whether they want to send the broker an order) Investigate orders (e.g., the client may be unhappy with the price or quantity of an execution) Find potential crosses with other brokers (LiquidityFinder). Scope Polaris provides users with multiple ways to view market data: Order grids. The user can view market data on each order in the order grids. Order details. The user can view more in-depth market data for an order within the Parent Order Details grid. Market Data Monitor. The user can view market data on a portfolio of symbols. Definitions See the Market Data Field Catalog here:  http://github.pragmafs.com/Core/Requirements/blob/master/Market%20Data/Polaris%20Market%20Data%20Field%20Catalog.rst NYSE Comments The NYSE Floor Brokers are entitled to see the closing imbalance feed early (2pm rather than 3:45pm on a regular day) if and only if they are on the floor of the exchange. For anyone not entitled to see this (not on the Floor), they will not see the imbalance data at all.
